<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006975 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00600: (2026x Refresh1) SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast

- page_id: `304006975`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/304006975/2026x+Refresh1+SvcV-9+Services+Technology+and+Skills+Forecast
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T14:15:11.180+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x Refresh1) Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-9-services-technology-and-skills-forecast', 'svcv-9']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SvcV-9 defines the underlying current and expected supporting technologies and skills. Expected supporting technologies and skills are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology and skills, and expected improvements or trends. New technologies and skills are tied to specific time periods, which can correlate against the time periods used in SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description model milestones and linked to Capability Phases.

The SvcV-9 provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the architecture. The SvcV-9 provides descriptions of relevant:

- Emerging capabilities.
- Industry trends.
- Predictions (with associated confidence factors) of the availability and readiness of specific hardware and software services.
- Current and possible future skills.

In addition to providing an inventory of trends, capabilities and services, the SvcV-9 also includes an assessment of the potential impact of these items on the architecture. Given the future-oriented nature of this model, forecasts are typically made in short, mid and long-term timeframes, such as 6, 12 and 18-month intervals. In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. As technologies change, like incorporation of Representational State Transfer (REST) services in the Web Services Description Language, this model can present a timeline of technologies related services over time.

The intended usage of the SvcV-9 includes:

- Forecasting technology readiness against time.
- HR Trends Analysis.
- Recruitment Planning.
- Planning technology insertion.
- Input to options analysis.

**Implementation**

SvcV-9 can be represented using a SvcV-9 table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The specific time periods selected (and the trends being tracked) can be coordinated with architecture transition plans (which the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description' space=''] can support). That is, insertion of new capabilities and upgrading or re-training of existing resources may depend on or be driven by the availability of new technology and associated skills.

If standards are an integral part of the technologies important to the evolution of a given architecture, then it may be convenient to combine SvcV-9 with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) StdV-2 Standards Forecast' space=''] into a composite Fit-for-Purpose View.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Forecast
- Service Access

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SvcV-9 defines the underlying current and expected supporting technologies and skills. Expected supporting technologies and skills are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology and skills, and expected improvements or trends. New technologies and skills are tied to specific time periods, which can correlate against the time periods used in SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description model milestones and linked to Capability Phases.</p><p>The SvcV-9 provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the architecture. The SvcV-9 provides descriptions of relevant:</p><ul><li>Emerging capabilities.</li><li>Industry trends.</li><li>Predictions (with associated confidence factors) of the availability and readiness of specific hardware and software services.</li><li>Current and possible future skills.</li></ul><p>In addition to providing an inventory of trends, capabilities and services, the SvcV-9 also includes an assessment of the potential impact of these items on the architecture. Given the future-oriented nature of this model, forecasts are typically made in short, mid and long-term timeframes, such as 6, 12 and 18-month intervals. In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. As technologies change, like incorporation of Representational State Transfer (REST) services in the Web Services Description Language, this model can present a timeline of technologies related services over time.</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-9 includes:</p><ul><li>Forecasting technology readiness against time.</li><li>HR Trends Analysis.</li><li>Recruitment Planning.</li><li>Planning technology insertion.</li><li>Input to options analysis.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SvcV-9 can be represented using a SvcV-9 table.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast" ac:title="SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SvcV-9.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The specific time periods selected (and the trends being tracked) can be coordinated with architecture transition plans (which the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description" /></ac:link> can support). That is, insertion of new capabilities and upgrading or re-training of existing resources may depend on or be driven by the availability of new technology and associated skills.</p><p>If standards are an integral part of the technologies important to the evolution of a given architecture, then it may be convenient to combine SvcV-9 with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) StdV-2 Standards Forecast" /></ac:link> into a composite Fit-for-Purpose View.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="e650a653-4dcd-42f8-8b6b-0d181c3661f9" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="e508bbff-baac-4438-8da2-ec1ab07e0165" ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="1" /> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="109d3750-a1c3-4875-bf29-38a3af767fea" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Forecast</li><li>Service Access</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006982 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00604: (2026x Refresh1) Systems viewpoint

- page_id: `304006982`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/304006982/2026x+Refresh1+Systems+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T14:15:11.475+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['systems-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

“The DoDAF-described Models within the Systems Viewpoint describes systems and interconnections providing for, or supporting, DoD functions. DoD functions include both warfighting and business functions. The Systems Models associate systems resources to the operational and capability requirements. These systems resources support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information. The Systems DoDAF-described Models are available for support of legacy systems. As architectures are updated, they should transition from Systems to Services and utilize the models within the Services Viewpoint.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

800700

#### PANEL: Views

Views

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

- labels: []

556378518

556378520

556378519

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A ratified and peer-reviewed specification that is used to guide or constrain the architecture. A Standard may be applied to any element in the architecture via the [constrainedItem] property of UML::Constraint (Standard).

DoDAF: Technical standards document specific technical methodologies and practices to design and implement.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF

##### Extensions

UML Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 00606: (2026x Refresh1) Temporal Part

- page_id: `304007240`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/304007240/2026x+Refresh1+Temporal+Part
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T14:15:24.872+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM 2.1 Capability Viewpoint and Strategic Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

917090645

917090648

917090647

##### Description

UPDM Artifact: An EnterprisePhase can be sub-divided into structural and temporal parts. TemporalPart describes the EnterprisePhase elements that have a time based nature.

MODAF/NAF: Asserts that one EnterprisePhase is a temporal part of another.

This means that both EnterprisePhases have the same spatial extent, i.e., this is only a temporal structure (MODAF::EnterpriseTemporalPart).

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Property

##### Related MODAF Views

[MODAF. StV-1 Enterprise Vision](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P190/MODAF.+StV-1+Enterprise+Vision)

##### Related NAF Views

[NCV-1 Capability Vision](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P190/NCV-1+Capability+Vision)

StV-1 Enterprise Vision</a></p><h3>Related NAF Views</h3><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P190/NCV-1+Capability+Vision">NCV-1 Capability Vision</a></p><p><span><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304008157 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00607: (2026x Refresh1) Time Periods dialog

- page_id: `304008157`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/304008157/2026x+Refresh1+Time+Periods+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T14:16:18.070+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Dialogs
- labels: ['time-periods-dialog']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Related Pages**:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating P1 Technology Forecast table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating A8 Standards Forecast table' space='']
- Creating StdV-2 table
- Creating SvcV-9 table
- Creating SV-9 table

In the UPDM plugin, time periods are used to specify resources for the architecture against a timeline indicating when they are expected to be in use. The **Time Periods** dialog is essential when defining time periods for the **forecast of the resources**. When [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Time Periods dialog' space=''], you need to select a pre-existing timeline or create a new one, specify different time period types, and provide additional required data.

The **Time Periods** dialog consists of three areas:

- **Toolbar** - This area allows you to control how properties are displayed in the dialog.
- **Properties** - Provides the list of all properties that are available to specify.
- Properties value cells - The spaces where you can set values for the properties.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Time periods can be defined when the subjects, or resources, of the forecast are already added in the table. In other words, the **Technology area** column must be filled in with resources before adding a specific time period. Now, it is possible to use the**Time Periods** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The steps below show you how to work with the**Time Periods**dialog by explaining all properties that should have values defined, and by showing you how to specify those values.

procedureTo specify time periods using the **Time Periods** dialog

1. Open the **Time Periods** dialog, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the table toolbar. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. (Optional) If you want to modify the view of listed properties, enable the buttons from the **Time Periods** dialog toolbar:
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to group properties by the categories.
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to list properties alphabetically.
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to expand all collapsed categories.
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to collapse all expanded categories.
3. Specify the Timeline property:
  - Select a preexisting Timeline element. How to select an existing TimelineClick on the Timeline property value box, and do one of the following: 
 - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], or press Alt+Down. 
 - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and select it from the opened **Select Timeline** dialog. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Create a new Timeline element via the dialog. How to create a new Timelinea) Click on the Timeline property value box. 
b) Select [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the **Select Timeline** dialog. 
c) Ensure that element **Creation Mode** is turned on. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
d) Select the appropriate package from the list in which you want to store a new Timeline element. 
e) Click the **Create** button, or, from the shortcut menu of the selected package, select **Create** > **Timeline**. The Specification window of the newly created Timeline opens automatically.[IMAGE alt='' src='']f) Type the name of the new Timeline, and click **Close**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
g) In the **Select Timeline** dialog, select the newly created Timeline, and click **OK**. 
The timeline is now selected as a Timeline property value. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Select one of three Forecast Kinds asproperty values:
  - Periodic - to divide the forecast into equal periods.
  - Specified Dates - to divide the forecast into specific dates.
  - Specified Periods - to divide the forecast into specific time periods. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Specify other properties from the selected Forecast Kind value:
  - 
    - If you selected the Periodic type, you need to define those active properties: 
 - From Date property. To select the date on which the forecast will begin, use one of the following ways: 
- Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or Press Alt+Down ,and select already existing date from the list. 
 - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and select it from the opened [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UPDMP183']**Date and Time Settings** dialog. 
 - Number of Periods property. Type the number of forecast periods. 
 - Period Length property. Type a number specifying the length of the forecast according to Length Unit property. 
 - Length Unit property. Select the unit to use when measuring the length of a forecast: Years, Months, or Days. 
 [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Example of the forecast table (SV-9) with Periodic Forecast KindAll data defined in the**Time Periods** dialog is displayed automatically in columns by diving periods.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - If you selected the Specified Dates type, you only need to define the Forecast Dates property value. How to define Forecast Dates property valuea) Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to edit the value cell. The **Select ISO8601 Date Time** dialog opens. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
b) Select the existing ISO8601DateTime element (date) from the **List** , or create a new one.How to create a new ISO8601DateTime (date)a. In the **Select ISO8601 Date Time** dialog, ensurethat element **Creation Mode** is turned on. 
b. Select the appropriate package from the list in which you want to store a new ISO8601DateTime element (date). 
c. Click the **Create** button, or, from the shortcut menu of the selected package, select **Create** > **ISO8601DateTime**. The Specification window of the newly created ISO8601DateTime elementopens automatically. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
d. Specify the Value property by clicking the [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and selecting an appropriate date from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UPDMP183']**Date and Time Settings** dialog. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']e. Click **Close,** or create more dates by repeating steps c. and d.c) Click **OK**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Example of the forecast table (SV-9) with Specified Dates Forecast KindAll data defined in the **Time Periods** dialog are displayed in columns in the table. In this case, three dates (2016-09-23, 2017-09-23, and 2018-03-23) were automatically divided into two columns (from 2016-09-23 to 2017-09-23, and from 2017-09-23 to 2018-03-23).[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - If you selected Specified Periods type, you only need to define the Time Periods property value. How to define Time Periods property valuea)Select the Time Periods property value box. 
b) Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''] to edit the value cell.c) In the **Select Time Period** dialog you can: - Double-click to select existing Time Periods ([IMAGE alt='' src='']) one by one from the **List**tab. 
 - Expand an existing Timeline element ([IMAGE alt='' src='']) with time periods defined in the **Tree** tab, and double-click on Time Periods to choose them one by one. 
 - Create a new Timeline element withTime Periods.How to create the Timeline with Time Periodsa. Ensure that element **Creation Mode** is turned on. 
b. Select the appropriate package from the list in which you want to store a new Timeline element. 
c. Click the **Create** button, or, from the shortcut menu of selected package, select **Create** > **Timeline**. The Specification window of the newly created Timeline opens automatically. 
d. Type a name for the new Timeline, and click **Close**. 
e. Select the newly created Timeline element. 
f. Click the **Create** button, and select **Time Period,** or, from the shortcut menu of the selected Timeline, select **Create** > **Time Period**. The Specification window of the newly created Time Period opens automatically. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
g. Type a name for the newly created Time Period. Specify its Start Date and End Date properties. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
h. Click **Close**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Example of the forecast table (SV-9) with Specified Periods Forecast KindAll time periods defined in the **Time Periods** dialog are displayed in the table as columns. In this case, three periods (2016 period, 2017 period, and 2018 period) with their specific start and end dates were defined and displayed as columns in the SV-9 table.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Click **OK**.

After specifying all required data in this dialog, the new columns appear in the table as time periods. The forecast of resources is now planned and divided into required periods. The following figure illustrates the results in a forecast table after specifying periodical time periods in the**Time Periods** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Use**Time Period****s** dialog to specify Forecast dates and time periods.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open **Time Periods** dialog

1. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']in the toolbar. The**Time Periods** dialog opens.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

In the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><strong>Select ISO8601 Date Time</strong> dialog, ensure </span>that element <strong>Creation Mode</strong> is turned on.<br />b. Select the appropriate package from the list in which you want to store a new <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">ISO8601DateTime element</span> (date).<br />c. Click the <strong>Create</strong> button, or, from the shortcut menu of the selected package, select <strong>Create</strong> &gt; <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><strong>ISO8601DateTime</strong></span>. The Specification window of the newly created <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">ISO8601DateTime element </span>opens automatically.<br /><ac:image ac:title="When creating a new date in the Select ISO8601 Date Time dialog, you need to select the package wherein you want to store the new date (b.), turn on the creation mode (a.), and click the Create button (c.)." ac:alt="When creating a new date in the Select ISO8601 Date Time dialog, you need to select the package wherein you want to store the new date (b.), turn on the creation mode (a.), and click the Create button (c.)."><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_ISO8601_date_time_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Time Periods dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">d. Specify the Value property by clicking the <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Time Periods dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>, and selecting an appropriate date from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDMP183" ri:content-title="Date and Time Settings dialog" /><ac:link-body><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Date and Time Settings</strong></span></ac:link-body></ac:link> dialog.</span><br /><ac:image ac:title="After the new ISO8601DateTime element is created, its specific date must be selected from the Date and Time Settings dialog which opens automatically when trying to edit the Value property box." ac:alt="After the new ISO8601DateTime element is created, its specific date must be selected from the Date and Time Settings dialog which opens automatically when trying to edit the Value property box."><ri:attachment ri:filename="specifying_value_property_of_ISO8601DateTime.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Time Periods dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">e. Click <strong>Close,</strong> or create more dates by repeating steps c. and d.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">c) Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:image ac:title="When the Forecast Kind property value is Specified Dates, in this case three specific dates of this forecast are defined: 2016-09-23, 2017-09-23, and 2018-03-23. (from <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">2016-09-23 to 2017-09-23, and from <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">2017-09-23</span></span> to 2018-03-23</span></span>).</p><p><ac:image ac:title="The forecast is divided into two periods and displayed as two columns in the the SV-9 table after <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304006859 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00608: (2026x Refresh1) Traceability relation maps

- page_id: `304006859`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/304006859/2026x+Refresh1+Traceability+relation+maps
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T14:15:01.542+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures > (2026x Refresh1) Managing Traceability and Impact Analysis
- labels: ['traceability-relation-maps', 'capability-structural-map', 'capability-behavioral-map', 'implementation-map', 'operational-activity-implementation-map', 'operational-activity-map', 'organization-structure-map', 'system-resource-map', 'variant-map']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Relation Map diagram enables you to review and analyze relations rapidly between the elements of the whole model.

The UPDM 2 Plugin presents you a number of predefined relation maps. You will be able to change the context and scope only.

You can change the context by dragging the selected element onto the map.

The predefined relation maps are the following:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Traceability relation maps' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Traceability relation maps' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Traceability relation maps' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Traceability relation maps' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Traceability relation maps' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Traceability relation maps' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Traceability relation maps' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Traceability relation maps' space='']

**StructuralCapability Structural Map**

A Capability Structural Map depicts the structural relationships of a Capability. The predefined map includes:

- the Capability itself
- related DoDAF Performers (or MODAF Nodes)
- related concrete System Resources including Capability Configurations, Organization Types, Software System Resources

A Capability Structural Map also presents the internal compositions of each mapped element as well as the relationships among these elements.

**BehavioralCapability Behavioral Map**

A Capability Behavioral Map depicts the behavioral relationships of a Capability. The predefined map includes:

- the Capability itself
- related Operational Activities
- related System Functions

A Capability Behavioral Map also presents the internal decomposition of each mapped behavioral element as well as the relationships among these elements.

****

**ImplementationImplementation Map**

The Implementation Map depicts the implementation relationships of a DoDAF Performer (or of a MODAF Node). The predefined map includes either the DoDAF Performer (or the MODAF Node) and the concrete System Resource(s) that has (have) been indicated as implementing that operational element. For example, the Capability Configuration, Organization Type, or Software element that implements the Performer or Node. When the Performer or Node has composite structure and when the System Resources have composite structure, the Implementation Map also includes the implementation elements for the composed elements.

**Activity_ImplementationOperational Activity Implementation Map**

The Operational Activity Implementation Map depicts the behavioral implementation of an Operational Activity, relating the Operational Activity and the Systems Functions that implement the operational behavior. When the Operational Activity has composite behaviors and when the System Functions have composite functionality, the Operational Activity Implementation Map also includes the behavioral elements for the composed elements.

**Operational_ActivityOperational Activity Map**

The Operational Activity Map depicts the behavioral decomposition of an Operational Activity.

**OrganizationOrganization Structure Map**

The Organization Structure Map depicts the structural composition of an Actual Organization. The predefined map includes: the Actual Organization itself, DoDAF Individual Person Roles (or MODAF Actual Posts), and Actual Person elements. The Organization Structure Map illustrates the relationships between these organizational elements.

**SystemSystem Resource Map**

The System Resource Map depicts the structural decomposition of a concrete System Resource-such as a Capability Configuration, Organization Type, or Software-element.

**VariantVariant Map**

The Variant Map depicts concrete specializations of an abstract System Resource such as the direct specializations Service Access, Capability Configuration, and Software and any other specializations of these that appear in the modeled generalization hierarchy.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

The predefined map includes either the DoDAF Performer (or the MODAF Node) and the concrete System Resource(s) that has (have) been indicated as implementing that operational element. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304007865 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00609: (2026x Refresh1) Trustline

- page_id: `304007865`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/304007865/2026x+Refresh1+Trustline
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T14:16:04.763+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM 2.1 Operational Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1101099757

1101099759

1101099758

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: Asserts that the trustingParty (either a Node or a KnownResource) trusts the trustedParty to a given level (indicated by the level attribute).

No unit of measure is associated with the level - security architects must define their own scale of trust levels for a given architecture or set of architectures.

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| level : String | String denoting the level of Trust in the information source |

##### Extensions

UML Dependency

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="363a4eae-fddb-4d6e-b8c5-f0e76ab8b669"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1101099757</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7bb1d844-2c23-4424-9e55-9cca3e8b6e0e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1101099759</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ddea6d2a-ebab-42c1-87dc-a163c1577762"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1101099758</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3><span>Description</span></h3><p><span>MODAF/NAF: Asserts that the trustingParty (either a Node or a KnownResource) trusts the trustedParty to a given level (indicated by the level attribute).</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f3140067-416d-4866-99ad-781c8d00fd1c"><ac:rich-text-body>No unit of measure is associated with the level - security architects must define their own scale of trust levels for a given architecture or set of architectures.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>DoDAF: NA</span></p><h3><span>Architecture Framework</span></h3><p><span>MODAF, NAF</span></p><h3><span>Properties</span></h3><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Property</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><p><span>level : String</span></p></td><td><p><span>String denoting the level of Trust in the information source</span></p></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Extensions</h3><p><span>UML Dependency</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304008279 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00620: (2026x Refresh1) Using Expand/Collapse Internal Structure Compartment

- page_id: `304008279`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/304008279/2026x+Refresh1+Using+Expand+Collapse+Internal+Structure+Compartment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T14:16:21.032+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures
- labels: ['expand-internal-structure-compartment', 'collapse-internal-structure-compartment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Expand / Collapse internal structure compartment** functionality is available from the element’s smart manipulator toolbar.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you have an internal structure defined for the selected element, after clicking the Expand internal structure compartment button the compartment is expanded and the existing internal structure is displayed in it.

If you have more that one internal structure defined, none of them are displayed in the expanded compartment after clicking the **Expand internal structure compartment** button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <strong>Expand / Collapse internal structure compartment</strong> functionality is available from the element’s smart manipulator toolbar.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_internal_structure_compartment_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using Expand/Collapse Internal Structure Compartment" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>If you have an internal structure defined for the selected element, after clicking the Expand internal structure compartment button the compartment is expanded and the existing internal structure is displayed in it.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you have more that one internal structure defined, none of them are displayed in the expanded compartment after clicking the <strong>Expand internal structure compartment</strong> button.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304008257 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00621: (2026x Refresh1) Using Libraries

- page_id: `304008257`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/304008257/2026x+Refresh1+Using+Libraries
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T14:16:20.260+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures
- labels: ['libraries', 'universal-joint-task-list', 'ujtl', 'dod-it-standards-registry', 'disr', 'joint-conditions-library', 'add-standard-policy-from-disr-library']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

UPDM 2 plugin supports three element libraries:

1. The Universal Joint Task List (UJTL).
2. DoD Information Technology Standards and Profile Registry (DISR).
3. Joint Conditions.

The **Universal Joint Task List (UJTL)**, when augmented with the Service task lists, is a comprehensive integrated menu of functional tasks, conditions, measures, and criteria. It supports all levels of the Department of Defense in executing the National Military Strategy. In UPDM UJTL library consists of a list of Operational Activities representing universal joint tasks.

The **DoD IT Standards Registry (DISR)** is an online repository of IT standards formerly captured in the Joint Technical Architecture (JTA), Version 6.0. DISR replaces JTA. In UPDM, DISR is the list of standard elements that any of UPDM element may conform to. DISR Library usage is the same as UJTL library. Library item indexes are included when searching for elements within the element Selection dialog.

The **Joint Conditions Library** supports physical, military, and civil locations, where joint tasks can be performed.You can use it, when selecting Locations.

**Case Study**

Let us say, we need to add a standard or a policy for a system element using the StdV-1 Standards Profile table. We will select a standard from the DISR library.

To add a standard/policy from the DISR library

1. Create the StdV-1 diagram.
2. Create new or add an existing system element.
3. In the Standard / Policy cell of the created or added row, click the Edit button. The Select Standard dialog opens.
4. Click the DISR Library tab. The list of DISR standards is displayed.
5. Select the standard and do one of the following:
  - Double-click the selected standard to add it to the Selected elements list.
  - Click the + button to add the selected standard to the Selected elements list.
6. Click OK , after you finish selecting the standards.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>UPDM 2 plugin supports three element libraries:</p><ol><li>The Universal Joint Task List (UJTL).</li><li>DoD Information Technology Standards and Profile Registry (DISR).</li><li>Joint Conditions.</li></ol><p>The <strong>Universal Joint Task List (UJTL)</strong>, when augmented with the Service task lists, is a comprehensive integrated menu of functional tasks, conditions, measures, and criteria. It supports all levels of the Department of Defense in executing the National Military Strategy. In UPDM UJTL library consists of a list of Operational Activities representing universal joint tasks.</p><p>The <strong>DoD IT Standards Registry (DISR)</strong> is an online repository of IT standards formerly captured in the Joint Technical Architecture (JTA), Version 6.0. DISR replaces JTA. In UPDM, DISR is the list of standard elements that any of UPDM element may conform to. DISR Library usage is the same as UJTL library. Library item indexes are included when searching for elements within the element Selection dialog.</p><p>The <strong>Joint Conditions Library</strong> supports physical, military, and civil locations, where joint tasks can be performed.You can use it, when selecting Locations.</p><p> </p><p><strong>Case Study</strong></p><p>Let us say, we need to add a standard or a policy for a system element using the StdV-1 Standards Profile table. We will select a standard from the DISR library.</p><p>To add a standard/policy from the DISR library</p><hr /><ol><li>Create the StdV-1 diagram.</li><li>Create new or add an existing system element.</li><li>In the Standard / Policy cell of the created or added row, click the <strong>Edit</strong> button. The<strong> Select Standard</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Click the <strong>DISR Library</strong> tab. The list of DISR standards is displayed.</li><li>Select the standard and do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Double-click the selected standard to add it to the <strong>Selected elements</strong> list.</li><li>Click the <strong>+</strong> button to add the selected standard to the <strong>Selected elements</strong> list.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>, after you finish selecting the standards.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:alt="Selecting standards from DISR Library" ac:title="Selecting standards from DISR Library"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_standard_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using Libraries" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304007411 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00622: (2026x Refresh1) Using SysML Compliance Mode

- page_id: `304007411`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/304007411/2026x+Refresh1+Using+SysML+Compliance+Mode
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T14:15:35.353+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures
- labels: ['sysml-compliance-mode']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can use SysML capabilities in your UPDM model. For that purpose you can enable the SysML compliance mode in one of the following ways:

- Enable the SysML compliance mode while creating a new project.
- Enable the SysML compliance mode after the project is created.

To enable SysML compliance mode while creating a new project

1. Click File > New Project . The New Project dialog opens.
2. Select project template, specify its name, and location.
3. In the <project template> Project Options area, set the SysML compliance mode (UPDM L1 compliance level) property value to true .
4. Click OK , when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To turn on SysML compliance mode after the project is created

1. Click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Select General > UPDM .
3. In the **UPDM** property group, set the **SysML compliance mode (UPDM L1 compliance level)** property value to *true*. You can use the Quick filter box to find the **SysML compliance mode (UPDM L1 compliance level)** property more quickly. Simply start typing the property name in the Quick filter box.
4. Click OK .

When the SysML compliance mode is turned on, control flows are represented as dashed line in activity based diagrams. You can change the representation style to the solid line.

To change the representation to the solid line

1. Click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Select General > UPDM .
3. In the **UPDM** properties group, set the **Display solid control flows in SysML compliance mode** property value to *true*. You can use the Quick filter box to find the **Display solid control flows in SysML compliance mode** property more quickly. Simply start typing the property name in the Quick filter box.
4. Click **OK**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Case Study**

The following example describes the actual situation, how you can use the SysML compliance mode in the UPDM model.

Let us say, we need to describe a full-time equivalent (FTE) calculations in the UPDM model.

First, we need to create the typical organizational structure using OV-4 Organizational Relationship Chart diagram. The typical organizational structure defines types of departments and roles and how they are associated together in the modeled enterprise. The Department can contain any number of Employees and any number of sub Departments.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Secondly, we generate an actual organizational chart based on the typical organizational chart (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Instantiating Structures' space='']). An actual organizational chart shows concrete departments and residing employees.

Each employee works a certain number of FTEs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Thirdly, we can use the SysML Parametric Diagram to describe the recursive FTE’s calculation for the selected department and each residing sub-department.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can execute the described calculations using Cameo Simulation Toolkit.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The <strong>New Project</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select project template, specify its name, and location.</li><li>In the <strong>&lt;project template&gt; Project Options</strong> area, set the <strong>SysML compliance mode (UPDM L1 compliance level)</strong> property value to <em>true</em>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>, when you are done.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="sysml_comliance_mode_property.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using SysML Compliance Mode" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p><p>To turn on SysML compliance mode after the project is created</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304007418 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00623: (2026x Refresh1) Using SysML elements in UPDM 2 plugin

- page_id: `304007418`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/304007418/2026x+Refresh1+Using+SysML+elements+in+UPDM+2+plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T14:15:35.447+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures > (2026x Refresh1) Using SysML Compliance Mode
- labels: ['expand-sysml-toolbars', 'use-sysml-elements-in-updm-2-plugin']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SysML elements can be used to extend UPDM provided set of concepts.

SysML toolbars are available in the following diagrams:

| Icon | View |
| --- | --- |
|  |  |
|  |  |
|  |  |
|  |  |
|  | StV-1 |
|  |  |
|  |  |
|  |  |

SysML toolbars are collapsed by default.

To expand SysML toolbars

1. Click Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog, click the UPDM property group.
3. Set the System Engineer User Interface Mode property value to true .
4. Click OK .

****

**Case Study**

We will use the Requirement element, which is a SysML element, in an OV-2 diagram.

Do the following

1. Create an OV-2 diagram.
2. Create a Performer/ Node in the diagram.
3. On the SysML Requirements Diagram toolbar, click the Requirement button.
4. Create a Requirement in the diagram pane.
5. Create the Satisfy relationship between the Performer/ Node and the Requirement.

As you can see in the following figure, we have expressed that the architecture element satisfies the Requirement.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

- labels: []

917121858

917121860

917121859

##### Description

An end that describes the future state of the enterprise, without regard to how it is to be achieved; a mental image of what the future will or could be like.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| statement : VisionStatement[*] | A description of the Vision. |
| enterprisePhase : EnterprisePhase[1] | The phase which temporally locates the Vision. |

##### Extensions

UML Class

##### Related DoDAF Views

[CV-1 Vision](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/CV-1+Vision)

##### Description

UPDM: A WholeLifeEnterprise is a purposeful endeavor of any size involving people, organizations and supporting systems (including physical systems and/or processes).

MODAF/NAF: An EnterprisePhase that represents the whole existence of an enterprise.

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Class

##### Related MODAF Views

[MODAF. StV-1 Enterprise Vision](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P190/MODAF.+StV-1+Enterprise+Vision)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304007360 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00629: (2026x Refresh1) Working With Predefined Metric Suites

- page_id: `304007360`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/304007360/2026x+Refresh1+Working+With+Predefined+Metric+Suites
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T14:15:32.811+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures > (2026x Refresh1) Coverage Analysis
- labels: ['create-metric-table', 'show-metric-suites', 'hide-metric-suites', 'calculate-new-metric', 'recalculate', 'add-new-metric-with-different-parameters', 'show-abbreviation-in-column-name']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In order to start calculating metrics, first you need to create the metric table.

To create a metric table

1. In the Containment tree, right-click a package which will be a metric parameter.
2. From the shortcut menu, select **Tools** > **Metrics** > **New Metric Table**. [ATTACHMENT filename='Create_metric_table.png']
3. Define a table name, change the table owner (if needed), and select one or more desired metric suites.
4. Click **OK**, when you are done. [ATTACHMENT filename='metric_table.png']

The names of the columns can be abbreviated. If you need to change the column names to abbreviations, in the metric table toolbar, click the **Options** button and select the **Show Abbreviation in Column Name**option.

When you have used more than one metric suite, you can hide some metric suites in the table that are not relevant at that time.

To show or hide metric suites in the metric table

1. In the metric table toolbar, click the Show Metric Suites button.
2. Select the metric suites you want to show or hide.

There are three actions with the metrics. You can:

- Calculate New Metric . Select to calculate a new set of metrics using the parameters of the selected rows. Results of the calculation are added to the table.
- Recalculate . Select to recalculate the set of metrics in the selected row with the same parameters.
- Add New Metric with Different Parameters . Select to add a new metric to the table with copied parameters. Then change the parameters and recalculate the metrics.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>In order to start calculating metrics, first you need to create the metric table.</p><p>To create a metric table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree, right-click a package which will be a metric parameter.</li><li><p>From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Metrics</strong> &gt; <strong>New Metric Table</strong>.</p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_metric_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working With Predefined Metric Suites" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>Define a table name, change the table owner (if needed), and select one or more desired metric suites.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong>, when you are done.</p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="metric_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working With Predefined Metric Suites" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><p> </p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="bbc2d883-7fa9-4baa-8fbe-705bc7349248" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The names of the columns can be abbreviated. If you need to change the column names to abbreviations, in the metric table toolbar, click the <strong>Options</strong> button and select the <strong>Show Abbreviation in Column Name </strong>option.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>When you have used more than one metric suite, you can hide some metric suites in the table that are not relevant at that time.</p><p>To show or hide metric suites in the metric table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the metric table toolbar, click the <strong>Show Metric Suites</strong> button.</li><li>Select the metric suites you want to show or hide.</li></ol><p>There are three actions with the metrics. You can:</p><ul><li><strong>Calculate New Metric</strong>. Select to calculate a new set of metrics using the parameters of the selected rows. Results of the calculation are added to the table.</li><li><strong>Recalculate</strong>. Select to recalculate the set of metrics in the selected row with the same parameters.</li><li><strong>Add New Metric with Different Parameters</strong>. Select to add a new metric to the table with copied parameters. Then change the parameters and recalculate the metrics.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438454 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00630: (2026x) A1 Meta-Data Definitions

- page_id: `254438454`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438454/2026x+A1+Meta-Data+Definitions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:17.906+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint
- labels: ['a1-meta-data-definitions', 'a1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The A1 Meta-Data Definitions view specifies the categories of meta-data tag used throughout the architecture.

Architectures, particularly large ones, usually require meta-data tags to aid with searching and discovery. NAF specifies some built-in meta-data tags, such as:

- Definition.
- Assumption.
- Finding.
- Recommendation.
- Purpose.
- Approver, Approval Milestone, Modeller, Manager, Responsible Owner, Tool Used.

**Implementation**

A1 can be represented using :

- An A1 Meta-Data Definitions diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='A1.png']
- An A1 MS Word document report.
- A DARS report.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Generating document based reports' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Generating DARS report in A1' space='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Architectural Description
- Defines Architecture
- Architecture Metadata

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

NAF specifies some built-in meta-data tags, such as:</p><ul><li>Definition.</li><li>Assumption.</li><li>Finding.</li><li>Recommendation.</li><li>Purpose.</li><li>Approver, Approval Milestone, Modeller, Manager, Responsible Owner, Tool Used.<br /><br /></li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>A1 can be represented using :</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4cfe41a3-120d-4bdc-90d5-0c3ae656868d">An A1 Meta-Data Definitions diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</ac:inline-comment-marker><br /><ac:image ac:alt="A1 Meta-Data Definitions diagram" ac:title="A1 Meta-Data Definitions diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="A1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) A1 Meta-Data Definitions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>An A1 MS Word document report.</li><li>A DARS report.<br /><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating document based reports" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating DARS report in A1" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Architectural Description</li><li>Defines Architecture</li><li>Architecture Metadata</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 00634: (2026x) A8 Standards

- page_id: `254438539`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438539/2026x+A8+Standards
- version_number: 1

- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint
- labels: ['a8-standards', 'a8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A8 Standards table describes the current standards of the architecture and the systems elements that should conform to them. A8 Standards Forecast table provides a description of emerging standards and their potential impact on current systems and services view elements within a set of time frames within the architecture.

**Implementation**

A8 can be represented using:

- An A8 Standards table. [ATTACHMENT filename='A8_standards_table.png']
- An A8 Standards Forecast table. [ATTACHMENT filename='A8_forecast.png']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating A8 Standards Forecast table' space='']

- Standard
- Protocol
- Forecast

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: UPDM Extension

1097442609

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: UPDM Extension

1097442611

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: UPDM Extension

1097442610

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A PhysicalLocation (MODAF::ActualLocation, NAF::ActualLocation) is a location anywhere on the earth. The means of describing the location is a string (locationDescription). The information contained in that string is governed by the taxonomy reference - e.g. if the PhysicalLocation is a “GPS reference”, the string will contain the GPS coordinates.

#### INFO: UPDM Extension

UPDM Extension

This has been extended in UPDM to include non-earth locations.

DoDAF: All subtypes of «IndividualType» Location, such as Facility, Site, etc.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Instance Specification

The information contained in that string is governed by the taxonomy reference - e.g. if the PhysicalLocation is a “GPS reference”, the string will contain the GPS coordinates.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="79b3e519-de8b-4329-a5b3-591a23e9706f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">UPDM Extension</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>This has been extended in UPDM to include non-earth locations.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>DoDAF: All subtypes of «IndividualType» Location, such as Facility, Site, etc.</span></p><h3><span>Architecture Framework</span></h3><p><span>DoDAF, MODAF, NAF</span></p><h3><span>Extensions</span></h3><p><span>UML Instance Specification</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

1343699537

1343699539

1343699538

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: An actual specific organization, an instance of an organization class, for example, “The US Department of Defense”.

DoDAF: [DoDAF::Organization]: A specific real-world assemblage of people and other resources organized for an on-going purpose.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| ratifiedStandards : Standard[*] | The standards that were ratified by the ActualOrganization. |

##### Extensions

UML Instance Specification

## PAGE 00645: (2026x) Actual Project

- page_id: `254439036`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439036/2026x+Actual+Project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:31.900+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Project Viewpoint and Acquisition Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1337141382

1337141384

1337141383

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: (MODAF::Project, NAF::Project): A time-limited endeavour to create a specific set of products or services.

DoDAF: (DoDAF::Project): A temporary endeavor undertaken to create Resources or Desired Effects.

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| endDate : ISO8601DateTime[0..1] | An end time of a Project. |
| startDate :ISO8601DateTime[1] | A start time for a Project. |
| part : Project[0..*] | The sub-projects. |
| whole : Project[0..1] | A parent project. |
| ownedMilestones : ActualProjectMilestone[1..*] | The milestones associated with a project. |

##### Extensions

UML Instance Specification

## PAGE 00646: (2026x) Actual Project Milestone

- page_id: `254439075`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439075/2026x+Actual+Project+Milestone
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:35.495+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Project Viewpoint and Acquisition Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1337194352

1337194354

1337194353

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: (ProjectMilestone): An event in a ActualProject (MODAF::Project, NAF::Project) by which progress is measured.

In the case of an acquisition project, there are two key types of milestones which shall be represented using subtypes - IncrementMilestone (MODAF::CapabilityIncrement, NAF::CapabilityIncrement) and OutOfServiceMilestone (MODAF::OutOfService, NAF::OutOfService)

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| date : ISO8601DateTime[1] | Defines time for a ProjectMilestone. |

##### Extensions

UML Instance Specification

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439720 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00647: (2026x) Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard

- page_id: `254439720`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439720/2026x+Actual+Project+Milestone+Creation+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:31.859+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Dialogs
- labels: ['actual-project-milestone-creation-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard** is used to define a new milestone for the selected project.

To open the **Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard**

1. Open a AcV-2 (MODAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart)' space=''] (NAF 4.0), NPV-1 (NAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) PV-2 Project Timelines' space=''] (DoDAF 2.0).
2. Select a project.
3. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram toolbar, click Add New > Milestone .
  - On the selected project shortcut menu, click Add new Actual Project Milestone .
4. Select one of the available milestone kinds: The Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard opens.
  - Out Of Service Milestone
  - Increment Milestone
  - Deployed Milestone
  - No Longer Used Milestone
  - Actual Project Milestone

The wizard consists of three steps:

1. Milestone name, kind, and date specification. How to specify a name, kind, and date of a Milestone?In this step, you can specify a name of a new Milestone you are creating.You can select one of the Milestone kinds, which are:Out Of Service MilestoneIncrement MilestoneDeployed MilestoneNo Longer Used MilestoneActual Project MilestoneAlso you can set the Milestone date and time. Date and time indicates the position of the milestone in the project time frame. For more information on setting date ant time, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Date and Time Settings dialog' space=''].[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Project milestone type specification. How to specify a milestone type?You can select one (or both) of the given milestone types:DLOD MilestoneDOTMLPF MilestoneYou can create your own milestone type as well.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Project status specification at a particular Milestone. How to specify a project status of a particular Milestone?After creating a new milestone, you can apply the project status according to the selected milestone type. Each milestone type can have specific project threads and status indicators.DLOD Milestone typeDOTMLPF Milestone typeProject threadsEquipmentLogisticsInfrastructureOrganizationDoctrine/ConceptsInformationPersonnelTrainingDoctrineOrganizationTrainingMaterielLeadershipPersonnelFacilitiesStatus indicatorsDLOD AbsentNot RequiredNo Outstanding IssuesManageable IssuesCritical IssuesMateriel Solution AnalysisTechnology DevelopmentEngineering and Manufacturing DevelopmentProduction & DeploymentOperations & SupportYou can also define you own milestone type with a custom set of project threads and status indicators.To create project threads and status indicators1. In the Project Thread area, click the **Add** button.2. Specify the thread name.3. In the Status Indicator area, click the **Add** button.4. Specify the indicator name.5. Click the **Fill Color** button.6. In the **Color** dialog select the color and click **OK**.To create a second thread with the same status indicators, select a previously created thread and click the **Clone** button. A new thread with previously defined status indicators are created.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding Milestones in Lr diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding Milestones in PV-2 diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The<strong> Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard</strong> is used to define a new milestone for the selected project.</p><p>To open the <strong>Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Open a AcV-2 (MODAF),<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart)" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ Lr Lines of Development]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (NAF 4.0), NPV-1 (NAF), <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) PV-2 Project Timelines" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[PV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (DoDAF 2.0).</li><li>Select a project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Milestone</strong>.</li><li>On the selected project shortcut menu, click <strong>Add new Actual Project Milestone</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Select one of the available milestone kinds:<br /><ul><li>Out Of Service Milestone</li><li>Increment Milestone</li><li>Deployed Milestone</li><li>No Longer Used Milestone</li><li>Actual Project Milestone</li></ul>The<strong> Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard</strong> opens.</li></ol><p>The wizard consists of three steps:</p><ol><li><p>Milestone name, kind, and date specification.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="cfba52f0-5924-4171-bb25-13e507a03da1" ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to specify a  name, kind, and date of a Milestone?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In this step, you can specify a name of a new Milestone you are creating.</p><p>You can select one of the Milestone kinds, which are:</p><ul><li>Out Of Service Milestone</li><li>Increment Milestone</li><li>Deployed Milestone</li><li>No Longer Used Milestone</li><li>Actual Project Milestone</li></ul><p> </p><p>Also you can set the Milestone date and time. Date and time indicates the position of the milestone in the project time frame. For more information on setting date ant time, see<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Date and Time Settings dialog" /></ac:link>.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Specifying milestone name, kind, and date in Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" ac:title="Specifying milestone name, kind, and date in Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_project_milestone_creation_wizard_step1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Project milestone type specification.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="b6f2e06c-d94e-4e52-9132-2028c17d803a" ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to specify a milestone type?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can select one (or both) of the given milestone types:</p><ul><li>DLOD Milestone</li><li>DOTMLPF Milestone</li></ul><p>You can create your own milestone type as well.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Selecting Actual Project Milestone Type in Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" ac:title="Selecting Actual Project Milestone Type in Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_project_milestone_creation_wizard_step2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Project status specification at a particular Milestone.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="c969062e-7f9b-45eb-9945-e8af5d9cb358" ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to specify a project status of a particular Milestone?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>After creating a new milestone, you can apply the project status according to the selected milestone type. Each milestone type can have specific project threads and status indicators.</p><table><tbody><tr><th> </th><th>DLOD Milestone type</th><th>DOTMLPF Milestone type</th></tr><tr><td>Project threads</td><td><ul><li>Equipment</li><li>Logistics</li><li>Infrastructure</li><li>Organization</li><li>Doctrine/Concepts</li><li>Information</li><li>Personnel</li><li>Training</li></ul></td><td><ul><li>Doctrine</li><li>Organization</li><li>Training</li><li>Materiel</li><li>Leadership</li><li>Personnel</li><li>Facilities</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>Status indicators</td><td><ul><li>DLOD Absent</li><li>Not Required</li><li>No Outstanding Issues</li><li>Manageable Issues</li><li>Critical Issues</li></ul></td><td><ul><li>Materiel Solution Analysis</li><li>Technology Development</li><li>Engineering and Manufacturing Development</li><li>Production &amp; Deployment</li><li>Operations &amp; Support</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p>You can also define you own milestone type with a custom set of project threads and status indicators.</p><p>To create project threads and status indicators</p><hr /><p>1. In the Project Thread area, click the <strong>Add</strong> button.</p><p>2. Specify the thread name.</p><p>3. In the Status Indicator area, click the <strong>Add</strong> button.</p><p>4. Specify the indicator name.</p><p>5. Click the <strong>Fill Color</strong> button.</p><p>6. In the <strong>Color</strong> dialog select the color and click <strong>OK</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="80994452-914a-4976-ad03-8607d73c10a0" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create a second thread with the same status indicators, select a previously created thread and click the <strong>Clone</strong> button. A new thread with previously defined status indicators are created.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><ac:image ac:alt="Specifying Project status at a particular Milestone in Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" ac:title="Specifying Project status at a particular Milestone in Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_project_milestone_creation_wizard_step3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="9679fdea-7166-452c-b060-b6b9497a4189" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Adding Milestones in Lr diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Adding Milestones in PV-2 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

##### Description

UPDM: A set or collection of ActualMeasurement(s). A date of measurement can be set. An intent of ActualMeasurementSet can be “Result”, “Required”, or “Estimate”.

MODAF/NAF: NA

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| date : ISO8601DateTime[1] | The date of Actual Measurement Set. |

##### Extensions

UML Instance Specification

##### Related DoDAF views

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-7 Systems Measures Matrix' space='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-7 Services Measures Matrix' space='']

##### Related MODAF views

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) MODAF. SV-7 Resource Performance Parameters Matrix' space='']

##### Related NAF views

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) NSV-7 System Typical Quality Requirements Description' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439045 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00651: (2026x) Adding Capabilities in Cr diagram

- page_id: `254439045`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439045/2026x+Adding+Capabilities+in+Cr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:32.511+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Concepts viewpoint > (2026x) Cr Capability Roadmap
- labels: ['add-capability-in-cr', 'add-capability-provision-in-cr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a new Capability

1. On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability .
2. Select the Capability owner in the Select Owner dialog. An empty line for the newly created capability appears on the diagram pane.

To add a new Capability Provision

1. Select the Capability for which you want to create a Capability Provision.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Dr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability Provision .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, choose Add New Capability Provision .
3. Follow the steps of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space=''].**
4. Click Finish when you are done.

To add an existing Capability

1. On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Capability .
2. In the Select Capability dialog, select a Capability and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To add a new Capability</p><hr /><ol><li>On the Cr diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Capability</strong>.</li><li>Select the Capability owner in the <strong>Select Owner</strong> dialog.<br />An empty line for the newly created capability appears on the diagram pane.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To add a new Capability Provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Capability for which you want to create a Capability Provision.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the Dr diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, choose <strong>Add <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">New</span> Capability Provision</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps of the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Capability Provision Creation Wizard" /></ac:link>.</strong></span></li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To add an existing Capability</p><hr /><ol><li>On the Cr diagram toolbar, click<strong> Add Existing </strong>&gt;<strong> Capability</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, select a Capability and click the <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

**To add a new Capability**

1. On the CV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability .
2. Select the Capability owner in the Select Owner dialog. An empty line for the newly created capability appears on the diagram pane.

**To add an existing Capability**

1. On the CV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Capability .
2. In the Select Capability dialog, select a Capability and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

**To add a new Capability Provision**

1. Select the Capability for which you want to create a Capability Provision.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the CV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability Provision .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, choose Add New Capability Provision .
3. Follow the steps of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space=''].**
4. Click Finish when you are done.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439077 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00653: (2026x) Adding custom columns in tables

- page_id: `254439077`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439077/2026x+Adding+custom+columns+in+tables
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:35.659+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures > (2026x) Managing Tables
- labels: ['add-custom-columns-in-tables']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add any desired column to your table by clicking**Show Columns** button.

When generating a report, only columns that are visible in your table will be included in the output.

To add a custom column

1. On the table toolbar, click Show Columns > Select Columns .
2. In the Select Columns dialog, choose the columns you want to display in the table.
3. Click OK , when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This capability is not exhibited by CV-5, StV-5, NCV-5, SV-9, NSV-9, SvcV-9, TV-2, NTV-2, and StdV-2 tables.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can add any desired column to your table by clicking<strong> Show Columns</strong> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When generating a report, only columns that are visible in your table will be included in the output.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To add a custom column</p><hr /><ol><li>On the table toolbar, click <strong>Show</strong><strong> Columns</strong> &gt; <strong>Select</strong><strong> Columns</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select</strong><strong> Columns</strong> dialog, choose the columns you want to display in the table.</li><li>Click<strong> OK</strong>, when you are done.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exchange_kind_and_confirms_to_columns_added.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Adding custom columns in tables" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This capability is not exhibited by CV-5, StV-5, NCV-5, SV-9, NSV-9, SvcV-9, TV-2, NTV-2, and StdV-2 tables.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438595 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00654: (2026x) Adding Milestones in Lr diagram

- page_id: `254438595`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438595/2026x+Adding+Milestones+in+Lr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:53.581+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) Lr Lines of Development > (2026x) Manipulations in Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart)
- labels: ['add-milestone-in-lr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a Milestone in Lr diagram

1. Select a project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram toolbar, click Add New > Milestone .
  - On the selected project shortcut menu, click Add new Actual Project Milestone .
3. Select one of the available milestone kinds:
  - Out Of Service Milestone
  - Increment Milestone
  - Deployed Milestone
  - No Longer Used Milestone
  - Actual Project Milestone
4. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard' space='']**Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard** .
5. Click the Finish button when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To add a Milestone in Lr diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Milestone</strong>.</li><li>On the selected project shortcut menu, click <strong>Add new Actual Project Milestone</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Select one of the available milestone kinds:<br /><ul><li>Out Of Service Milestone</li><li>Increment Milestone</li><li>Deployed Milestone</li><li>No Longer Used Milestone</li><li>Actual Project Milestone</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps of the<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Finish</strong> button when you are done.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````
