<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438558 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00656: (2026x) Adding Projects in Lr diagram

- page_id: `254438558`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438558/2026x+Adding+Projects+in+Lr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:42.088+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) Lr Lines of Development > (2026x) Manipulations in Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart)
- labels: ['add-project-in-lr', 'add-sub-project-in-lr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a new Project

1. On the Lr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Project . An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.
2. Specify a time frame for the Project.

To add an existing Project

1. On the Lr diagram toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the Select Project dialog, select a Project (or several Projects) and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

To add a new Sub Project

1. Select the Project for which you want to create a Sub Project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Lr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Sub Project .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Project , choose Add new Sub Project .
3. An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.
4. Specify a time frame for the Sub Project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To add a new Project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the Lr diagram toolbar, click<strong> Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>. An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.</li><li>Specify a time frame for the Project.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To add an existing Project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the Lr diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Project</strong> dialog, select a Project (or several Projects) and click the <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To add a new Sub Project</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Project for which you want to create a Sub Project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the Lr diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Sub Project</strong>.</li><li>From the shortcut menu of the selected Project<strong>, </strong>choose<strong> Add new Sub Project</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.</li><li>Specify a time frame for the Sub Project.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bf487b99-ae88-4497-bfde-6f1c2fdba155"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">30994809</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67a4bdf2-f96a-4836-9f4d-1a375958c94f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">30994811</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4128802f-838a-4ddd-9f49-c24362b8093d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">30994810</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To add a new Project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the PV-2 diagram toolbar, click<strong> Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>. An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.</li><li>Specify a time frame for the Project.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To add an existing Project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the PV-2 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Project</strong> dialog, select a Project (or several Projects) and click the <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To add a new Sub Project</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Project for which you want to create a Sub Project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the PV-2 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Sub Project</strong>.</li><li>From the shortcut menu of the selected Project<strong>, </strong>choose<strong> Add new Sub Project</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.</li><li>Specify a time frame for the Sub Project.<br /><br /></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439080 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00658: (2026x) Adding Sub Capabilities in Cr diagram

- page_id: `254439080`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439080/2026x+Adding+Sub+Capabilities+in+Cr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:36.007+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Concepts viewpoint > (2026x) Cr Capability Roadmap
- labels: ['add-sub-capability-in-cr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding Sub Capabilities in Cr diagram' space='']or already [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding Sub Capabilities in Cr diagram' space='']Sub Capability to Cr diagram.

newTo add a new Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability for which you want to create a Sub Capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Sub Capability .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select Add New Sub Capability .
3. An empty line for the newly created Capability appears.

existingTo add an existing Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability for which you want to add a Sub Capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Sub Capability .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select Add Existing Sub Capability .
3. From the Select Capability dialog, select a Capability and click the + button.
4. Click OK when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can add <ac:link ac:anchor="new"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[new ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Adding Sub Capabilities in Cr diagram" /></ac:link>or already <ac:link ac:anchor="existing"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[existing ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Adding Sub Capabilities in Cr diagram" /></ac:link>Sub Capability to Cr diagram.</p><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="4f25bc8d-86ce-47b5-a8d3-a07f00032d8c" ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">new</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add a new Sub Capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Capability for which you want to create a Sub Capability.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the Cr diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Sub Capability</strong>.</li><li>From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select <strong>Add New Sub Capability</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>An empty line for the newly created Capability appears.</li></ol><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="c8cfecd2-13b2-45dc-8bb5-017c4d8e5100" ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">existing</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add an existing Sub Capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Capability for which you want to add a Sub Capability.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the Cr diagram toolbar, click<strong> Add Existing</strong> &gt; <strong>Sub Capability</strong>.</li><li>From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select <strong>Add Existing Sub Capability</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>From the <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, select a Capability and click the<strong> +</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 00661: (2026x) All Views viewpoint

- page_id: `254439353`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439353/2026x+All+Views+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:00.227+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['all-views-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

566188273

566188275

566188274

“There are some overarching aspects of an Architectural Description that are captured in the AV DoDAF-described Models. The AV DoDAF-described Models provide information pertinent to the entire Architectural Description rather than representing a distinct viewpoint. AV DoDAF described Models provide an overview of the architectural effort including such things as the scope, context, rules, constraints, assumptions, and the derived vocabulary that pertains to the Architectural Description. It captures the intent of the Architectural Description to help ensure its continuity in the face of leadership, organizational, and other changes that can occur over a long development effort.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

566188271

**Related Pages:**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438951 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00662: (2026x) Applying Actual Measurements

- page_id: `254438951`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438951/2026x+Applying+Actual+Measurements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:24.937+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures > (2026x) Managing Measurement Sets and Actual Measurements
- labels: ['make-element-measurable', 'set-default-value-for-actual-property-set', 'dv', 'apply-actual-measurement']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Actual Measurements can be applied to measurable elements only. 
 
 To make an element measurable

1. Create a Measurement Set with one Measurement at least.
2. Select the element you want to measure and open its Specification window.
3. Add the created Measurement Set as a value for the Measurement Types property.

You can apply the Measurement Set by dragging it from the Model Browser to the element.

As soon as you make the element measurable, define actual values for all measurements contained within the Measurement Set.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Define the default values in the Specification window of the Measurement Set and you will be able to use them when creating Actual Property Sets. You can use the default values with just one click of the **Default Value** button in the element's Specification window. 
 
To set the default value for the Actual Property Set

1. Select the element for which you want to specify the Actual Property Set and open its Specification window.
2. In the Actual <measurement type name> property group, click the **dv** button to assign the default value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- The default values are set automatically when the measurement’s multiplicity is set to 1 and more.
- When you remove all values of the Actual Measurement Set, the Actual Property Set is removed from the model as well.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

You can use the default values with just one click of the <strong>Default Value</strong> button in the element's Specification window. </span><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> To set the default value for the Actual Property Set</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Select the element for which you want to specify the Actual Property Set and open its Specification window.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">In the Actual &lt;measurement type name&gt; property group, click the <strong>dv</strong> button  to assign the default value.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="measurements_1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Applying Actual Measurements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The default values are set automatically when the measurement’s multiplicity is set to 1 and more.</li><li>When you remove all values of the Actual Measurement Set, the Actual Property Set is removed from the model as well.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><br /></span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439938 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00663: (2026x) Applying military symbols

- page_id: `254439938`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439938/2026x+Applying+military+symbols
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:45.486+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures
- labels: ['applying-military-symbols', 'using-military-symbols', 'mil-std-2525c-symbology']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Military symbols can be applied to resources in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space=''] views.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To apply military symbol for resource shape

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Select Symbol dialog' space='']**Select Symbol**dialog:
  - Create resources from MIL-STD-2525C Symbology toolbar.
  - Select resource and click [ATTACHMENT filename='military_symbols.png'] on the smart manipulator toolbar.
  - Open the Specification window of any UPDM element, select Image property cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='military_symbols.png'] .
2. Select the military symbol you want to apply, specify affiliation and frame style. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click **OK**. 
The military symbol is applied for resource shape.

## PAGE 00664: (2026x) Applying Project Status in PV-2 diagram

- page_id: `254439497`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439497/2026x+Applying+Project+Status+in+PV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:12.329+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Project viewpoint > (2026x) PV-2 Project Timelines
- labels: ['apply-project-status-in-pv-2', 'edit-project-status-in-pv-2', 'edit-status-in-pv-2', 'apply-status-in-pv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

31012031

31012034

31012032

To apply the Project Status to a new Milestone

1. From the Milestone shortcut menu, select Apply Status .
2. Specify the Project status by following the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Project Status Application Wizard' space=''] .

To edit the Project Status

1. From the Milestone shortcut menu, select Edit Status .
2. Edit the project status following the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Project Status Application Wizard' space='']. If the Milestones overlap, from the shortcut menu select **Specify Project Status** and then select the Milestone you want to edit the status for.

31012030

**Related Pages:**

**true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) PV-2 Project Timelines' space='']**

To apply the Project Status to a new Milestone

To edit the Project Status

##### Description

UPDM: Represents a visual indication of a connection used in high level operational concept diagrams. The connections are purely visual and cannot be related to any architectural semantics.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Dependency

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439644 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00667: (2026x) Architectural Description

- page_id: `254439644`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439644/2026x+Architectural+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:27.403+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 All Views Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1095585534

1095585537

1095585536

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A specification of a system of systems at a technical level which also provides the business context for the system of systems.

DoDAF: Information describing architecture.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| approvalAuthority: ActualOrganizationalResource[*] | References the actual organizational resource that has the authority to approve the architectural description. |
| architect : String[*] | The name of the architect responsible for the ArchitecturalDescription. |
| assumptionAndConstraint : String[*] | Any assumptions, constraints, and limitations contained in the ArchitecturalDescription, including those affecting deployment, communications performance, information assurance environments, etc. |
| creatingOrganization : String[1] | Describes the ActualOrganizationalResource creating the ArchitecturalDescription. |
| dateCompleted : String[0..1] | Date that the Architectural Description was completed. |
| purpose : String[1] | Explains the need for the Architecture, what it will demonstrate, the types of analyses that will be applied to it, who is expected to perform the analyses, what decisions are expected to be made on the basis of each form of analysis, who is expected to make those decisions, and what actions are expected to result. |
| recommendations : String[1] | States the recommendations that have been developed based on the architecture effort. Examples include recommended system implementations, and opportunities for technology insertion. |
| summaryOfFindings : String[1] | Summarizes the findings that have been developed so far. This may be updated several times during the development of the ArchitecturalDescription. |
| toolsUsed : String[1] | Identifies any tools used to develop the ArchitecturalDescription as well as the file names and formats if appropriate. |
| toBe : Boolean[1] | Indicates whether the ArchitecturalDescription is an existing or future one. |
| Viewpoint : String[1] |  |
| Views : View[1..*] |  |
| architectureFramework : Architecture- FrameworkKind [1] | The architectureFramework tag identifies the subset of aliases to use within the context of the ArchitecturalDescription (i. e. DoDAF, MODAF or none) |

##### Extensions

UML Package

##### Related DoDAF views

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) AV-1 Overview and Summary Information' space='']

##### Related MODAF views

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='MODAF. AV-1 Overview and Summary Information' space='UPDM2P190']

##### Related NAF views

[NAV-1 Overview and Summary Information](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P190/NAV-1+Overview+and+Summary+Information)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438452 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00669: (2026x) Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint

- page_id: `254438452`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438452/2026x+Architecture+Meta-Data+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:17.536+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['architecture-meta-data-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint provides information pertinent to the entire architecture but do not represent a distinct view of the architecture. The Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint subviews set the scope and context of the architecture. The scope includes the subject area and timeframe for the architecture. The setting in which the architecture exists comprises the interrelated conditions that compose the context for the architecture. These conditions include doctrine, tactics, techniques and procedures, relevant goals and vision statements, concepts of operations, scenarios, and environmental conditions.

#### PANEL: Views

Views

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The scope includes the subject area and timeframe for the architecture. The setting in which the architecture exists comprises the interrelated conditions that compose the context for the architecture. These conditions include doctrine, tactics, techniques and procedures, relevant goals and vision statements, concepts of operations, scenarios, and environmental conditions.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Views</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="454534d5-6a1f-4d07-8917-d3d5eb2a614d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">555693262</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8d0e8a6-e0a2-4596-a383-3a7cc673d787"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">555693264</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48983dc4-8bd0-4195-9a8e-345f4e8e9649"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">555693263</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3><span>Description</span></h3><p>MODAF/NAF: Asserts that there is a relationship between two entities (Entity Relationship).</p><p>DoDAF: A relationship or association between two elements of information.</p><h3>Architecture Framework</h3><p>DoDAF</p><h3>Extensions</h3><p>UML Association</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439730 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00672: (2026x) Automatic Instantiation wizard

- page_id: `254439730`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439730/2026x+Automatic+Instantiation+wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:32.415+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Dialogs
- labels: ['automatic-instantiation-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Automatic Instantiation**wizard is used to automatically instantiate any typical structure of UPDM architecture.

To open the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard

1. Select any number of instantiable UPDM elements and right-click them.
2. On the shortcut menu, choose Tools > Create Instance . The Automatic Instantiation wizard opens.

The wizard consists of these three steps:

1. Selecting parts. How to select a part?[IMAGE alt='' src='']In this step, select elements and their internal parts to instantiate.
2. Selecting a package. How to select a package?[IMAGE alt='' src='']In this step, select, create or clone the package to store the created actual structure.
3. Creating a diagram. How to create a diagram?[IMAGE alt='' src='']In this step, specify diagram name, select diagram type to visualize the actual structure and the package to store the diagram.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Instantiating Structures' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439355 space=UPDM2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00673: (2026x) AV-1 Overview and Summary Information

- page_id: `254439355`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439355/2026x+AV-1+Overview+and+Summary+Information
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:16:14.077+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) All Views viewpoint
- labels: ['av-1-overview-and-summary-information', 'av-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

596919250

596919253

596919251

**Description**

The overview and summary information contained within the AV-1 provides executive-level summary information in a consistent form that allows quick reference and comparison between Architectural Descriptions. The written content of the AV-1 content describes the concepts contained in the pictorial representation of the OV-1.

The AV-1 frames the context for the Architectural Description. The AV-1 includes assumptions, constraints, and limitations that may affect high-level decisions relating to an architecture-based work program. It should contain sufficient information to enable a reader to select a single Architectural Description from among many to read in more detail. The AV-1 serves two additional purposes:

- In the initial phases of architecture development, it serves as a planning guide.
- When the architecture is built, the AV-1 provides summary information concerning who, what, when, why, and how of the plan as well as a navigation aid to the models that have been created.

The AV-1 may be used to:

- Scope the architecture effort.
- Provide context to the architecture effort.
- Define the architecture effort.
- Summarize the findings from the architecture effort.
- Assist search within an architecture repository.

**Implementation**

The AV-1 can be represented:

- Using an AV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- Using the following (.docx) reports: - AV-1 Overview & Summary Information. - AV-1 DARS.

The AV-1 Overview & Summary Information report includes summarizing information about all developed viewpoints and views.

The AV-1 DARS report includes the same information as in the AV-1 Overview & Summary Information report and also some additional data that are required particularly by DoD Architecture Registry System (DARS). The AV-1 DARS report has the DARS compatible structure. Before generating the report you must load the DARS template in AV-1 and provide the template with relevant data.

596919249

**Related Pages:**

- Architectural Description
- Architectural Reference
- Architecture Metadata
- Generating document based reports
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Generating DARS report in AV-1' space='']

The AV-1 includes assumptions, constraints, and limitations that may affect high-level decisions relating to an architecture-based work program. The AV-1 serves two additional purposes:</p><ul><li>In the initial phases of architecture development, it serves as a planning guide.</li><li>When the architecture is built, the AV-1 provides summary information concerning who, what, when, why, and how of the plan as well as a navigation aid to the models that have been created.</li></ul><p>The AV-1 may be used to:</p><ul><li>Scope the architecture effort.</li><li>Provide context to the architecture effort.</li><li>Define the architecture effort.</li><li>Summarize the findings from the architecture effort.</li><li>Assist search within an architecture repository.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The AV-1 can be represented:</p><ul><li>Using an AV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li>Using the following (.docx) reports:<br /><p>- AV-1 Overview &amp; Summary Information.</p><p>- AV-1 DARS.</p></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The AV-1 Overview &amp; Summary Information report includes summarizing information about all developed viewpoints and views.</p><p>The AV-1 DARS report includes the same information as in the AV-1 Overview &amp; Summary Information report and also some additional data that are required particularly by DoD Architecture Registry System (DARS). The AV-1 DARS report has the DARS compatible structure. Before generating the report you must load the DARS template in AV-1 and provide the template with relevant data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="45f4a4d0-e187-4313-bbb1-c8fc256f896c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">596919249</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) Architectural+Description">Architectural Description</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) Architectural+Reference">Architectural Reference</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) Architecture+Metadata">Architecture Metadata</a></li><li><a href="http://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDMP/Generating+document+based+reports">Generating document based reports</a></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating DARS report in AV-1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generating DARS template in AV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254440009 space=UPDM2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00674: (2026x) AV-2 Integrated Dictionary

- page_id: `254440009`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254440009/2026x+AV-2+Integrated+Dictionary
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:16:14.216+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) All Views viewpoint
- labels: ['av-2', 'av-2-integrated-dictionary']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

650486003

650486006

650486005

**Description**

The AV-2 presents all the metadata used in an architecture. An AV-2 presents all the data as a hierarchy, provides a text definition for each one and references the source of the element (e.g., DoDAF Meta-model, a published document or policy).

An AV-2 shows elements from the DoDAF Meta-model that have been described in the Architectural Description and new elements that have been introduced by the Architectural Description.

It is essential that organizations within the DoD use the same terms to refer to a thing. Because of the interrelationship among models and across architecture efforts, it is useful to define common terminology with common definitions (referred to as taxonomies) in the development of the models within the Architectural Description. These taxonomies can be used as building blocks for DoDAF-described Models and Fit-for-Purpose Views within the Architectural Description. The need for standard taxonomies derives from lessons learned from early DoD Architectural Description development issues as well as from federation pilots conducted within the Department. Federation of Architectural Descriptions were made much more difficult because of the use of different terminology to represent the same architectural data. Use of taxonomies to build models for the architecture has the following benefits over free-text labeling:

- Provides consistency across populated views, based on DoDAF-described Models.
- Provides consistency across Architectural Descriptions.
- Facilitates Architectural Description development, validation, maintenance, and re-use.
- Traces architectural data to authoritative data sources.

**Implementation**

AV-2 can be represented:

- Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data. Since only a partial AV-2 report can be generated, the rest of data must be filled in manually.
- An AV-2 table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

650486002

**Related Pages:**

- Definition
- Alias
- Same As
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Generating document based reports' space='']

Because of the interrelationship among models and across architecture efforts, it is useful to define common terminology with common definitions (referred to as taxonomies) in the development of the models within the Architectural Description. These taxonomies can be used as building blocks for DoDAF-described Models and Fit-for-Purpose Views within the Architectural Description. The need for standard taxonomies derives from lessons learned from early DoD Architectural Description development issues as well as from federation pilots conducted within the Department. Federation of Architectural Descriptions were made much more difficult because of the use of different terminology to represent the same architectural data. Use of taxonomies to build models for the architecture has the following benefits over free-text labeling:</p><ul><li>Provides consistency across populated views, based on DoDAF-described Models.</li><li>Provides consistency across Architectural Descriptions.</li><li>Facilitates Architectural Description development, validation, maintenance, and re-use.</li><li>Traces architectural data to authoritative data sources.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>AV-2 can be represented:</p><ul><li>Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data. Since only a partial AV-2 report can be generated, the rest of data must be filled in manually.</li><li>An AV-2 table.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title=" AV-2 table" ac:alt=" AV-2 table"><ri:attachment ri:filename="AV-2_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) AV-2 Integrated Dictionary" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9af85c59-bc97-46a5-929b-e35704b36d7c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">650486002</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) Definition">Definition</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) Alias">Alias</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) Same+As">Same As</a></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating document based reports" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438897 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00675: (2026x) C1 Capability Taxonomy

- page_id: `254438897`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438897/2026x+C1+Capability+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:22.041+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['c1-capability-taxonomy', 'c1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The C1 Capability Taxonomy view covers capabilities and the hierarchical relationships between them. It specifies all the capabilities that are referenced throughout one or more architectures – i.e. one C1 may provide the definitive list of capabilities for a number of logical and resource architectures. In NAF, Capabilities are ‘enduring’, that is they are intended to provide an enduring framework across the lifetime of one or more enterprises. This means that it is feasible to develop a capability taxonomy that will apply to all Enterprise Phases listed in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) C2 Enterprise Vision' space=''], and an C1 may even cover more than one logical / resource architecture.

The capabilities may be organized into specialization hierarchies (taxonomies). A capability taxonomy persists over time (an architect may wish to specify historical, current or future capability) and may be referenced by multiple architectures.

In NAF, a capability is a description of an ability to do something. In C1, the capabilities are only described in the abstract – i.e. C1 does not specify how a capability is to be implemented. A C1 is most commonly structured as a specialization hierarchy of capabilities, with the most general at the root and most specific at the leaves. Measures of effectiveness may be specified for each capability. Note that measures of effectiveness are inherited down a capability taxonomy.

A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) C3 Capability Dependency' space=''] is the view that describes dependencies between the capabilities shown in the C1 taxonomy.

The C1 view may be used to:

- Identify an existing and required capabilities.
- Source for the derivation of cohesive sets of KURs.
- Provide a reference capabilities for multiple architectures.

**Implementation**

The C1 view can be represented using a C1 Capability Taxonomy diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating C1 diagram' space='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Exhibits
- Climate
- Environment
- Environment Property
- Light Condition
- Location

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

This means that it is feasible to develop a capability taxonomy that will apply to all Enterprise Phases listed in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) C2 Enterprise Vision" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and an C1 may even cover more than one logical / resource architecture.</p><p>The capabilities may be organized into specialization hierarchies (taxonomies). A capability taxonomy persists over time (an architect may wish to specify historical, current or future capability) and may be referenced by multiple architectures.</p><p>In NAF, a capability is a description of an ability to do something. In C1, the capabilities are only described in the abstract – i.e. C1 does not specify how a capability is to be implemented. A C1 is most commonly structured as a specialization hierarchy of capabilities, with the most general at the root and most specific at the leaves. Measures of effectiveness may be specified for each capability. Note that measures of effectiveness are inherited down a capability taxonomy.</p><p class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link">A <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) C3 Capability Dependency" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> is the view that describes dependencies between the capabilities shown in the C1 taxonomy.</p><p>The C1 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>Identify an existing and required capabilities.</li><li><span>Source for the derivation of cohesive sets of KURs.</span></li><li>Provide a reference capabilities for multiple architectures.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The C1 view can be represented using a C1 Capability Taxonomy diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="C1 Capability Taxonomy" ac:title="C1 Capability Taxonomy"><ri:attachment ri:filename="c1_capability_taxonomy.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) C1 Capability Taxonomy" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating C1 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capability</li><li>Exhibits</li><li>Climate</li><li>Environment</li><li>Environment Property</li><li>Light Condition</li><li>Location</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439008 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00676: (2026x) C2 Enterprise Vision

- page_id: `254439008`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439008/2026x+C2+Enterprise+Vision
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:29.918+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['c2-enterprise-vision', 'c2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The C2 Enterprise Vision view provides the high-level scope for the architecture and a strategic context for the capabilities the architecture contains. In essence it describes the end-state for any business transformation activity. The purpose of an C2 is to provide a strategic context for the capabilities described in the architecture and to specify the scope for the architecture. The C2 describes the vision, goals, enduring tasks and capabilities using terminology that is easily understood by non-technical readers.

The Enterprise Goals and Capabilities are ‘enduring’, that is they are intended to provide an enduring framework across the lifetime of the enterprise that is being modeled. An Enterprise is essentially time-bounded: it has a start and end date. It is broken into a set of phases for capability planning purposes. The time periods associated with these phases are sometimes referred to as ‘epochs’ by military planners although NAF does not use this specific terminology. It is sometimes necessary in NAF to identify one Enterprise Phase as being that represents the ‘As Is’ capability of the Enterprise. The Enterprise Goals relate to the entire enterprise lifetime, they are not specific to an Enterprise Phase. Each goal may optionally include attributes associated with the measurable outcomes that relate to it.

The C2 only shows the capabilities exhibited by enterprises. It does not show how the enterprise is structured in order to deliver those capabilities – [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) L2 Logical Scenario' space=''], is used to define the logical structure of the enterprise, and the individual logical nodes in the enterprise that deliver the capability. P1 & D2are used to show the physical and organizational structure of the enterprise.

The C2 view may be used to:

- Capture and communication of the strategic vision related to capability evolution.
- Identify the capabilities required to meet the vision and goals.
- Identify the required timescales for the capabilities (as opposed to Cr which provides a summary of when projects are estimated to deliver capability).
- Identify any enduring tasks the enterprise performs.

It should be noted that visions for capabilities of the enterprise should therefore be placed within entitiesstereotyped as «EnterpriseVision».

**Implementation**

The C2 view can be represented using a C2 Enterprise Vision diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating C2 diagram' space='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Enterprise Goal
- Enterprise Vision
- Enterprise Phase
- Whole-Life Enterprise
- Structural Part
- Temporal Part
- Enduring Task
- Capability
- Exhibits

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The C2 Enterprise Vision view provides the high-level scope for the architecture and a strategic context for the capabilities the architecture contains. In essence it describes the end-state for any business transformation activity. The purpose of an C2 is to provide a strategic context for the capabilities described in the architecture and to specify the scope for the architecture. The C2 describes the vision, goals, enduring tasks and capabilities using terminology that is easily understood by non-technical readers.</p><p>The Enterprise Goals and Capabilities are ‘enduring’, that is they are intended to provide an enduring framework across the lifetime of the enterprise that is being modeled. An Enterprise is essentially time-bounded: it has a start and end date. It is broken into a set of phases for capability planning purposes. The time periods associated with these phases are sometimes referred to as ‘epochs’ by military planners although NAF does not use this specific terminology. It is sometimes necessary in NAF to identify one Enterprise Phase as being that represents the ‘As Is’ capability of the Enterprise. The Enterprise Goals relate to the entire enterprise lifetime, they are not specific to an Enterprise Phase. Each goal may optionally include attributes associated with the measurable outcomes that relate to it.</p><p>The C2 only shows the capabilities exhibited by enterprises. It does not show how the enterprise is structured in order to deliver those capabilities – <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) L2 Logical Scenario" /></ac:link>, is used to define the logical structure of the enterprise, and the individual logical nodes in the enterprise that deliver the capability. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">P1 &amp; D2 </span>are used to show the physical and organizational structure of the enterprise.</p><p>The C2 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>Capture and communication of the strategic vision related to capability evolution.</li><li>Identify the capabilities required to meet the vision and goals.</li><li>Identify the required timescales for the capabilities (as opposed to Cr which provides a summary of when projects are estimated to deliver capability).</li><li>Identify any enduring tasks the enterprise performs.</li></ul><p>It should be noted that visions for capabilities of the enterprise should therefore be placed within entities<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> stereotyped as «EnterpriseVision».</span></p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The C2 view can be represented using a C2 Enterprise Vision diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="C2 Enterprise Vision" ac:title="C2 Enterprise Vision"><ri:attachment ri:filename="C2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) C2 Enterprise Vision" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating C2 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Enterprise Goal</li><li>Enterprise Vision</li><li>Enterprise Phase</li><li>Whole-Life Enterprise</li><li>Structural Part</li><li>Temporal Part</li><li>Enduring Task</li><li>Capability</li><li>Exhibits</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439003 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00677: (2026x) C3 Capability Dependency

- page_id: `254439003`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439003/2026x+C3+Capability+Dependency
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:29.554+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['c3-capability-dependency', 'c3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The C3 Capability Dependency view describes the dependencies between capabilities. It also defines logical composition of capabilities (i.e.capability clusters). The C3 is intended to provide a means of analyzing the dependencies between capabilities and between capability clusters. The composition of capabilities (into clusters) is logical, and the purpose of the clusters is to guide enterprise management.

The C3 view contrasts with [C1](http://documentation.nomagic.com/display/UPDM182/C1+Capability+Taxonomy)which only addresses specialization and composition relationships (i.e. capability taxonomy).

The C3 view may be used to:

- Identify capability dependencies.
- Manage capability (impact analysis for options, disposal etc).

**Implementation**

The C3 view can be represented using a C3 Capability Dependency diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating C3 diagram' space='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Capability Property

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

It also defines logical composition of capabilities (i.e.capability clusters). The C3 is intended to provide a means of analyzing the dependencies between capabilities and between capability clusters. The composition of capabilities (into clusters) is logical, and the purpose of the clusters is to guide enterprise management.</p><p>The C3 view contrasts with <a href="http://documentation.nomagic.com/display/UPDM182/C1+Capability+Taxonomy">C1 </a>which only addresses specialization and composition relationships (i.e. capability taxonomy).</p><p>The C3 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>Identify capability dependencies.</li><li>Manage capability (impact analysis for options, disposal etc).</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The C3 view can be represented using a C3 Capability Dependency diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt=" C3 Capability Dependency" ac:height="552" ac:title=" C3 Capability Dependency" ac:width="542"><ri:attachment ri:filename="C3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) C3 Capability Dependency" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating C3 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capability</li><li>Capability Property</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438967 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00678: (2026x) C4 Standard Processes

- page_id: `254438967`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438967/2026x+C4+Standard+Processes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:26.222+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['c4-standard-processes', 'c4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The C4 Standard Processes view specifies Standard Operational Activities, and optionally traces them to the capabilities they support. Some processes are standard across the whole enterprise, or even more than one enterprise. The Standard Operational Activities can be re-used across multiple logical architectures (e.g. in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) L4 Logical Activities' space='']).

The C4 view may be used to:

- Specify a doctrine.
- Trace capabilities to enduring tasks.
- Trace capabilities to standard operational activities.
- Audit a capability.

**Implementation**

The C4 view can be represented using a C4 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Capabilities will be used as row elements and the Standard Operational Activities will be used as column elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating C4 matrix' space='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Standard Operational Activity
- Maps to Capability

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The purpose of C5 Effects view is to identify effects, their consequential effects, and the capabilities that can bring about those effects.

**Implementation**

The C5 view can be represented using the C5 Effects diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438981 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00680: (2026x) C7 Performance Parameters

- page_id: `254438981`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438981/2026x+C7+Performance+Parameters
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:27.754+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['c7-performance-parameters', 'c7']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The C7 Performance Parameters view lists the measures of effectiveness and the Capabilities they are applicable to. The capability requirements (and existing capabilities) are often expressed in terms of measures of effectiveness (MoE). These are high-level metrics used to judge the level of capability.

The C7 view does not present any values for measures of effectiveness, it simply specifies what types of measure apply to each Capability.

The C7 view may be used to:

- Set a Capability Requirements.
- Military Estimates.
- Strategic Reviews.
- Plan an Assumptions.

**Implementation**

The C7 view can be represented using:

- C7 Actual Performance Parameters table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- C7 Performance Parameters table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating C7 typical table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating C7 Actual table' space='']Creating C7 Actual table

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Measurement Set
- Measurement

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

915142784

915142786

915142785

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A high level specification of the enterprise's ability.

DoDAF: The ability to achieve a desired effect under specified [performance] standards and conditions through combinations of ways and means [activities and resources] to perform a set of activities.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| environmentConditions : Environment[0..*] | The environmental conditions pertinent to this Capability typically including weather, threat, security, terrain, etc. |

##### Extensions

UML Class

## PAGE 00682: (2026x) Capability Configuration

- page_id: `254438672`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438672/2026x+Capability+Configuration

- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Systems Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

314878458

314878461

314878459

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A composite structure representing the physical and human resources (and their interactions) in an enterprise. A CapabilityConfiguration is a set of artifacts or an organization configured to provide a capability, and should be guided by [doctrine] which may take the form of Standard or OperationalConstraint stereotypes.

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| doctrine : Constraint[1..*] | Represents the doctrinal line of the capability development. |
| deployedMilestone : ConfigurationDeployed[*] | The milestone at which the configuration is deployed. |
| noLongerUsedMilestone : ConfigurationNoLongerUsed[0..1] | The milestone at which the configuration is no longer used. |
| incrementMilestone : CapabilityIncrementMilestone[*] | The milestone for incrementing a CapabilityConfiguration. |
| outOfServiceMilestone : OutOfServiceMilestone[0..1] | The milestone at which the capability is out of service. |

##### Extensions

UML Class

916399279

916399282

916399281

##### Description

UPDM:A couple that represents the capability that a resource, node or enterprise phase exhibits (Exhibits).

MODAF/NAF: An assertion that a Node is required to have a Capability (Capability for node).

DoDAF: A couple that represents the capability that a performer has.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Dependency

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439764 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00685: (2026x) Capability Provision Creation Wizard

- page_id: `254439764`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439764/2026x+Capability+Provision+Creation+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:34.713+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Dialogs
- labels: ['capability-provision-creation-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Capability Provision Creation Wizard** is used to define a Capability Provision by one or more Resources at ta specific period of time.

To open the **Capability Provision Creation Wizard**

1. Open a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) CV-3 Capability Phasing' space=''] (DoDAF), StV-3 (MODAF), NCV-3 (NAF), or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Cr Capability Roadmap' space=''] (NAF 4.0)
2. Select a Capability.
3. Do one of the following: The Capability Provision Creation Wizard opens.
  - On the <viewpoint name> diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability Provision .
  - Right-click the Capability and select Add New Capability Provision .

The wizard consists of three steps:

1. Resources selection. How to select the existing or create new resources?In this step, you can select the existing or create new resources.For more information on selecting elements, see **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**, Step #2.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Capability increment date specification. How to specify a the date and time?In this step, you can set the date and time, when the resource should begin provisioning the capability. The increment milestone is created.For more information on setting the day and time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Date and Time Settings dialog' space='']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Out of service date specification (Optional). How to specify out of service date?In this step, you can specify when the Resource should stop provisioning the Capability. The out of service Milestone is created. This step is optional. If you skip this step, the capability exhibition bar on the chart will have the triangle ending.For more information on setting date ant time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Date and Time Settings dialog' space='']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding Capabilities in Cr diagram' space='']
- Adding Capabilities to CV-3 diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The increment milestone is created.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="aa8fd4be-5129-4f6f-9ff4-a6afe4a23067" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information on setting the day and time, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Date and Time Settings dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Date and Time Settings</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:alt="Specifying Capability increment date in Capability Provision Creation Wizard" ac:title="Specifying Capability increment date in Capability Provision Creation Wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="capability_provision_creation_wizard_step2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Capability Provision Creation Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Out of service date specification (Optional).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="e6291c54-4992-427b-9270-3b035891658c" ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to specify out of service date?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In this step, you can specify when the Resource should stop provisioning the Capability. The out of service Milestone is created. This step is optional. If you skip this step, the capability exhibition bar on the chart will have the triangle ending.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="09aff95e-3003-40fc-9a26-4c1a8dd7f254" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information on setting date ant time, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Date and Time Settings dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Date and Time Settings</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:alt="Specifying out of service date in Capability Provision Creation Wizard" ac:title="Specifying out of service date in Capability Provision Creation Wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="capability_provision_creation_wizard_step3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Capability Provision Creation Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="405bc295-5fce-4966-a72f-8163549c0f6a" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Adding Capabilities in Cr diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><a href="http://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDMP/Adding+Capabilities+to+CV-3+diagram">Adding Capabilities to CV-3 diagram</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438367 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00686: (2026x) Capability viewpoint

- page_id: `254438367`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438367/2026x+Capability+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:07.172+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['capability-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

845143062

845143064

845143063

“The Capability Viewpoint and the DoDAF-described Models within the viewpoint are introduced into DoDAF V2.0 to address the concerns of Capability Portfolio Managers. In particular, the Capability Models describe capability taxonomy and capability evolution.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

“The DoD increasingly employs incremental acquisition to help manage the risks of complex procurements. Consequently, there is a need to provide visualizations of the evolving capabilities so that Portfolio Managers can synchronize the introduction of capability increments across a portfolio of projects. The Capability Models included within DoDAF are based on the program and capability information used by Portfolio Managers to capture the increasingly complex relationships between interdependent projects and capabilities.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

800700

800700

845143061

**Related Pages:**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438895 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00691: (2026x) Concepts viewpoint

- page_id: `254438895`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438895/2026x+Concepts+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:21.984+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['concepts-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Concepts viewpoint supports the process of analyzing and optimizing the delivery of capabilities in line with NATO‘s strategic intent. It achieves this by capturing essential elements of NATO‘s strategic vision, concepts, NATO‘s capability planning process, and decomposing this data into a capability taxonomy. The taxonomy is augmented with schedule data and measures of effectiveness to enable the analysis of capability gaps and overlaps. The Concepts viewpoint further details the dependencies between capabilities, enabling capability options to be built in a more coherent manner and effective trade-offs to be conducted across NATO common funded programmes.

#### PANEL: Views

Views

1

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

It achieves this by capturing essential elements of NATO‘s strategic vision, concepts, NATO‘s capability planning process, and decomposing this data into a capability taxonomy. The taxonomy is augmented with schedule data and measures of effectiveness to enable the analysis of capability gaps and overlaps. The <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Concepts viewpoint</span> further details the dependencies between capabilities, enabling capability options to be built in a more coherent manner and effective trade-offs to be conducted across NATO common funded programmes.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Views</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children"><ac:parameter ac:name="depth">1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

1096512645

1096512647

1096512646

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A definition of the conditions in which something exists or functions. An Environment may be specified in terms of LocationType (e.g. terrain), Climate (e.g. tropical), and LightCondition (e.g. dark, light, dusk, etc.).

DoDAF: An object that encompasses meteorological, geographic, and control features mission significance.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Data Type

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438885 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00695: (2026x) Converting Model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks

- page_id: `254438885`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438885/2026x+Converting+Model+between+Enterprise+Architecture+Frameworks
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:21.672+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures
- labels: ['convert-to', 'convert-model-between-enterprise-architecture-frameworks']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To convert an enterprise model to another Enterprise Architecture Framework (EAF)

1. Do either:
  - Change the perspective to another EAF's perspective.
  - Click **File** > **Convert****To** and select an EAF.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Question dialog' space='']**Question** dialog, specify the model conversion options.
3. Click Yes .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439087 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00696: (2026x) Coverage Analysis

- page_id: `254439087`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439087/2026x+Coverage+Analysis
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:36.817+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures
- labels: ['coverage-analysis', 'metrics']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Coverage analysis is based on the calculation of your model parameters. For this purpose, metrics are introduced. You can calculate metrics for the selected elements using the predefined metric suites. Also you can create your own metrics suites from a scratch or based on the predefined metric suites.

Metric is an item of information about some specific aspect of the model. The collection of various metrics can be used to evaluate the current state of your model. Calculated at regular intervals they can help tracking the evolution of the model development. The results of metric calculation are stored in the metric tables.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

You can calculate metrics for the selected elements using the predefined metric suites. Also you can create your own metrics suites from a scratch or based on the predefined metric suites.</p><p>Metric is an item of information about some specific aspect of the model. The collection of various metrics can be used to evaluate the current state of your model. Calculated at regular intervals they can help tracking the evolution of the model development. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439042 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00697: (2026x) Cr Capability Roadmap

- page_id: `254439042`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439042/2026x+Cr+Capability+Roadmap
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:32.204+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['cr-capability-roadmap', 'cr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Cr Capability Roadmap view provides a representation of the actual or estimated availability of capabilities over a period of time (derived from capability delivery milestones in acquisition projects). The Cr view support the Capability Audit process and similar processes used across different communities of interest by providing a method to identify gaps or duplication in capability provision. The Cr view indicates capability increments, which are derived from delivery milestones within acquisition projects.

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) C2 Enterprise Vision' space=''] view specifies the requirement for capability at each Enterprise Phase, whereas the Cr view reflects the output of capability programmes (e.g. acquisition, training, etc.) over time. The requirement specified in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) C2 Enterprise Vision' space='']may or may not be met by those programmes.**

The Cr view may be used to:

- Plan Capability (capability phasing).
- Plan Capability integration.
- Analyze Capability gap.
- Manage acquisition with High-level dashboard.

**Implementation**

The Cr view can be represented using a Cr Capability Roadmap diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Cr diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding Capabilities in Cr diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding Sub Capabilities in Cr diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying all Sub Capabilities in Cr diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying Sub Capabilities deleted from Cr diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Removing Capabilities from Cr diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Removing Sub Capabilities from Cr diagram' space='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Capability Configuration
- Exhibits
- Increment Milestone
- Out Of Service Milestone
- Actual Project

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The requirement specified in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) C2 Enterprise Vision" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C2 ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>may or may not be met gap.</li><li>Manage acquisition with High-level dashboard.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Cr view can be represented using a Cr Capability Roadmap diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Cr Capability Roadmap diagram " ac:title="Cr Capability Roadmap diagram "><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cr.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Cr Capability Roadmap" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating Cr diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Adding
