<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438591 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00698: (2026x) Creating A8 Standards Forecast table

- page_id: `254438591`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438591/2026x+Creating+A8+Standards+Forecast+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:52.790+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint > (2026x) A8 Standards
- labels: ['create-a8-standards-forecast-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an A8 Standards Forecast table

1. Add Rows to the table by doing any of the following:
  - Adding a new Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the Add New button, select a Standard or Protocol and its owner.
  - Adding an existing Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the Add Existing button and select one or more existing Standards or/and Protocols.
2. Add columns to the table by clicking the Time Period button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Time Periods dialog' space='']**Time Periods** dialog opens. Specify the forecast time periods:
  - Select or create a Timeline package to store the Forecast dates.
  - Select the Forecast Kind.
  - Specify additional options needed according to the selected Forecast Kind.
3. Enter the Subjects of Forecast by clicking the **…** button on the cell you want to fill in and select the Standards and Protocols to forecast. Specify the date of the Forecast. By default the date is the starting date of the selected time period.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an A8 Standards Forecast table</p><hr /><ol><li>Add Rows to the table by doing any of the following:<br /><ul><li>Adding a new Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the <strong>Add New</strong> button, select a Standard or Protocol and its owner.</li><li>Adding an existing Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select one or more existing Standards or/and Protocols.</li></ul></li><li>Add columns to the table by clicking the <strong>Time Period</strong> button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Time Periods dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Time Periods</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens. Specify the forecast time periods:<br /><ul><li>Select or create a Timeline package to store the Forecast dates.</li><li>Select the Forecast Kind.</li><li>Specify additional options needed according to the selected Forecast Kind.</li></ul></li><li><p>Enter the Subjects of Forecast by clicking the <strong>…</strong> button on the cell you want to fill in and select the Standards and Protocols to forecast. Specify the date of the Forecast.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="f1570457-54e3-4d88-a1ad-2a875582754a" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default the date is the starting date of the selected time period.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438543 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00699: (2026x) Creating A8 Standards table

- page_id: `254438543`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438543/2026x+Creating+A8+Standards+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:37.996+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint > (2026x) A8 Standards
- labels: ['create-a8-standards-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an A8 Standards table, do either

- Add a new element by clicking the Add New button. In the Create element wizard select element and its owner.
- Add the existing elements by clicking the Add Existing button and in the Select UPDM element dialog select the elements.
- Assign one or more Standards or Protocols to each row that the particular UPDM element must conform to by clicking the **…** button on any cell in the **Standard/Policy** column.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an A8 Standards table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new element by clicking the <strong>Add New</strong> button. In the <strong>Create element</strong> wizard select element and its owner.</li><li>Add the existing elements by clicking the<strong> Add Existing</strong> button and in the <strong>Select UPDM element</strong> dialog select the elements.</li><li><p>Assign one or more Standards or Protocols to each row that the particular UPDM element must conform to by clicking the <strong>…</strong> button on any cell in the <strong>Standard/Policy</strong> column.</p></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439928 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00700: (2026x) Creating Blank UPDM Project

- page_id: `254439928`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439928/2026x+Creating+Blank+UPDM+Project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:45.072+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures
- labels: ['new-project', 'create-blank-updm-project']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create the blank UPDM project. In that project, there will be no predefined structure, so you will be able to make your own. 
 
 To create a blank project

1. Do one of the following:
  - Select **File**>**New** **Project**.
  - On the main toolbar, click the **New** **Project** button.
  - Press Ctrl+N.
2. In the **New****Project** dialog, under **Enterprise Modeling**, select **Blank****UPDM****Project**.
3. Specify name, location, and other preferences if needed.
4. Click **OK** when you are done.

By default, DoDAF 2.0 Architectural Framework will be selected in the **General** property group. You can select any of the available Architectural Frameworks.

You can always change the Architectural Framework in the **Project Options** dialog **General project options** group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can create the blank UPDM project. In that project, there will be no predefined structure, so you will be able to make your own. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To create a blank project</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Do one of the following:<br /></span></span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Select <strong>File</strong>&gt;<strong>New</strong> <strong>Project</strong>. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> On the main toolbar, click the <strong>New</strong> <strong>Project</strong> button.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Press Ctrl+N.</span></li></ul><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></li><li class="_mce_tagged_br"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the <strong style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">New</strong><strong> Project</strong> dialog, under <strong>Enterprise Modeling</strong>, select <strong>Blank </strong><strong>UPDM</strong><strong> Project</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Specify name, location, and other preferences if needed.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</span></li></ol><p>By default, DoDAF 2.0 Architectural Framework will be selected in the <strong>General</strong> property group. You can select any of the available Architectural Frameworks.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can always change the Architectural Framework in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog <strong>General project options</strong> group.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:alt="New Project dialog" ac:title="New Project dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_project_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating Blank UPDM Project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><span style="color: rgb(167,165,165);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(167,165,165);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(167,165,165);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(167,165,165);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(167,165,165);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(167,165,165);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(167,165,165);"> </span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438900 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00701: (2026x) Creating C1 diagram

- page_id: `254438900`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438900/2026x+Creating+C1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:22.251+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Concepts viewpoint > (2026x) C1 Capability Taxonomy
- labels: ['create-c1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a C1 diagram

1. Create Capabilities.
2. Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general - specific relationships) or Aggregations (whole-part relationships).

You can link Capabilities with the Logical and Physical Resource elements using Exhibits relationship. To specify environmental requirements for Capability exhibition fill in the Exhibits relationship Environment Conditions property.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a C1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Capabilities.</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general - specific relationships) or Aggregations (whole-part relationships).</li></ol><p>You can link Capabilities with the Logical and Physical Resource elements using Exhibits relationship. To specify environmental requirements for Capability exhibition fill in the Exhibits relationship Environment Conditions property.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439411 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00702: (2026x) Creating C1-S1 matrix

- page_id: `254439411`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439411/2026x+Creating+C1-S1+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:03.712+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Service viewpoint > (2026x) S1 Service Taxonomy
- labels: ['create-c1-s1-matrix', 'build-c1-s1-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The rows of this matrix represent Service Interfaces and the columns represent Capabilities.

To create C1-S1 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Service Interfaces).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The rows of this matrix represent Service Interfaces and the columns represent Capabilities.</p><p>To create C1-S1 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Service Interfaces).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439011 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00703: (2026x) Creating C2 diagram

- page_id: `254439011`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439011/2026x+Creating+C2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:30.118+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Concepts viewpoint > (2026x) C2 Enterprise Vision
- labels: ['create-c2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a C2 diagram

1. Create a Whole Life Enterprise.
2. Add the Structural and Temporal Parts for the Whole Life Enterprise by clicking [ATTACHMENT filename='create_element_from_smart_manipulator.png'] smart manipulator.
3. Specify the types of parts. The types for both parts are the Enterprise Phase elements.

Additionally the Enterprise Goals and Visions can be created and added to the Enterprise Phase’s or Whole Life Enterprise’s goal and vision property.

To complete fully the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) C2 Enterprise Vision' space=''], the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) C1 Capability Taxonomy' space=''] should be modeled in order to create Capabilities that Enterprise Phase and Whole Life Enterprise exhibit. The Exhibits relationship specifies the relation between Capabilities and Enterprises.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a C2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Whole Life Enterprise.</li><li>Add the Structural and Temporal Parts for the Whole Life Enterprise by clicking <ac:image ac:alt="Create Element" ac:title="Create Element"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_from_smart_manipulator.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating C2 diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> smart manipulator.</li><li><p>Specify the types of parts.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The types for both parts are the Enterprise Phase elements.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p>Additionally the Enterprise Goals and Visions can be created and added to the Enterprise Phase’s or Whole Life Enterprise’s goal and vision property.</p><p>To complete fully the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) C2 Enterprise Vision" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) C1 Capability Taxonomy" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> should be modeled in order to create Capabilities that Enterprise Phase and Whole Life Enterprise exhibit. The Exhibits relationship specifies the relation between Capabilities and Enterprises.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

2. Connect the Capabilities using Dependencies.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438970 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00705: (2026x) Creating C4 matrix

- page_id: `254438970`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438970/2026x+Creating+C4+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:26.553+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Concepts viewpoint > (2026x) C4 Standard Processes
- labels: ['create-c4', 'build-c4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The rows of the matrix are Capabilities and the columns are Standard Operational Activities.

To create a C4 matrix

1. Specify the Rows Scope (Capabilities).
2. Specify the Columns Scope (Standard Operational Activities).
3. Click the Refresh button.

Standard Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps to Capability relationship.

To map Standard Operational Activity to Capability

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The rows of the matrix are Capabilities and the columns are Standard Operational Activities.</p><p>To create a C4 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Rows Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Columns Scope</strong> (Standard Operational Activities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Refresh</strong> button.</li></ol><p>Standard Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps to Capability relationship.</p><p> </p><p>To map Standard Operational Activity to Capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438985 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00707: (2026x) Creating C7 Actual table

- page_id: `254438985`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438985/2026x+Creating+C7+Actual+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:27.914+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Concepts viewpoint > (2026x) C7 Performance Parameters
- labels: ['create-c7-actual-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a C7 Actual table, do either

- Add a new measurable Capability by clicking the Add New button and select one or more Capabilities that have at least one Measurement Set, defined (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) C7 Performance Parameters' space=''] ), and then specify the values of each Actual Measurement in the table cells.
- Add the existing Actual Property Sets by clicking the Add Existing button and select the existing Actual Property Sets.
- Add the missing Actual Measurements by clicking the **Add Missing Measurements** button to update model changes to the table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a C7 Actual table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new measurable Capability by clicking the <strong>Add New</strong> button and select one or more Capabilities that have at least one Measurement Set, defined (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) C7 Performance Parameters" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C7]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>), and then specify the values of each Actual Measurement in the table cells. </li><li>Add the existing Actual Property Sets by clicking the <strong>Add Existing </strong> button and select the existing Actual Property Sets.</li><li><p>Add the missing Actual Measurements by clicking the <strong>Add Missing Measurements</strong> button to update model changes to the table.</p></li></ul><p style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438996 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00708: (2026x) Creating C7 typical table

- page_id: `254438996`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438996/2026x+Creating+C7+typical+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:28.988+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Concepts viewpoint > (2026x) C7 Performance Parameters
- labels: ['create-c7-typical-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a C7 typical table, do either

- Add a new Measurement Set by clicking the Add New button. The new row in the table is created. Type name, add Measurements, and edit Resources in the table cells.
- Add an existing Measurement Sets by clicking the **Add Existing** button and select existing Measurement Sets from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a C7 typical table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Measurement Set by clicking the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The new row in the table is created. Type name, add Measurements, and edit Resources in the table cells.</li><li><p>Add an existing Measurement Sets by clicking the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select existing Measurement Sets from the model.</p></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439055 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00709: (2026x) Creating Cr diagram

- page_id: `254439055`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439055/2026x+Creating+Cr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:33.236+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Concepts viewpoint > (2026x) Cr Capability Roadmap
- labels: ['create-cr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you choose to create Cr diagram, **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']** opens.

To create a Cr diagram from the wizard

1. Right-click the Cr Package and select Create Diagram > Cr Capability Roadmap .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space=''].
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create Cr Capability Roadmap** check box.

To create an Cr diagram manually when **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']** is disabled

1. Right-click the Cr Package and select Create Diagram > Cr Capability Roadmap .
2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter. The blank Cr Capability Roadmap chart is created.

To restore the usage of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UPDM options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>When you choose to create Cr diagram, <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> opens.</p><p>To create a Cr diagram from the wizard</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Cr Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Cr Capability Roadmap</strong>.</li><li>Follow the steps of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</strong></li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not wish to use the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> next time, clear the <strong>Show the wizard next time, when I create Cr Capability Roadmap</strong> check box.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an Cr diagram manually when <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> is disabled</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Cr Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Cr Capability Roadmap</strong>.</li><li><p>Type the diagram name.</p></li><li>Press Enter. <br />The blank Cr Capability Roadmap chart is created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To restore the usage of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Select<strong> Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog select the <strong>UPDM</strong> options group.</li><li>In the General properties, set the <strong>Show CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart</strong> property value to <em>true</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439409 space=UPDM2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00710: (2026x) Creating CV-1 diagram

- page_id: `254439409`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439409/2026x+Creating+CV-1+diagram
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:16:14.565+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Capability viewpoint > (2026x) CV-1 Vision
- labels: ['create-cv-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1355067341

1355067343

1355067342

The CV-1 should be the first product for DoDAF 2.0 architectures.

**To create a CV-1 diagram**

1. Create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enterprise Phase' space='UPDM2P190'] .
2. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Vision' space='UPDM2P190'] and define a Vision Statement .
3. Associate the Vision with the Enterprise Phase.
4. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UPDM2P190'] .
5. Connect the Capability and the Enterprise Phase using the Capability of Performer relationship.

An additional Enterprise Phase can be created and linked to Goals and the Vision by using the goal and vision properties of the Enterprise Phase.

In order to complete the CV-1, a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space=''] should be modelled to create the Capabilities that Enterprise Phase exhibits.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="75470d55-b66e-4a64-a044-071ad55ac81b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355067341</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="336ed9a4-3300-4f1a-aefa-9f622956cfe2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355067343</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7ad3e002-77f7-4351-923f-d27aba4cfd99"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355067342</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The CV-1 should be the first product for DoDAF 2.0 architectures.</p><p><strong>To create a CV-1 diagram</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Create an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P190" ri:content-title="Enterprise Phase" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Create a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P190" ri:content-title="Vision" /></ac:link> and define a <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) Vision+Statement">Vision Statement</a>.</li><li>Associate the Vision with the Enterprise Phase.</li><li>Create a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P190" ri:content-title="Capability" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Connect the Capability and the Enterprise Phase using the Capability of Performer relationship.</li></ol><p>An additional Enterprise Phase can be created and linked to Goals and the Vision by using the goal and vision properties of the Enterprise Phase.</p><p>In order to complete the CV-1, a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) CV-2 Capability Taxonomy" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> should be modelled to create the Capabilities that Enterprise Phase exhibits.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439398 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00711: (2026x) Creating CV-2 diagram

- page_id: `254439398`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439398/2026x+Creating+CV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:03.054+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Capability viewpoint > (2026x) CV-2 Capability Taxonomy
- labels: ['create-cv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1414337094

1414337097

1414337096

The CV-2 should be modeled just before the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) CV-1 Vision' space=''] is completed.

**To create a CV-2 diagram**

1. Create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Capability' space=''] .
2. Connect the Capabilities using [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization' space='MDTWRT'] (general-specific relationships) or Aggregations (whole-part relationships).

You can also link Capabilities with the Performers and Resources using Capability of Performer relationship. Relationship can be associated with environmental Conditions by filling in the *environmentConditions* property and expressing the civil, military or physical conditions required for the realization of target Capability.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f60e19b-dfe9-417d-8196-bee73d1c3818"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1414337094</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="16baeadf-8566-46dd-b540-af4e734d28ad"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1414337097</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b759e35c-8b2a-4d3d-8610-3a60f9a0ffa8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1414337096</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The CV-2 should be modeled just before the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) CV-1 Vision" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is completed.</p><p><strong>To create a CV-2 diagram</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Create <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Capability" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Capabilities]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MDTWRT" ri:content-title="Generalization" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generalizations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (general-specific relationships) or Aggregations (whole-part relationships).</li></ol><p>You can also link Capabilities with the Performers and Resources using Capability of Performer relationship. Relationship can be associated with environmental Conditions by filling in the <em>environmentConditions</em> property and expressing the civil, military or physical conditions required for the realization of target Capability.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

When you choose to create CV-3 diagram, **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']** opens.

To create a CV-3 diagram from the wizard

1. Right-click the CV-3 Package and select Create Diagram > CV-3 Capability Phasing .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space=''].
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create CV-3** check box.

To create an CV-3 diagram manually when **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']** is disabled

2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter.
4. The blank CV-3 chart is created.

To restore the usage of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439438 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00713: (2026x) Creating CV-4 diagram

- page_id: `254439438`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`

1415279322

1415279324

1415279323

The CV-4 is created just after the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space=''] is completed.

**To create a CV-4 diagram**

1. Create or reuse Capabilities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space=''] (recommended).
2. Connect the Capabilities with Dependencies.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439361 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00714: (2026x) Creating CV-5 table

- page_id: `254439361`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439361/2026x+Creating+CV-5+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:01.133+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Capability viewpoint > (2026x) CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping
- labels: ['create-cv-5']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: How to add a Resources in the CV-5 table?

1415639449

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: How to add a Resources in the CV-5 table?

1415639451

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: How to add a Resources in the CV-5 table?

1415639450

The content of the CV-5 table are Resources used by Actual Organizational Resources (Organizations and Actual Posts) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. Rows of the CV-5 table are Actual Organizational Resources, and columns are Capabilities.

A relationship between an Actual Organizational Resource and a Resource is expressed by the Deployed and No Longer Used Milestones. There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The Deployed Milestone defines the time when the resource is started to use. The No Longer Used Milestone defines the time when the resource is no longer used.

A CV-5 table can be owned by a Capability Phase that will be the context element for this table. The Capability Phase element is related to the CV-1 view.

A relationship between a Capability and a context element is represented by the Capability of Performer.

**To create the CV-5 table**

1. Create an empty CV-5 table for the selected Enterprise Phase. How to create an empty CV-5 table?The owner of the CV-5 table can be only an Enterprise Phase.- Right-click a **CV-5** package as the diagram owner and select **Create Diagram** > **CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping**. An Enterprise Phase is created automatically as the owner of the CV-5 table.- Right-click an Enterprise Phase and select **Create Diagram** > **UPDM CV Diagrams** > **CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping**. An empty table with the column number equal to associated Capabilities in the CV-1 view is created.
2. Add Actual Organizational Resources as table rows. How to add an Actual Organizational Resources in CV-5 table?1. Click the**Add Rows**button.2. In the**Select Actual Organizational Resources** dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.3. Click **OK** when you are done.
3. Add Resources in table cells as table content. How to add a Resources in the CV-5 table?1. Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.2. Click the **+** button that appears at the right of the selected cell.[IMAGE alt='' src='']3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Deployment Milestones wizard' space='']**Deployment Milestones** wizard, select Resources and specify both deployment and no longer used dates for them.4. Click **Finish** when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b656a146-6e29-4bfe-8629-c37f039821e5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415639449</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e7f9541-4099-4516-9fef-dfc12fa8ec5a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415639451</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="26fddf02-da05-44a5-bb77-fa34b562fcde"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415639450</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The content of the CV-5 table are Resources used by Actual Organizational Resources (Organizations and Actual Posts) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. Rows of the CV-5 table are Actual Organizational Resources, and columns are Capabilities.</p><p>A relationship between an Actual Organizational Resource and a Resource is expressed by the Deployed and No Longer Used Milestones. There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The Deployed Milestone defines the time when the resource is started to use. The No Longer Used Milestone defines the time when the resource is no longer used.</p><p>A CV-5 table can be owned by a Capability Phase that will be the context element for this table. The Capability Phase element is related to the CV-1 view.</p><p>A relationship between a Capability and a context element is represented by the Capability of Performer.</p><p><strong>To create the CV-5 table</strong></p><hr /><ol><li><p>Create an empty CV-5 table for the selected Enterprise Phase.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="646d7cb8-f51d-41bd-bf3e-8298743eb9b5"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to create an empty CV-5 table?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6e7da3e5-35f1-4f4e-8f88-8c5ab70f7199"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The owner of the CV-5 table can be only an Enterprise Phase.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>-  Right-click a <strong>CV-5</strong> package as the diagram owner and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping</strong>. An Enterprise Phase is created automatically as the owner of the CV-5 table.</p><p>-  Right-click an Enterprise Phase and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>UPDM CV Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping</strong>. An empty table with the column number equal to associated Capabilities in the CV-1 view is created.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>Add Actual Organizational Resources as table rows.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4a23ecdb-5428-463f-a8b5-1eea226eee57"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to add an Actual Organizational Resources in CV-5 table?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>1. Click the<strong> Add Rows </strong>button.</p><p>2. In the<strong> Select Actual Organizational Resources</strong> dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.</p><p>3. Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>Add Resources in table cells as table content.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ec8885d3-82a8-43d0-b038-2ecc4d47df2e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to add a Resources in the CV-5 table?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>1. Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.</p><p>2. Click the <strong>+</strong> button that appears at the right of the selected cell.</p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Dr_table_add_resources.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating CV-5 table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><p>3. In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Deployment Milestones wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Deployment Milestones</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link>, select Resources and specify both deployment and no longer used dates for them.</p><p>4. Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439478 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00715: (2026x) Creating CV-6 matrix

- page_id: `254439478`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439478/2026x+Creating+CV-6+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:10.212+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Capability viewpoint > (2026x) CV-6 Capability to Operational Activities Mapping
- labels: ['create-cv-6-matrix', 'relate-operational-activity-to-capability', 'delete-relation-from-cv-6-matrix', 'build-cv-6-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1416217377

1416217380

1416217379

A CV-6 Capability to Operational Activity Mapping matrix describes the mapping between Capabilities required by an Enterprise and the Operational Activities that these Capabilities support.

The rows of the matrix are Capabilities and the columns are Operational Activities.

**To create a CV-6 matrix**

1. Specify the Row Scope (Capabilities).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Activity Part of Capability relationship.

**To map an Operational Activity to a Capability**

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

**To remove the relation**

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b9a4e95-9372-4053-bfa2-e1046a32c83e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1416217377</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="77bc8374-32db-4e62-b4dc-6ebaa9c8e309"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1416217380</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b6520e07-0424-4d41-81a6-10dc85407cab"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1416217379</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A CV-6 Capability to Operational Activity Mapping matrix describes the mapping between Capabilities required by an Enterprise and the Operational Activities that these Capabilities support.</p><p>The rows of the matrix are Capabilities and the columns are Operational Activities.</p><p><strong>To create a CV-6 matrix</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p>Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Activity Part of Capability relationship.</p><p> </p><p><strong>To map an Operational Activity to a Capability</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p> </p><p><strong>To remove the relation</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1416458510

1416458512

1416458511

The rows of this matrix are Service Accesses and the columns are Capabilities.

**To create a CV-7 matrix**

1. Specify Row Scope (Service Accesses).
2. Specify Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Service Accesses expose Capabilities using the Capability of Performer relationship.

**To map a Service Access to a Capability**

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

**To remove the relation**

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438483 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00717: (2026x) Creating D2 diagram

- page_id: `254438483`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438483/2026x+Creating+D2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:22.062+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Deployed Resources viewpoint > (2026x) D2 Deployed Resources
- labels: ['create-d2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Organizations Resources are the resources and in general they could be modeled before [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) P1 Resource Types' space=''] view.

To create an D2 diagram

1. Create Organizations and Posts.
2. Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.
3. Create Actual Organizations and Actual Posts.
4. Relate Actual Organizational resources with Actual Organization Relationship.

You can display:

- the Competencies of the Organizational Resources.
- the Operational Resource that is in charge of others by using the Commands relationship.
- the Persons and Actual Persons in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) D2 Deployed Resources' space=''] diagram. Actual Persons can fill in Actual Posts. You can display this relationship using the Fills Post dependency.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Organizations Resources are the resources and in general they could be modeled before <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) P1 Resource Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an D2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Organizations and Posts.</li><li>Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.</li><li>Create Actual Organizations and Actual Posts.</li><li>Relate Actual Organizational resources with Actual Organization Relationship.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can display:</p><ul><li>the Competencies of the Organizational Resources.</li><li>the Operational Resource that is in charge of others by using the Commands relationship.</li><li>the Persons and Actual Persons in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) D2 Deployed Resources" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[D2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram. Actual Persons can fill in Actual Posts. You can display this relationship using the Fills Post dependency.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439960 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00718: (2026x) Creating Desired Effects

- page_id: `254439960`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439960/2026x+Creating+Desired+Effects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:46.507+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures
- labels: ['select-states', 'create-desired-effect-relationship']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Desired Effect relationship connects the Desirer with the Operational / Resource state. If your Desirer has the state machine (Operational State Description or Resource State Machine), all the states are displayed in the Desirer compartments area.. This allows you to draw the Desired Effect relationship from the Desirer directly to the Operational / Resource state.

To view the states, click the Compartments smart manipulator and select States.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- The states are not editable in the element compartment. When you want to edit states, you can do that in the model.
- Double-click the state to open the state diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can choose which states you want to see in the compartment area. 
 
 To select states

1. Right-click the element and select Edit Compartment.
2. Choose State tab in the **Compartment Edit** dialog.
3. Double-click or click the **>** button to select which states you want to see in the element compartment.
4. Click OK when you are done.

If one element has several state machine or region, you will see the names near the states in the element compartment.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a desired effect relationship

1. Select a desirer and on smart manipulator, click [ATTACHMENT filename='desired_effect_button.png'] .
2. Connect it to the desired state.

Additionally, you can create the desired effect relationship in the following ways:

- In the diagram pallet, click the Desired Effects button and connect the desirer to the Operational / Resource state.
- In the Model browser, select the desirer and from its shortcut menu, select New Relation > Outgoing > Desired Effect .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438563 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00719: (2026x) Creating DIV-1 diagram

- page_id: `254438563`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438563/2026x+Creating+DIV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:43.872+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Data and Information viewpoint > (2026x) DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model
- labels: ['create-div-1-conceptual-data-model']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a DIV-1 diagram

1. Create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exchange Element' space=''] .
2. Connect Exchange Elements using [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization' space='MED'] .

You can also use a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model' space=''] to display Entities taxonomy. Entities can be linked with Exchange Elements.

To relate these UPDM Elements, use Details relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a DIV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="716e37c3-ad3c-46ae-8ea8-98198f927483">Create <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Exchange Element" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Exchange Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="ebbb1e66-7d1f-416d-8d5c-d93e329e50f1">Connect Exchange Elements using <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Generalization" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generalizations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol><p>You can also use a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[DIV-1 diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to display Entities taxonomy. Entities can be linked with Exchange Elements.</p><p>To relate these UPDM Elements, use Details relationship.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438512 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00720: (2026x) Creating DIV-2 diagram

- page_id: `254438512`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`

- labels: ['create-div-2-logical-data-model']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

639577900

639577903

639577902

To create a DIV-2 diagram

1. Create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Entity Item' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exchange Element' space=''] .
2. Add [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Entity Attribute' space=''] for Entity Items.
3. Create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Association of Information' space=''] relationships between Entity Items.

Entities can be linked with Exchange Elements. To relate these elements, use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Details' space=''] relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="45d1d971-c308-4b9c-906c-899c46c21395"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">639577900</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2c29d24d-a915-441e-96ed-064d1f36bca3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">639577903</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c734e0d4-21bc-4554-9627-0929d8d33a5b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">639577902</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create a DIV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Entity Item" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Entity Items]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Exchange Element" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Exchange Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Add <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Entity Attribute" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Entity Attributes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for Entity Items.</li><li>Create <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Association of Information" /></ac:link> relationships between Entity Items.</li></ol><p>Entities can be linked with Exchange Elements. To relate these elements, use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Details" /></ac:link> relationship.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438476 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00722: (2026x) Creating Dr table

- page_id: `254438476`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438476/2026x+Creating+Dr+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:21.054+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Deployed Resources viewpoint > (2026x) Dr Deployment Schedule
- labels: ['create-dr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The content of the Dr table are Resources used by Actual Organizational Resources (Actual Organizations and Actual Posts) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period. Rows of the Dr table are Actual Organizational Resources, and columns are Capabilities.

A relationship between an Actual Organizational Resource and a Resource is expressed by the Deployed and No Longer Used Milestones. There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The Deployed Milestone defines the time when the resource is started to use. The No Longer Used Milestone defines the time when the resource is no longer used.

A Dr table can be owned by an Enterprise Phase that will be the context element for this table. The Enterprise Phases are modeled in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) C2 Enterprise Vision' space=''] view.

A relationship between a Capability and a context element is represented by the Exhibits.

To create a Dr table

1. Create an empty Dr table for the selected Enterprise Phase. How to create an empty CV-5 table?The owner of the Dr table can be only an Enterprise Phase.1. Right-click a Dr package as the diagram owner and select **Create Diagram** > **Dr Deployment Schedule**. An Enterprise Phase is created automatically as the owner of the Dr table.
2. Add Actual Organizational Resources as table rows. How to add an Actual Organizational Resources in Dr table?1. Click the**Add Rows**button.2. In the**Select Actual Organizational Resources** dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.3. Click **OK** when you are done.
3. Add Resources in table cells as table content. How to add a Resources in a Dr table?1. Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.2. Click the **+** button that appears at the right of the selected cell.[IMAGE alt='' src='']3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Deployment Milestones wizard' space='']**Deployment Milestones** wizard, select Resources and specify both deployment and no longer used dates for them.4. Click **Finish** when you are done.

The empty table with the column number equal to exhibited Capabilities in the selected time frame is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439133 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00723: (2026x) Creating Implementation Matrix

- page_id: `254439133`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439133/2026x+Creating+Implementation+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:39.971+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures > (2026x) Implementation Matrix
- labels: ['create-implementation-matrix', 'build-implementation-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Rows of the Implementation Matrix represent the implementation elements and the Columns represent the specification elements.

To create an Implementation Matrix

1. Specify the Rows Scope (implementation elements).
2. Specify the Columns Scope (specification elements).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

The implementation elements map to the specification elements by using the Implements relation.

To map the implementation elements to the specification elements

1. Double-click an empty intersection between the desired elements.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

The Implements relationship can be created according to the implementation rules:

- A System Resource can implement a Node/Performer.
- A Function can implement an Operational Activity.
- A Resource Interaction can implement an Operational Exchange.
- A Service Function can implement an Operational Activities
- A Service Function can implement a Function.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Rows of the Implementation Matrix represent the implementation elements and the Columns represent the specification elements.</p><p>To create an Implementation Matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Rows Scope</strong> (implementation elements).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Columns Scope</strong> (specification elements).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p>The implementation elements map to the specification elements by using the Implements relation.</p><p> </p><p>To map the implementation elements to the specification elements</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p>The Implements relationship can be created according to the implementation rules:</p><ul><li>A System Resource can implement a Node/Performer.</li><li>A Function can implement an Operational Activity.</li><li>A Resource Interaction can implement an Operational Exchange.</li><li>A Service Function can implement an Operational Activities</li><li>A Service Function can implement a Function.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439096 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00724: (2026x) Creating L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram

- page_id: `254439096`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439096/2026x+Creating+L1+High-Level+Operational+Concept+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:38.344+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) L1 Node Types
- labels: ['create-l1-high-level-operational-concept-description']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description Diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram. Since it is a bit complex, the L1 Free Form diagram can be used alternatively.

A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UPDM specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.

Concept Role represents logical or physical unit of your architecture. It means that you should have those units in the architecture before creating the diagram.

To create an L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram

1. Create Concept Roles.
2. Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) L1 Node Types' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) P1 Resource Types' space=''] diagrams completed).
3. Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">An L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description Diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram. Since it is a bit complex, the L1 Free Form diagram can be used alternatively.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UPDM specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Concept Role represents logical or physical unit of your architecture. It means that you should have those units in the architecture before creating the diagram.<br /></span></p><p>To create an L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Concept Roles.</li><li>Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) L1 Node Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) P1 Resource Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams completed).</li><li>Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00725: (2026x) Creating L1 Node Types diagram

- page_id: `254439121`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`

- labels: ['create-l1-node-types']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an L1 Node Types diagram

1. Create Logical Architectures or Nodes.
2. Connect Nodes using Node Associations.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439138 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00726: (2026x) Creating L2 diagram

- page_id: `254439138`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439138/2026x+Creating+L2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:40.271+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) L2 Logical Scenario
- labels: ['create-l2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an L2 diagram

1. Model internal structures of created context elements using Node Roles typed by Nodes.
2. Associate Node Roles using Needlines.
3. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Needlines.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an L2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="74e40a9f-bcea-41a4-8f08-0d730ceaa168">Model internal structures of created context elements using Node Roles typed by Nodes.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Associate Node Roles using Needlines.</li><li>Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Needlines.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439194 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00727: (2026x) Creating L3 Node Interactions table

- page_id: `254439194`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439194/2026x+Creating+L3+Node+Interactions+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:44.895+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) L3 Node Interactions
- labels: ['create-l3-node-interactions-table', 'add-operational-exchange-in-l3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating L3 Node Interactions table' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating L3 Node Interactions table' space=''] Operational Exchange to fill in a L3 table.

newTo add new Operational Exchange

1. In the L3 diagram toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

existingTo add an existing Operational Exchange

1. In the L3 diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Operational Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select Operational Exchanges by clicking + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

- Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Performer, Receiving Performer, and Implements cells are read only.
- You may use an L1 view to create or modify an Operational Exchange.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can add <ac:link ac:anchor="new"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating L3 Node Interactions table" /></ac:link> or <ac:link ac:anchor="existing"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating L3 Node Interactions table" /></ac:link> Operational Exchange to fill in a L3 table.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="296727d2-894a-4d95-b61a-d578e1a992f0" ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">new</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add new Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the L3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="f07942c7-23e4-4010-b873-bb1d2373f2fc" ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">existing</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add an existing Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the L3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Operational Exchange</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select Operational Exchanges by clicking <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li>Sending Performer, Receiving Performer, and Implements cells are read only.</li><li>You may use an <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span class="confluence-link">L1</span> view</span></span> to create or modify an Operational Exchange.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span> that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><br /></span></p><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````
