<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439228 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00728: (2026x) Creating L4 Logical Activities diagram

- page_id: `254439228`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439228/2026x+Creating+L4+Logical+Activities+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:48.611+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) L4 Logical Activities
- labels: ['create-l4-logical-activities']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the Logical Activities diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram' space='']Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram should be created first.

To create an L4 Logical Activities diagram for Operational Activity flows

1. Create Operational Activity Actions or drop them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect Operational Activity Actions with Operational Activity Edges.
3. Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Activity Edges.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating the Logical Activities diagram, the <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Logical Activities Taxonomy</span> diagram</ac:link-body></ac:link> should be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an L4 Logical Activities diagram for Operational Activity flows</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activity Actions or drop them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect Operational Activity Actions with Operational Activity Edges.</li><li>Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Activity Edges.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439294 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00729: (2026x) Creating L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `254439294`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439294/2026x+Creating+L4+Logical+Activities+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:54.608+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) L4 Logical Activities
- labels: ['create-l4-logical-activities-taxonomy']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies

1. Create Operational Activities.
2. Create or reuse Nodes from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) L1 Node Types' space=''] view (recommended) .
3. Draw Is Capable of Performing relationship between Nodes and Operational Activities.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activities.</li><li>Create or reuse Nodes from <span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) L1 Node Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">view </span>(recommended) .</li><li>Draw Is Capable of Performing relationship between Nodes and Operational Activities.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438415 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00730: (2026x) Creating L4-P4 Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix

- page_id: `254438415`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438415/2026x+Creating+L4-P4+Systems+Function+to+Operational+Activity+Traceability+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:11.914+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P4 Resource Functions
- labels: ['create-l4-p4-systems-function-to-operational-activity-traceability-matrix', 'build-l4-p4-systems-function-to-operational-activity-traceability-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a L4-P4 Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Functions).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Functions implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.

To map a Function to an Operational Activity

1. Click the intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a L4-P4 Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Functions).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p>Functions implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.</p><p> </p><p>To map a Function to an Operational Activity</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click the intersection between the desired elements.</p></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439306 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00731: (2026x) Creating L7 diagram

- page_id: `254439306`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439306/2026x+Creating+L7+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:56.125+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) L7 Logical Data Model
- labels: ['create-l7']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an L7 diagram

1. Create Exchange Elements or Entity Items.
2. Add Entity Attributes.
3. Create Entity Relationships.

Entities can be linked with Exchange Elements. To relate these elements, use Details relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an L7 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Exchange Elements or Entity Items.</li><li>Add Entity Attributes.</li><li>Create Entity Relationships.</li></ol><p>Entities can be linked with Exchange Elements. To relate these <span>elements</span>, use Details relationship.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439265 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00732: (2026x) Creating L8 table

- page_id: `254439265`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439265/2026x+Creating+L8+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:52.541+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) L8 Logical Constraints
- labels: ['create-l8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an L8 table, do either

- Add a new Operational Constraint by clicking the Add New button. The new row in the table is created. Select constrained Operational Element (Node, Operational Activity, Entity Item, Exchange Element, Operational Exchange, and Mission). Edit the Rule Specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).
- Add existing Operational Constraints by clicking the Add Existing button and select Operational Constraints from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an L8 table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Operational Constraint by clicking the <strong>Add New </strong>button. <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6ce018c8-0c16-450f-b89b-b2c7c9909dd7">The new row in the table is created.</ac:inline-comment-marker><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6ce018c8-0c16-450f-b89b-b2c7c9909dd7"> Select constrained Operational Element (Node, Operational Activity, Entity Item, Exchange Element, Operational Exchange, and Mission).</ac:inline-comment-marker> </span>Edit the Rule Specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).</li><li>Add existing Operational Constraints by clicking the <strong>Add Existing </strong>button and select Operational Constraints from the model.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439250 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00733: (2026x) Creating Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart)

- page_id: `254439250`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439250/2026x+Creating+Lr+Lines+of+Development+Lr+Gantt+chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:50.624+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) Lr Lines of Development
- labels: ['create-lr-lines-of-development', 'create-lr-gantt-chart']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you choose to create Lr Gantt chart, the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**opens.

To create an Lr Gantt chart from the wizard

1. Right-click the Lr Package and select Create Diagram > Lr Lines of Development .
2. Follow the steps of the `****` [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space=''] .
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the******[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create Lr Lines of Development** check box.

To create an Lr diagram manually when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**Lr Lines of Development Creation Wizard** is disabled

1. Right-click the Lr Package and select Create Diagram > Lr Lines of Development .
2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter . The blank Lr Lines of Development chart is created.

To restore the usage of the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UPDM options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>When you choose to create Lr Gantt chart, the<strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lr Lines of Development Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>opens.</p><p>To create an Lr Gantt chart from the wizard</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Lr Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Lr Lines of Development</strong>.</li><li>Follow the steps of the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><code><strong> </strong></code></span><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lr Lines of Development Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="eba5ac24-51bf-4a20-88c8-afd68a3afc79" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not wish to use the<strong> </strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><strong><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lr Lines of Development Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></strong></span> next time, clear the <strong>Show the wizard next time, when I create Lr Lines of Development</strong> check box.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>To create an Lr diagram manually when <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Lr Lines of Development Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> is disabled</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Lr Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Lr Lines of Development</strong>.</li><li><p>Type the diagram name.</p></li><li>Press <strong>Enter</strong>. <br />The blank Lr Lines of Development chart is created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="a5da39ff-0302-4822-b740-1df1ccaacb49" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To restore the usage of the<strong> <span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lr Lines of Development Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span></span></strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog select the <strong>UPDM</strong> options group.</li><li>In the General properties, set the <strong>Show PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1 Creation Wizard</strong> <strong>each Time Creating New Chart</strong> property value to<em> true</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439338 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00734: (2026x) Creating Lr Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram

- page_id: `254439338`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439338/2026x+Creating+Lr+Programme+Portfolio+Relationships+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:58.965+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) Lr Lines of Development
- labels: ['create-lr-programme-portfolio-relationships']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to create the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) D2 Deployed Resources' space='']before creating the Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram.

To create an Lr Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram

1. Create Actual Projects.
2. Associate Actual Projects by dragging one project (part) to another (whole) . Alternatively you can specify the whole and part properties.
3. Associate Actual Projects with Actual Organizations([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) D2 Deployed Resources' space='']) or Actual Posts([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) D2 Deployed Resources' space='']) responsible for them by using the Organizational Project Relationship.

You can:

- Nest Actual Projects, Actual Organizations, and Actual Posts to one another in the diagram to make it look more attractive and easier to read.
- Use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Lr Responsibility Matrix' space=''] to define the association between Actual Projects and Actual Organization resources.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is recommended to create the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) D2 Deployed Resources" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[D2 diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </span>before creating <span>the Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an Lr Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Actual Projects.</li><li>Associate Actual Projects by dragging one project <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">(part)</span> to another<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> (whole)</span>. Alternatively you can specify the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> whole and part </span>properties. <span> </span></li><li><p>Associate Actual Projects with Actual Organizations<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) D2 Deployed Resources" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[D2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) o</span>r Actual Posts<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) D2 Deployed Resources" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[D2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) r</span>esponsible for them by using the Organizational Project Relationship.</p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can:</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b87a6e49-5998-4084-9df0-12dd640e4b24">Nest Actual Projects, Actual Organizations, and Actual Posts to one another in the diagram to make it look more attractive and easier to read.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating Lr Responsibility Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lr Responsibility Matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to define the association between Actual Projects and Actual Organization resources.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439288 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00735: (2026x) Creating Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix

- page_id: `254439288`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439288/2026x+Creating+Lr+Programme+to+Capability+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:53.931+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) Lr Lines of Development
- labels: ['create-lr-programme-to-capability-mapping']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Lr Programme to Capability Mapping describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a Capability.

The rows of this matrix are Capabilities and the columns are Projects.

To create an Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix

1. Specify Row Scope (Capabilities).
2. Specify Column Scope (Projects).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Activity is part of Project (“Activity Part of Project” relationship and a part of Capability (“Activity Part of Capability”) relationship). This transitive structure of relations maps Capabilities to Projects.

Project owns Increment Milestones that are related to Resources exhibiting capabilities.

Both transitive structures of relations map Capabilities to Projects. The mapping is displayed in the Matrix.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Lr Programme to Capability Mapping describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a Capability.</p><p>The rows of this matrix are Capabilities and the columns are Projects.</p><p>To create an Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Specify <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Projects).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p>Activity is part of Project (“Activity Part of Project” relationship and a part of Capability (“Activity Part of Capability”) relationship). This transitive structure of relations maps Capabilities to Projects.</p><p>Project owns Increment Milestones that are related to Resources exhibiting capabilities.</p><p>Both transitive structures of relations map Capabilities to Projects. The mapping is displayed in the Matrix.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439276 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00736: (2026x) Creating Lr Responsibility Matrix

- page_id: `254439276`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439276/2026x+Creating+Lr+Responsibility+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:53.316+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) Lr Lines of Development
- labels: ['create-lr-responsibility-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to create the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) D2 Deployed Resources' space='']D2 diagrambefore creating the Lr Responsibility Matrix.

The Lr Responsibility Matrix is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Organizational Resources (Actual Organizations or Actual Posts) for Actual Projects.

The rows in of this matrix represent Actual Projects and the columns represent Actual Organizational Resources (Actual Organization or Actual Post).

To create the Lr Responsibilitymatrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Actual Projects).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Actual Organizations and Actual Posts).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="b20a7230-c44c-4ae1-8fd6-2079374a1070" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is recommended to create the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) D2 Deployed Resources" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">D2</span> diagram</ac:link-body></ac:link> </span>before creating the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Lr Responsibility Matrix</span>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The Lr Responsibility Matrix is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Organizational Resources (Actual Organizations or Actual Posts) for Actual Projects.</p><p>The rows in of this matrix represent Actual Projects and the columns represent Actual Organizational Resources (Actual Organization or Actual Post).</p><p style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">To create the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Lr Responsibility </span>matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Actual Projects).</li><li>Specify the<strong> Column Scope</strong> (Actual Organizations and Actual Posts).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439149 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00737: (2026x) Creating Operational Exchanges in L1 Node Types diagram

- page_id: `254439149`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439149/2026x+Creating+Operational+Exchanges+in+L1+Node+Types+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:40.634+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) L1 Node Types
- labels: ['create-operational-exchanges-in-l1-node-types']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Exchange in the L1 Node Types diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Node Association and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_operational_exchange.png'] .
  - In the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='operational_exchange.png'] and select the Node Association on the diagram pane.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

An L1 Node Types diagram also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Nodes, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Nodes.

The structure of each Node can be modeled using the Logical Scenario diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

Once the L1 diagram has been completed, you can proceed creating the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) L4 Logical Activities' space=''] diagram.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized </strong><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Operational Exchange</strong></span> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link></span> by doing one of the following:<span class="confluence-link"> </span><br /><ul><li>Select the Node Association and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:alt="New Operational Exchange" ac:title="New Operational Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>In the diagram pallet, click <ac:image ac:alt="Operational Exchange" ac:title="Operational Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the Node Association on the diagram pane.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p>An L1 Node Types diagram also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Nodes, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Nodes.</p><p>The structure of each Node can be modeled using the Logical Scenario diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p><p>Once the L1 diagram has been completed, you can proceed creating the <ac:link><ri:page ac:macro-id="ee1ff623-8826-4c2a-9317-b7f80ae9f995" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439206 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00738: (2026x) Creating Operational Exchanges in L2 Logical Scenario diagram

- page_id: `254439206`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439206/2026x+Creating+Operational+Exchanges+in+L2+Logical+Scenario+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:45.450+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) L2 Logical Scenario
- labels: ['create-operational-exchanges-in-l2-logical-scenario']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Exchange in the L2 Logical Scenario diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Needline and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_operational_exchange.png'] .
  - In the diagram pallet click [ATTACHMENT filename='operational_exchange.png'] and select the Needline.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an Operational Exchange in the L2 Logical Scenario diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized </strong><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Operational Exchange</strong></span> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select the Needline and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:alt="New Operational Exchange " ac:title="New Operational Exchange "><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>In the diagram pallet click <ac:image ac:alt="Operational Exchange" ac:title="Operational Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the Needline.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="7268b862-1271-4f2f-8573-5f9d91a9ef41" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439292 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00739: (2026x) Creating Operational Exchanges in L4 Logical Activities diagram

- page_id: `254439292`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439292/2026x+Creating+Operational+Exchanges+in+L4+Logical+Activities+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:54.311+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) L4 Logical Activities
- labels: ['create-operational-exchanges-in-l4-logical-activities']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Exchange in the L4 Logical Activities diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange**wizard . To open the wizard, do either:
  - Select a Operational Activity Edge and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_operational_exchange.png'] .
  - In the diagram pallet click [ATTACHMENT filename='operational_exchange.png'] and select the Operational Activity Edge.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an Operational Exchange in the L4 Logical Activities diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Operational Exchange </strong>wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><span class="confluence-link"> </span>. To open the wizard, do either:<br /><ul><li>Select a Operational Activity Edge and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:alt="New Operational Exchange" ac:title="New Operational Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>In the diagram pallet click <ac:image ac:alt="Operational Exchange" ac:title="Operational Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the Operational Activity Edge.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="d55a547b-8d05-4c0e-97a0-bb3297855056" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439315 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00740: (2026x) Creating Operational Exchanges in L6 Logical Sequence diagram

- page_id: `254439315`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439315/2026x+Creating+Operational+Exchanges+in+L6+Logical+Sequence+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:56.880+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) L6 Logical Sequence
- labels: ['create-operational-exchanges-in-l6-logical-sequence']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Exchange in the L6 Logical Sequence diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select a Operational Message and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_operational_exchange.png'] .
  - In the diagram pallet click [ATTACHMENT filename='operational_exchange.png'] and select the Operational Message.
  - On the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='operational_exchange.png'] and connect the Lifeline elements on the diagram pane.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

## PAGE 00741: (2026x) Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-2 diagram

- page_id: `254439513`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439513/2026x+Creating+Operational+Exchanges+in+OV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:13.800+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Operational viewpoint > (2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description
- labels: ['create-operational-exchanges-in-ov-2', 'create-operational-exchange-in-operational-resource-flow-description']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

898696775

898696777

898696776

To create an Operational Exchange in the OV-2 diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Associationbetween a pair of Nodes and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and on the diagram pane, connect Nodes.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

An OV-2 view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Nodes, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Nodes.

The structure of each Node can be modeled using the Operational Node Internal Relationship Description diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

Once the OV-2 diagram has been completed, you can proceed creating the OV-5 diagram.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Realized </strong><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Operational Exchange</strong></span> wizard</span></ac:link-body></ac:link><span class="confluence-link"> </span> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select the Operational Message between a pair of two Lifelines of <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Node Roles</span> and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="New Operational Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM3P" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>.</li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Operational Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM3P" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> and on the diagram pane, select the Operational Message.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b760de6-bb46-46a2-b8ff-dcd77e993da1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439548 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00745: (2026x) Creating OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic diagram

- page_id: `254439548`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439548/2026x+Creating+OV-1+High-Level+Operational+Concept+Graphic+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:18.122+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Operational viewpoint > (2026x) OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic
- labels: ['create-ov-1-high-level-operational-concept-graphic-diagram']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic Diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram. Since it is a bit complex, the OV-1 Free Form diagram can be used alternatively.

A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UPDM specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.

To create an OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic diagram

1. Create Concept Roles.
2. Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] diagrams completed).
3. Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>An OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic Diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram. Since it is a bit complex, the OV-1 Free Form diagram can be used alternatively.</p><p>A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UPDM specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.</p><p>To create an OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Concept Roles.</li><li>Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams completed).</li><li>Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439520 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00746: (2026x) Creating OV-2 diagram

- page_id: `254439520`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439520/2026x+Creating+OV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:14.372+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Operational viewpoint > (2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description
- labels: ['create-ov-2-operational-resource-flow-description']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

OV-2 diagram name in DoDAF differs from the one in MODAF, though the diagram concept in both architecture frameworks is the same.

OV-2 is one of the starting views of the architecture for DoDAF and requires the Strategic Viewpoint to be developed for MODAF.

To create an OV-2 diagram

1. Create Logical Architectures (MODAF) or Performers (DoDAF).
2. Model internal structures of created context elements using Node Roles typed by Nodes (MODAF) or Performers (DoDAF).
3. Associate Node Roles using Needlines.
4. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Needlines.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>OV-2 diagram name in DoDAF differs from the one in MODAF, though the diagram concept in both architecture frameworks is the same.</p><p>OV-2 is one of the starting views of the architecture for DoDAF and requires the Strategic Viewpoint to be developed for MODAF.</p><p>To create an OV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Logical Architectures (MODAF) or Performers (DoDAF).</li><li>Model internal structures of created context elements using Node Roles typed by Nodes (MODAF) or Performers (DoDAF).</li><li>Associate Node Roles using Needlines.</li><li>Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Needlines.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

**To create an OV-2 Internal diagram**

1. Create Logical Architectures (MODAF) or Performers (DoDAF).
2. Model internal structures of created context elements using Node Roles typed by Nodes (MODAF) or Performers (DoDAF).
3. Associate Node Roles using Needlines.
4. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Needlines.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438589 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00748: (2026x) Creating OV-3 table

- page_id: `254438589`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438589/2026x+Creating+OV-3+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:52.283+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Operational viewpoint > (2026x) OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix
- labels: ['add-operational-exchange-in-ov-3', 'create-ov-3-table', 'create-operational-exchange-in-ov-3-operational-resource-flow-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

899419172

899419174

899419173

You can add [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating OV-3 table' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating OV-3 table' space=''] Operational Exchange to fill in a OV-3 table.

newTo add new Operational Exchange

1. In the OV-3 diagram toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

existingTo add an existing Operational Exchange

1. In the OV-3 diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Operational Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select Operational Exchanges by clicking + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

- Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Performer, Receiving Performer, and Implements cells are read only.
- You may use an OV-2 view to create or modify an Operational Exchange.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="93a021eb-86b4-4b7a-953b-b87956b21dc0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">899419172</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0c1a83b2-b392-4279-aa10-989b430ea85c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">899419174</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9d9e282c-25d4-40e3-90da-63e6df8c1247"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">899419173</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can add <ac:link ac:anchor="new"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating OV-3 table" /></ac:link> or <ac:link ac:anchor="existing"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating OV-3 table" /></ac:link> Operational Exchange to fill in a OV-3 table.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="296727d2-894a-4d95-b61a-d578e1a992f0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">new</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add new Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the OV-3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f07942c7-23e4-4010-b873-bb1d2373f2fc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">existing</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add an existing Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the OV-3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Operational Exchange</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select Operational Exchanges by clicking <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li>Sending Performer, Receiving Performer, and Implements cells are read only.</li><li>You may use an <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span class="confluence-link">OV-2</span> view</span></span> to create or modify an Operational Exchange.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span> that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438577 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00749: (2026x) Creating OV-4 diagram

- page_id: `254438577`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438577/2026x+Creating+OV-4+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:48.690+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Operational viewpoint > (2026x) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart
- labels: ['create-ov-4-organizational-relationship-chart']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

899995524

899995527

899995526

**To create an OV-4 diagram**

1. Create Organization Types and Person Types.
2. Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.
3. Create Actual Organizations and Individual Person Roles.
4. Relate Actual Organizational resources with Actual Organization Relationship.

You can display:

- the Skills of the Organizational Resources.
- the Operational Resource that is in charge of others using the Commands relationship.
- the Resources and in general they could be modelled just before [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] view.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c8e8470f-b8c7-4392-9f44-57e679f81dcd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">899995524</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="666c4a6d-7fc5-4acc-bf05-c22931faf0b8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">899995527</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ef0efaee-3785-43b8-9839-bf2cc4bf5c00"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">899995526</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>To create an OV-4 diagram</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Create Organization Types and Person Types.</li><li>Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.</li><li>Create Actual Organizations and Individual Person Roles.</li><li>Relate Actual Organizational resources with Actual Organization Relationship.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="439ad953-f2b7-48c9-9049-4b629719adf6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can display:</p><ul><li>the Skills of the Organizational Resources.</li><li>the Operational Resource that is in charge of others using the Commands relationship.</li><li>the Resources and in general they could be modelled just before <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 00750: (2026x) Creating OV-5a Operational Activity Model diagram

- space_key: `UPDM2P`

- version_number: 1

- labels: ['create-ov-5a-operational-activity-model']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

291207411

291207414

291207413

The Operational Activity Model represents Operational Activity hierarchies. It must be created first.

To create an OV-5a diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies

1. Create Operational Activities.
2. Create or Reuse from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] (recommended) Performers.
3. Draw Is Capable of Performing relationship between them and Operational Activities.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d89d405-1dd5-485d-a1c1-b0f37df461be"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">291207411</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="939f5892-f6e0-44be-8c54-44666be7bbfa"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">291207414</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6c461292-688f-4b33-9925-588a90f28ef4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">291207413</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Operational Activity Model represents Operational Activity hierarchies. It must be created first.</p><p>To create an OV-5a diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activities.</li><li>Create or Reuse from <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (recommended) Performers.</li><li>Draw Is Capable of Performing relationship between them and Operational Activities.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438593 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00751: (2026x) Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram

- page_id: `254438593`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438593/2026x+Creating+OV-5b+Operational+Activity+Flow+Model+diagram
- version_number: 1

- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Operational viewpoint > (2026x) OV-5 Operational Activity Model
- labels: ['create-ov-5b-operational-activity-flow-model']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

291280549

291280551

291280550

An Operational Activity Flow Model represents Operational Activity flows. Before creating this diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating OV-5a Operational Activity Model diagram' space=''] must be created first.

To create an OV-5b diagram for Operational Activity flows

1. Create Operational Activity Actions or just drop Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the Diagram.
2. Connect Operational Activity Actions with Operational Activity Edges.
3. Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Activity Edges.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="923fc1cb-fbb0-4166-8ddc-8332e946f2e8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">291280549</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="754de095-e969-4a8b-9f35-396b41b9d2a9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">291280551</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74265ee5-9265-48d7-802a-18183dbf1cd0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">291280550</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>An Operational Activity Flow Model represents Operational Activity flows. Before creating this diagram, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating OV-5a Operational Activity Model diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Activity Model diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> must be created first.</p><p>To create an OV-5b diagram for Operational Activity flows</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activity Actions or just drop Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the Diagram.</li><li>Connect Operational Activity Actions with Operational Activity Edges.</li><li>Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Activity Edges.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

382808291

382808290

**To create an OV-6a table, do one of the following**

- Add a new Operational Constraint by clicking the Add New button and select constrained Operational Element (Performer, Operational Activity, Entity Item, Exchange Element, Operational Exchange, and Mission). Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).
- Add Existing Operational Constraints by clicking the Add Existing button and select Operational Constraints from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="75d28ea3-197f-4100-b5b7-5418d0174667"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">382808289</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c515fa85-0eaa-4af8-a2fe-78a6e9791db0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">382808291</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e06c3ca4-dbc1-4ae4-89a7-e511b8f42056"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">382808290</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>To create an OV-6a table, do one of the following</strong></p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Operational Constraint by clicking the <strong>Add New </strong> button and select constrained Operational Element (Performer, Operational Activity, Entity Item, Exchange Element, Operational Exchange, and Mission). Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).</li><li>Add Existing Operational Constraints by clicking the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select Operational Constraints from the model. </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439388 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00753: (2026x) Creating P1 Resource Types Actual Quality Requirements table

- page_id: `254439388`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439388/2026x+Creating+P1+Resource+Types+Actual+Quality+Requirements+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:02.676+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-p1-resource-types-actual-quality-requirements-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating P1 Resource Types Actual Quality Requirements table, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements table' space='']P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements table should be created firstly.

To create P1 Actual table, do either

- Add a new measurable Resource by clicking the Add New button and select one or more Resources that have at least one Measurement Set, defined (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements table' space='']P1 Typical ). Specify the values of each Actual Measurement in the table cells.
- Add the existing Actual Property Sets by clicking the **Add Existing** button and select the existing Actual Property Sets.
- Add the missing Actual Measurements by clicking the**Add Missing Measurements** button to update model changes to the table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Specify the values of each Actual Measurement in the table cells.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Add the existing Actual Property Sets by clicking the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select the existing Actual Property Sets.</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Add the missing Actual Measurements by clicking the<strong> Add Missing Measurements</strong> button to update model changes to the table.</span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439379 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00754: (2026x) Creating P1 Resource Types diagram

- page_id: `254439379`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439379/2026x+Creating+P1+Resource+Types+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:02.442+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-p1-resource-types']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P1 Resource Types diagram

1. Create Resources.
2. Connect Resources using Resource Associations.
3. Create Resource Interactions flowing via Resource Associations.

The P1 Resource Types diagram also allows you to show the Capabilities and Resources mapping the provided and required services and Competencies provided by the Resources. You can model each Resource internal structure using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) P2 Resource Structure' space=''] diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) P4 Resource Functions' space=''] view as soon as the P1 has been completed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a P1 Resource Types diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Resources.</li><li>Connect Resources using Resource Associations.</li><li>Create Resource Interactions flowing via Resource Associations.</li></ol><p>The P1 Resource Types diagram also allows you to show the Capabilities and Resources mapping the provided and required services and Competencies provided by the Resources. You can model each Resource internal structure using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) P2 Resource Structure" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can proceed to create <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) P4 Resource Functions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view as soon as the P1 has been completed.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><br /></span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

- labels: ['create-p1-resource-types-typical-quality-requirements-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create P1 Resource Types Typical Quality Requirements table, do either

- Add a new Measurement Set by clicking the Add New button. The new row in the table is created. S elect an owning element for the Measurement Set, and then specify the Measurements and Resources to be measured in the table cells.
- Add the existing Measurement Sets by clicking the Add Existing button and select existing Measurement Sets from the model.

The new row in the table is created.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6ce018c8-0c16-450f-b89b-b2c7c9909dd7"> S</ac:inline-comment-marker></span>elect an owning element for the Measurement Set, and then specify the Measurements and Resources to be measured in the table cells.</li><li>Add the existing Measurement Sets by clicking the <strong>Add Existing </strong>button and select existing Measurement Sets from the model.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
