<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438448 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00756: (2026x) Creating P1 Service Provision matrix

- page_id: `254438448`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438448/2026x+Creating+P1+Service+Provision+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:17.067+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-p1-service-provision-matrix', 'build-p1-service-provision-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The rows in a P1 Service Provision matrix represent Service Interfaces and the columns represent Resources.

To create a P1 Service Provision matrix

1. Specify the Rows scope (Service Interfaces).
2. Specify the Columns scope (Resources).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

The cells in the matrix represent a Service or Request Port. If a resource provides a Service Interface, the Service Port icon will be displayed. If it requests a Service Interface, the Request Port icon is displayed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The rows in a P1 Service Provision matrix represent Service Interfaces and the columns represent Resources.</p><p>To create a P1 Service Provision matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Rows scope</strong> (Service Interfaces).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Columns scope</strong> (Resources).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p>The cells in the matrix represent a Service or Request Port. If a resource provides a Service Interface, the Service Port icon will be displayed. If it requests a Service Interface, the Request Port icon is displayed.</p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438446 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00757: (2026x) Creating P1 Systems to Systems Matrix

- page_id: `254438446`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438446/2026x+Creating+P1+Systems+to+Systems+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:16.834+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-p1-systems-to-systems-matrix', 'build-p1-systems-to-systems-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A P1 Systems to Systems Matrix is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.

Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. The P1 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.

To create a P1 Systems to Systems matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (System Resources).
2. Specify the Column Scope (System Resources).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>A P1 Systems to Systems Matrix is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.</p><p>Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. The P1 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.</p><p>To create a P1 Systems to Systems matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438450 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00758: (2026x) Creating P1 Technology Forecast table

- page_id: `254438450`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438450/2026x+Creating+P1+Technology+Forecast+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:17.323+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-p1-technology-forecast-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P1 Technology Forecast table

1. Add Rows to the table by doing any of the following:
  - Adding a new Resource as the row Header: click the Add New button, select a Resource you want to create, and specify the owner of the selected Resource.
  - Adding an Existing Resource as the row Header: click the Add Existing button and select one or more existing Resources.
2. Add columns to the table by clicking the Time Periods button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Time Periods dialog' space='']**Time Periods** dialog opens. Specify the forecast time periods:
  - Select or create a Timeline package to store the Forecast dates.
  - Select a Forecast Kind.
  - Specify additional options needed according to the selected Forecast Kind.
3. Click the **…** button on the cell to select the Resources, Standards, Protocols or Competencies to forecast, and then specify the date for the Forecast. By default the date will be the starting date of the selected time period.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a P1 Technology Forecast table</p><hr /><ol><li>Add Rows to the table by doing any of the following:<br /><ul><li>Adding a new Resource as the row Header: click the <strong>Add New</strong> button, select a Resource you want to create, and specify the owner of the selected Resource.</li><li>Adding an Existing Resource as the row Header: click the <strong>Add Existing </strong>button and select one or more existing Resources.</li></ul></li><li>Add columns to the table by clicking the Time Periods button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Time Periods dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Time Periods</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens. Specify the forecast time periods:<br /><ul><li>Select or create a Timeline package to store the Forecast dates.</li><li>Select a Forecast Kind.</li><li>Specify additional options needed according to the selected Forecast Kind.</li></ul></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Click the <strong>…</strong> button on the cell to select the Resources, Standards, Protocols or Competencies to forecast, and then specify the date for the Forecast.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="49fbc8a7-1dd1-4213-8e35-c236a258222a" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">By default the date will be the starting date of the selected time period.</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> <br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><br /></span></p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438428 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00759: (2026x) Creating P2 diagram

- page_id: `254438428`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438428/2026x+Creating+P2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:15.260+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P2 Resource Structure
- labels: ['create-p2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P2 diagram

1. Create Resources Roles or drag Resources from the Containment tree directly to the diagram.
2. Specify the types of the Resource Roles.
3. Associate Resource Roles using Resource Connectors or Resource Interfaces.
4. Create Resource Ports.
5. Specify the types for the Resource Ports.

A P2 diagram allows to show the provided and required services by the particular Resource Roles.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a P2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Resources Roles or drag Resources <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">from the Containment tree directly to the diagram.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,51,102);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Specify the types of the Resource Roles.</span><br /></span></li><li>Associate Resource Roles using Resource Connectors or Resource Interfaces.</li><li>Create Resource Ports.</li><li>Specify the types for the Resource Ports.</li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">A P2 diagram allows to show the provided and required services by the particular Resource Roles.</span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438436 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00760: (2026x) Creating P3 table

- page_id: `254438436`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438436/2026x+Creating+P3+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:16.179+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P3 Resource Connectivity
- labels: ['create-p3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating P3 table' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating P3 table' space=''] Resource Interactions to fill in a P3 table.

newTo add new Resource Interaction

1. In the P3 diagram toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Resource Interactions are added to the table.

existingTo add an existing Resource Interaction

1. In the P3 diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Resource Interaction dialog opens.
2. Select Resource Interactions by clicking + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Interactions are added to the table.

- Resource Interaction Identifier, Resource Interaction Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.
- You may use an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) P1 Resource Types' space=''] view to create or modify a Resource Interaction.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Interaction Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can add <ac:link ac:anchor="new"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating P3 table" /></ac:link> or <ac:link ac:anchor="existing"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating P3 table" /></ac:link> Resource Interactions to fill in a P3 table.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="296727d2-894a-4d95-b61a-d578e1a992f0" ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">new</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add new Resource Interaction</p><hr /><ol><li>In the P3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Resource Interaction wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Interaction</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Resource Interactions are added to the table.</li></ol><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="f07942c7-23e4-4010-b873-bb1d2373f2fc" ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">existing</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add an existing Resource Interaction</p><hr /><ol><li>In the P3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Resource Interaction</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select Resource Interactions by clicking <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Resource Interactions are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Interaction Identifier, Resource Interaction Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li>Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.</li><li>You may use an <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) P1 Resource Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view</span></span> to create or modify a Resource Interaction.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Resource Interaction Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Interaction Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><br /></span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438430 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00761: (2026x) Creating P4 Resource Functions diagram

- page_id: `254438430`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438430/2026x+Creating+P4+Resource+Functions+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:15.573+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P4 Resource Functions
- labels: ['create-p4-resource-functions']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the P4 Resource Functions diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram' space=''] should be created first.

To create a P4 Resource Functions diagram

1. Either create Function Actions or drag the Functions from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect the Function Actions using the Function Edges.
3. Display the possible Resource Interactions on every Function Edge.

To display possible Resource Interactions, do either

- Select Function Edge and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].****
- In the diagram pallet, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the Function Edge.The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard opens.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="b6105221-2fb5-4b1d-9268-05a0995eb683" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating the P4 Resource Functions diagram, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> should be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a P4 Resource Functions diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Either create Function Actions or drag the Functions from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect the Function Actions using the Function Edges.</li><li>Display the possible Resource Interactions on every Function Edge.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="71af54d1-3d83-4531-b4de-b16ed860afc7" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">To display possible Resource Interactions, do either<br /></span></p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> Select Function Edge and on the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">smart manipulator</span> toolbar, click <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:image ac:alt="New Resource Interaction" ac:title="New Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<strong> </strong></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">In the diagram pallet, click <ac:image ac:alt="Resource Interaction" ac:title="Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the Function Edge.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><br />The </span></span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Resource Interaction wizard" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Realized Resource Interaction</strong> wizard</span> </ac:link-body></ac:link><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> opens.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438417 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00762: (2026x) Creating P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `254438417`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438417/2026x+Creating+P4+Resource+Functions+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:12.177+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P4 Resource Functions
- labels: ['create-p4-resource-functions-taxonomy']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram

1. Create Functions.
2. Create or reuse (recommended) Resources from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) P1 Resource Types' space=''] view.
3. Use Is Capable of Performing relationship to connect the Resources and Functions.
4. Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.
5. Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) L4 Logical Activities' space=''] view.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Functions.</li><li>Create or reuse (recommended) Resources from<span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) P1 Resource Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</span></li><li>Use Is Capable of Performing relationship to connect the Resources and Functions.</li><li>Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.</li><li>Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) L4 Logical Activities" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</span></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438488 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00763: (2026x) Creating P7 diagram

- page_id: `254438488`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438488/2026x+Creating+P7+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:22.933+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P7 Physical Data Model
- labels: ['create-p7']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P7 diagram

1. Create Entity Items or Exchange Elements.
2. Add Entity Attributes for Entity Items.
3. Draw Association of Information relationships between Entity Items or Exchange Elements.

Entities can be linked with Exchange Elements. To relate these elements, use Details relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a P7 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Entity Items or Exchange Elements.</li><li>Add Entity Attributes for Entity Items.</li><li>Draw Association of Information relationships between Entity Items or Exchange Elements.</li></ol><p>Entities can be linked with Exchange Elements. To relate these elements, use Details relationship.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438500 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00764: (2026x) Creating P8 table

- page_id: `254438500`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438500/2026x+Creating+P8+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:27.759+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P8 Resource Constraints
- labels: ['create-p8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P8 table, do either

- Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the **Add New** button. The new row in the table is created.Select a constrained Systems Element (Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization, Post, Function, Exchange Element, Entity Item, Resource Interaction). Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).
- Add the existing Resource Constraints by clicking the Add Existing button and select existing Resource Constraints from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a P8 table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the <strong>Add New</strong> button. The new row in the table is created.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6ce018c8-0c16-450f-b89b-b2c7c9909dd7"> S</ac:inline-comment-marker></span>elect a constrained Systems Element (Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization, Post, Function, Exchange Element, Entity Item, Resource Interaction). Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).</span></li><li>Add the existing Resource Constraints by clicking the <strong>Add Existing </strong> button and select existing Resource Constraints from the model.</li></ul><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438495 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00765: (2026x) Creating Pr diagram

- page_id: `254438495`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438495/2026x+Creating+Pr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:25.591+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) Pr Configuration Management
- labels: ['create-pr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Pr diagram

truenone

##### Create new Version Of Configuration

To create new Version Of Configuration in Pr diagram

1. Click the**Add New**button. New Version of Configuration is created.
2. Type its name.

##### Define the resource for the Version Of Configuration

To define the resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. Right-click the Version Of Configuration in the diagram pane and select **Resource**.
2. In the **Select Resource** dialog do one of the following:
  - Select an existing resource that has milestones defined.
  - Create new resource (described in the following procedure).

To create new resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. In the **Select Resource** dialog switch on the **Creation Mode**. 
 2. Select package where you want to create a resource. 
 3. Click the **Create** button, choose a resource category**Systems**,**Service**, or**Operational**, and select the desired resource.[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 4. In the Specification window of the newly createdresource type its name.

##### Specify Actual Project Milestones of the resource

Each resource must have two Actual Project Milestone kinds defined:

- Increment Milestone (defines the start of a period) .
- Out Of Service Milestone (defines the end of a period).

To specify Actual Project Milestones of the resource

1. On the left of the resource Specification window, select the **Milestones**property group.
2. Click the **Add** button to define an Actual Project Milestone for the resource. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the**Select Actual Project Milestone** dialog do one of the following:
  - Select an existing Actual Project Milestone .
  - Create new Actual Project Milestone (described in the following procedure).
4. Click **Close** to close the Specification window of anActual ProjectMilestone.

To create new Actual Project Milestoneof the resource

1. In the**Select Actual Project Milestone** dialog switch on the **Creation Mode**.
2. Select package where you want to create anActual Project Milestone.
3. Click the **Create** button, choose an Actual Project Milestonecategory**Strategic**, or**Acquisition**, and select the desired milestone kind. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the Specification window of the newly createdmilestone specify two properties: [ATTACHMENT filename='milestone_specification.png']
  - Name. Type a name of the the milestone.
  - Date . Select the Date property value celland click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Date and Time Settings dialog' space='']**Date and Time Settings** dialog choosea date and click **OK**.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438584 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00766: (2026x) Creating predefined relation map

- page_id: `254438584`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438584/2026x+Creating+predefined+relation+map
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:51.125+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures > (2026x) Managing Traceability and Impact Analysis > (2026x) Traceability relation maps
- labels: ['create-predefined-relation-map']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create predefined relation maps for the selected context elements.

To create the predefined relation map for the context element, do either

- Right-click the element, select Create Diagram > UPDM Analysis Diagrams , and choose the relation map you want to create.
- Right-click the element, select Related Elements and then choose the relation map you want to create.

You can also create the predefined relation maps in one of the following ways:

- From the Diagrams menu, select UPDM Analysis Diagrams , choose the relation map you want to create, and then select the context element.
- From the Package shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > UPDM Analysis Diagrams , choose the relation map you want to create, and then select the context element.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can create predefined relation maps for the selected context elements.</p><p>To create the predefined relation map for the context element, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click the element, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>UPDM Analysis Diagrams</strong>, and choose the relation map you want to create.</li><li>Right-click the element, select <strong>Related Elements</strong> and then choose the relation map you want to create.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also create the predefined relation maps in one of the following ways:</p><ul><li>From the Diagrams menu, select <strong>UPDM Analysis Diagrams</strong>, choose the relation map you want to create, and then select the context element.</li><li>From the Package shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>UPDM Analysis Diagrams</strong>, choose the relation map you want to create, and then select the context element.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439487 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00767: (2026x) Creating PV-1 diagram

- page_id: `254439487`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439487/2026x+Creating+PV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:11.400+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Project viewpoint > (2026x) PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships
- labels: ['create-pv-1-diagram']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

29486754

29486756

29486755

It is recommended to create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space=''] before creating a PV-1.

**To create a PV-1 diagram**

1. Create Projects.
2. Associate Projects by dragging one project (part) to the other (whole). Alternatively you can specify the whole and part properties.
3. Associate Projects with Organizations ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space=''] ) or Individual Person Roles ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space=''] ) responsible for them using Organizational Project Relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fb4f7f06-3bb3-4fe6-a17a-44cc79281125"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">29486754</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="152efd29-2bf6-40aa-b4bd-da77bd821ab5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">29486756</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9da694b2-2870-4d6a-aa42-fdd77c278668"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">29486755</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is recommended to create an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> before creating a PV-1.</p><p><strong>To create a PV-1 diagram</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Create Projects.</li><li>Associate Projects by dragging one project (part) to the other (whole). Alternatively you can specify the whole and part properties.</li><li>Associate Projects with Organizations (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) or Individual Person Roles (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) responsible for them using Organizational Project Relationship.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439466 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00768: (2026x) Creating PV-1 matrix

- page_id: `254439466`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439466/2026x+Creating+PV-1+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:08.165+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Project viewpoint > (2026x) PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships
- labels: ['create-pv-1-matrix', 'build-pv-1-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

29627372

29627374

29627373

It is recommended to create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space=''] before creating a PV-1.

The PV-1 is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Organizational resources (Organizations or Individual Person Roles) for Projects.

The rows in the PV-1 Responsibility Matrix represent Projects and the columns represent Actual Organizational resources (Organization or Individual Person Roles).

**To create a PV-1 matrix**

1. Specify the Row Scope (Projects).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Organizations and Individual Person Roles).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca3c2127-9851-4cb3-b9ad-a1b641a4d8b2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">29627372</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff39839b-02d7-43a4-942e-0347f1e52c68"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">29627374</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3aec0792-d756-4eb8-ae1d-c8731e8cc1d0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">29627373</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is recommended to create an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> before creating a PV-1.</p><p>The PV-1 is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Organizational resources (Organizations or Individual Person Roles) for Projects.</p><p>The rows in the PV-1 Responsibility Matrix represent Projects and the columns represent Actual Organizational resources (Organization or Individual Person Roles).</p><p><strong>To create a PV-1 matrix</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Projects).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Organizations and Individual Person Roles).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439460 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00769: (2026x) Creating PV-2 diagram

- page_id: `254439460`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439460/2026x+Creating+PV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:07.614+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Project viewpoint > (2026x) PV-2 Project Timelines
- labels: ['create-pv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

30904153

30904155

30904154

When you choose to create PV-2 diagram, the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**PV-2 Creation Wizard** opens.

To create a PV-2 diagram

1. Right-click the PV-2 Package and select Create Diagram > PV-2 Project Timelines .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**PV-2 Creation Wizard** .
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**PV-2 Creation Wizard** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create PV-2 Project Timelines** check box.

To create a PV-2 diagram manually when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**PV-2 Creation Wizard** is disabled

1. Right-click the PV-2 Package and select Create Diagram > PV-2 Project Timelines .
2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter . The blank PV-2 Project Timelines diagram is created.

To restore the usage of the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UPDM options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0c8b1982-ca96-4fa6-9cfb-3727db5a8a47"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">30904153</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c5402cee-5787-430b-a82b-f7e72513ecba"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">30904155</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a1ef739-a3bd-469b-ae2b-aad25ea4c206"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">30904154</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When you choose to create PV-2 diagram, the<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body> <strong>PV-2 Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</p><p>To create a PV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the PV-2 Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>PV-2 Project Timelines</strong>.</li><li>Follow the steps of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>PV-2 Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c77ed02-f212-4ece-8767-9903e864e6c7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not wish to use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>PV-2 Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> next time, clear the <strong>Show the wizard next time, when I create PV-2 Project Timelines</strong> check box.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a PV-2 diagram manually when <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>PV-2 Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> is disabled</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the PV-2 Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>PV-2 Project Timelines</strong>.</li><li><p>Type the diagram name.</p></li><li>Press <strong>Enter</strong>. <br />The blank <strong>PV-2 Project Timelines</strong> diagram is created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be5825d5-3d47-4481-9536-da3fe0194dee"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To restore the usage of the<strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[PV-2 Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog select the <strong>UPDM</strong> options group.</li><li>In the General properties, set the <strong>Show PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1 Creation Wizard</strong> <strong>each Time Creating New Chart</strong> property value to<em> true</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439555 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00770: (2026x) Creating PV-3 matrix

- page_id: `254439555`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439555/2026x+Creating+PV-3+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:19.516+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Project viewpoint > (2026x) PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping
- labels: ['create-pv-3-matrix', 'build-pv-3-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

35757522

35757524

35757523

PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a capability.

The rows of this matrix are Capabilities and the columns are Projects.

To create a PV-3 matrix

1. Specify Row Scope (Capabilities).
2. Specify Column Scope (Projects).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Activity is part of Project (“Activity Part of Project” relationship and a part of Capability (“Activity Part of Capability”) relationship). This transitive structure of relations maps Capabilities to Projects.

Project owns Increment Milestones that are related to Resources exhibiting capabilities.

Both transitive structures of relations map Capabilities to Projects. The mapping is displayed in the Matrix.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d1c24062-a21b-4cd5-b24a-7d343de04eba"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">35757522</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fba86877-4b52-42af-809e-65135fec200e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">35757524</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aefe6673-d749-46ed-b8a6-333ed3f13f74"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">35757523</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a capability.</p><p>The rows of this matrix are Capabilities and the columns are Projects.</p><p>To create a PV-3 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Specify <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Projects).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p>Activity is part of Project (“Activity Part of Project” relationship and a part of Capability (“Activity Part of Capability”) relationship). This transitive structure of relations maps Capabilities to Projects.</p><p>Project owns Increment Milestones that are related to Resources exhibiting capabilities.</p><p>Both transitive structures of relations map Capabilities to Projects. The mapping is displayed in the Matrix.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439368 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00771: (2026x) Creating Resource Interaction in P1 diagram

- page_id: `254439368`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439368/2026x+Creating+Resource+Interaction+in+P1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:02.180+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-p1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Interaction in P1 diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Resource Association and on the smart manipulator, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
  - In the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction.png'] and connect the Resources on the diagram pane.
2. Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Interaction Manager**dialog to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a Resource Interaction in P1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Resource Interaction wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Interaction</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Select the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Association</span> and on the smart manipulator, click <ac:image ac:alt="New Resource Interaction" ac:title="New Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br /></span></li><li>In the diagram pallet, click <ac:image ac:alt="Resource Interaction" ac:title="Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and connect the Resources on the diagram pane.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="0d895a14-702c-4420-8c23-20e1efdc794e" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span class="confluence-link">You can use <span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Resource Interaction Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><strong><span class="confluence-link">Resource Interaction Manager</span> </strong>dialog</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438424 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00772: (2026x) Creating Resource Interaction in P2 diagram

- page_id: `254438424`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438424/2026x+Creating+Resource+Interaction+in+P2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:12.829+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Physical Resource viewpoint > (2026x) P2 Resource Structure
- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-p2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Interaction in P2 diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Resource Interface or Resource Connector and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_interaction.png'] .
  - On the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction.png'] and then click the Resource Interface or Resource Connector.
  - On the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction.png'] and then connect the Resource elements on the diagram pane.
2. Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Interaction Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select a Function Edge and on the smart manipulator toolbar click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_interaction.png'] .
  - On the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction.png'] and then click the Function Edge.
2. Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use ****[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Interaction Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interactions data.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438911 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00777: (2026x) Creating Resource Interaction in SV-10c diagram

- page_id: `254438911`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438911/2026x+Creating+Resource+Interaction+in+SV-10c+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:22.794+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Systems viewpoint > (2026x) SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description
- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-sv-10c']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Interaction in SV-10c diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='']**RealizedResource Interaction** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Resource Message and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_interaction.png'] .
  - On the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction.png'] and then click the Resource Message.
  - On the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction.png'] and connect the Resource Lifeline elements on the diagram pane.
2. Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Interaction Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a Resource Interaction in SV-10c diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong><span class="confluence-link"> </span></strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Resource Interaction wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong><span class="confluence-link">Realized </span>Resource Interaction</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select the Resource Message and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:alt="New Resource Interaction" ac:title="New Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>On the diagram pallet, click <ac:image ac:alt="Resource Interaction" ac:title="Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and then click the Resource Message.</li><li>On the diagram pallet, click <ac:image ac:alt="Resource Interaction" ac:title="Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and connect the Resource Lifeline elements on the diagram pane.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="c2bfb81a-7f38-42d5-8cc1-8b64b196c8a6" ac:name="info" data.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Interaction in SV-2 diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Resource Association and on the smart manipulator, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
  - In the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction.png'] and connect the Resources on the diagram pane.
2. Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Interaction Manager**dialog to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.

- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-sv-4-systems-functionality-description']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Resource Interaction in the SV-4 Systems Functionality Description diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select a Function Edge and on the smart manipulator toolbar click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_interaction.png'] .
  - On the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction.png'] and then click the Function Edge.
2. Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use ****[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Interaction Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interactions data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

  - Select the Resource Association and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_interaction.png'] .
  - On the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction.png'] and then click the Resource Association .
  - On the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction.png'] and then connect the Service Accesses on the diagram pane.
2. Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Interaction Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438692 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00784: (2026x) Creating Resource Interaction in SvcV-4 Service Functionality Description

- page_id: `254438692`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438692/2026x+Creating+Resource+Interaction+in+SvcV-4+Service+Functionality+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:05.955+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Services viewpoint > (2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description
- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-svcv-4-service-functionality-description']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Resource Interaction in the SvcV-4 Service Functionality Description diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select a Function Edge and on the smart manipulator toolbar click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_interaction.png'] .
  - On the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction.png'] and then click the Function Edge.
2. Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use ****[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Interaction Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interactions data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Realized Resource Interaction wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Interaction</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> </span>by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select a Function Edge and on the smart manipulator toolbar click <ac:image ac:alt="New Resource Interaction" ac:title="New Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>On the diagram pallet, click <ac:image ac:alt="Resource Interaction" ac:title="Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and then click the Function Edge.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="01e441ba-f595-4280-8b87-f4201a65392e" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can use <strong><span class="confluence-link"> </span></strong><span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Resource Interaction Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><strong><span class="confluence-link">Resource Interaction</span> Manager</strong></span></ac:link-body></ac:link> dialog that enables to analyze and manage the <span class="confluence-link">Resource Interaction</span>s data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
