Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button and select the owning element for Measure Type. Specify Measurements to the Measure Type and Service Accesses to be Measured - directly in the table cells.</li><li>Add existing Measure Types. Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select Existing Measurement Types.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438681 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00819: (2026x) Creating SvcV-8 diagram

- page_id: `254438681`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438681/2026x+Creating+SvcV-8+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:04.946+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Services viewpoint > (2026x) SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description
- labels: ['create-svcv-8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a SvcV-8 diagram, do either

- Create or select existing Whole Life Configuration element and create its own SvcV-8 diagram.
- Create SvcV-8 diagram under the Package. The Whole Life Configuration as the owner is created automatically.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a SvcV-8 diagram, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Create or select existing Whole Life Configuration element and create its own SvcV-8 diagram.</li><li>Create SvcV-8 diagram under the Package. The Whole Life Configuration as the owner is created automatically.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438721 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00820: (2026x) Creating SvcV-9 table

- page_id: `254438721`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438721/2026x+Creating+SvcV-9+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:08.465+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Services viewpoint > (2026x) SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast
- labels: ['create-svcv-9']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a SvcV-9 table

1. Add Rows to the table by doing any of the following:
  - Adding a new Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the Add New button, select a Standard or Protocol and its owner.
  - Adding an existing Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the Add Existing button and select one or more existing Standards or/and Protocols.
2. Add columns to the table by clicking the Time Period button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Time Periods dialog' space='']**Time Periods** dialog opens. Specify the forecast time periods:
  - Select or create a Timeline package to store the Forecast dates.
  - Select the Forecast Kind.
  - Specify additional options needed according to the selected Forecast Kind.
3. Enter the Subjects of Forecast by clicking the **…** button on the cell you want to fill in and select the Standards and Protocols to forecast. Specify the date of the Forecast. By default the date is the starting date of the selected time period.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a SvcV-9 table</p><hr /><ol><li>Add Rows to the table by doing any of the following:<br /><ul><li>Adding a new Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the <strong>Add New</strong> button, select a Standard or Protocol and its owner.</li><li>Adding an existing Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select one or more existing Standards or/and Protocols.</li></ul></li><li>Add columns to the table by clicking the <strong>Time Period</strong> button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Time Periods dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Time Periods</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens. Specify the forecast time periods:<br /><ul><li>Select or create a Timeline package to store the Forecast dates.</li><li>Select the Forecast Kind.</li><li>Specify additional options needed according to the selected Forecast Kind.</li></ul></li><li><p>Enter the Subjects of Forecast by clicking the <strong>…</strong> button on the cell you want to fill in and select the Standards and Protocols to forecast. Specify the date of the Forecast.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default the date is the starting date of the selected time period.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439406 space=UPDM2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00821: (2026x) CV-1 Vision

- page_id: `254439406`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439406/2026x+CV-1+Vision
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:16:14.444+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-1', 'cv-1-vision']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

845636083

845636085

845636084

**Description**

The CV-1 addresses the enterprise concerns associated with the overall vision for transformational endeavors and thus defines the strategic context for a group of capabilities. The purpose of a CV-1 is to provide a strategic context for the capabilities described in the Architectural Description. It also provides a high-level scope for the

Architectural Description which is more general than the scenario-based scope defined in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic' space='']. CV-1 purpose is identical to MODAF StV-1 product usage. The difference between CV-1 model and StV-1 product lies in different concepts and relationships between them.

**Implementation**

CV-1 can be represented using a CV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

CV-1 provides a high-level scope for the Architectural Description which is more general than the scenario-based scope defined in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic' space=''].

845636082

**Related Pages:**

- Desired Effect
- Vision
- Vision Statement
- Capability
- Enterprise Phase

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438369 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00822: (2026x) CV-2 Capability Taxonomy

- page_id: `254438369`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438369/2026x+CV-2+Capability+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:07.464+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-2', 'cv-2-capability-taxonomy']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1354523297

1354523300

1354523299

**Description**

The CV-2 captures capability taxonomies. The model presents a hierarchy of capabilities. These capabilities may be presented in context of a timeline – i.e., it can show the required capabilities for current and future capabilities.

The CV-2 specifies all the capabilities that are referenced throughout one or more architectures. In addition, it can be used as a source document for the development of high-level use cases and user requirements.

The intended usage of the CV-2 includes:

- Identification of capability requirements.
- Capability planning (capability taxonomy).
- Codifying required capability elements.
- Capability audit.
- Capability gap analysis.
- Source for the derivation of cohesive sets of user requirements.
- Providing reference capabilities for architectures.

**Implementation**

CV-2 can be represented using a CV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The CV-2 is used to capture and organize the capability functions – required for the vision set out in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) CV-1 Vision' space=''].

1354523296

Related Pages:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Capability' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Capability of Performer' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activity Part of Capability' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438372 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00823: (2026x) CV-3 Capability Phasing

- page_id: `254438372`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438372/2026x+CV-3+Capability+Phasing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:07.811+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-3', 'cv-3-capability-phasing']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1414409397

1414409399

1414409398

**Description**

The CV-3 addresses the planned achievement of capability at different points in time or during specific periods of time, i.e., capability phasing. The CV-3 supports the capability audit processes and similar processes used across the different COIs by providing a method to identify gaps or duplication in capability provision. The CV-3 indicates capability increments, which should be associated with delivery milestones within acquisition projects (when the increments are associated with capability deliveries).

The intended usage of the CV-3 includes:

- Capability planning (capability phasing).
- Capability integration planning.
- Capability gap analysis.

**Implementation**

CV-3 can be represented using a CV-3 diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The CV-3 can be presented as a table consisting of rows representing Capabilities (derived from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space=''] model) and columns representing phases (from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) CV-1 Vision' space=''] model).

1414409396

**Related Pages:**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Capability' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Capability of Performer' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Increment Milestone' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Out of Service Milestone' space='UPDM2P190']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Actual Project' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438375 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00824: (2026x) CV-4 Capability Dependencies

- page_id: `254438375`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438375/2026x+CV-4+Capability+Dependencies
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:08.068+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-4', 'cv-4-capability-dependencies']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1415019815

1415019818

1415019817

**Description**

The CV-4 describes the dependencies between planned capabilities. It also defines logical groupings of capabilities.

The CV-4 is intended to provide a means of analyzing the dependencies between capabilities. The groupings of capabilities are logical, and the purpose of the groupings is to guide enterprise management. In particular, the dependencies and groupings may suggest specific interactions between acquisition projects to achieve the overall capability.

The intended usage of the CV-4 includes:

- Identification of capability dependencies.
- Capability management (impact analysis for options, disposal etc.).

**Implementation**

CV-4 can be represented using a CV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This contrasts with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space=''] model which also deals with relationships between Capabilities; but [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space=''] only addresses specialization-generalization relationship (i.e., capability taxonomy). However Capabilities used in CV-4 are usually created in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space=''] model.

1415019814

**Related Pages:**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Capability' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Capability Property' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

It also defines logical groupings of capabilities.</p><p>The CV-4 is intended to provide a means of analyzing the dependencies between capabilities. The groupings of capabilities are logical, and the purpose of the groupings is to guide enterprise management. In particular, the dependencies and groupings may suggest specific interactions between acquisition projects to achieve the overall capability.</p><p>The intended usage of the CV-4 includes:</p><ul><li>Identification of capability dependencies.</li><li>Capability management (impact analysis for options, disposal etc.).</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>CV-4 can be represented using a CV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="CV-4 Capability Dependencies" ac:alt="CV-4 Capability Dependencies"><ri:attachment ri:filename="CV-4.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) CV-4 Capability Dependencies" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>This contrasts with <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) CV-2 Capability Taxonomy" /></ac:link> model which also deals with relationships between Capabilities; but <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) CV-2 Capability Taxonomy" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> only addresses specialization-generalization relationship (i.e., capability taxonomy). However Capabilities used in CV-4 are usually created in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) CV-2 Capability Taxonomy" /></ac:link> model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="07eaaf28-d857-4529-b88d-f0deb5988925"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415019814</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="21d3b4f1-1d38-4fab-acf6-e2a01c6c1f0e" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Capability" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Capability Property" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438378 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00825: (2026x) CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping

- page_id: `254438378`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438378/2026x+CV-5+Capability+to+Organizational+Development+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:08.388+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-5', 'cv-5-capability-to-organizational-development-mapping']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1415358073

1415358076

1415358075

**Description**

The CV-5 addresses the fulfillment of capability requirements.

This model shows the planned capability deployment and interconnection for a particular Capability Phase. The CV-5 can also be used to support the capability management process and, in particular, assist the planning of fielding.

The intended usage of the CV-5 includes:

- Fielding planning.
- Capability integration planning.
- Capability options analysis.
- Capability redundancy/overlap/gap analysis.
- Identification of deployment level shortfalls.

**Implementation**

CV-5 can be represented using:

- A CV-5 table.
- A CV-5 spreadsheet report.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To conduct a comprehensive analysis, several CV- 5 can be created to represent the different Enterprise Phases. Each CV-5 represents different Enterprise Phases defined in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) CV-1 Vision' space=''].

The CV-5 shows deployment of Capabilities defined in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space=''] to specific organizations.

Deployed or No Longer Used Resources are taken from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space=''].

Actual Organizational Resources are taken from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space=''].

The timing is based on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) PV-2 Project Timelines' space=''] indicating delivery of Capabilities to actual organizational resources, and also the point at which those organizational resources cease to use a particular Capability.

1415358072

**Related Pages:**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating CV-5 table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying CV-5 table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Generating document based reports' space='']

The CV-5 can also be used to support the capability management process and, in particular, assist the planning of fielding.</p><p>The intended usage of the CV-5 includes:</p><ul><li>Fielding planning.</li><li>Capability integration planning.</li><li>Capability options analysis.</li><li>Capability redundancy/overlap/gap analysis.</li><li>Identification of deployment level shortfalls.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>CV-5 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A CV-5 table.</li><li>A CV-5 spreadsheet report.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="CV-5 table" ac:alt="CV-5 table"><ri:attachment ri:filename="CV-5_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>To conduct a comprehensive analysis, several CV- 5 can be created to represent the different Enterprise Phases. Each CV-5 represents different Enterprise Phases defined in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) CV-1 Vision" /></ac:link>.</p><p>The CV-5 shows deployment of Capabilities defined in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) CV-2 Capability Taxonomy" /></ac:link> to specific organizations.</p><p>Deployed or No Longer Used Resources are taken from <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-1 Services Context Description" /></ac:link>.</p><p>Actual Organizational Resources are taken from <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>The timing is based on <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) PV-2 Project Timelines" /></ac:link> indicating delivery of Capabilities to actual organizational resources, and also the point at which those organizational resources cease to use a particular Capability.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ee8ab54e-bf12-4126-9fd5-c2554ff1271e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415358072</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438381 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00826: (2026x) CV-6 Capability to Operational Activities Mapping

- page_id: `254438381`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438381/2026x+CV-6+Capability+to+Operational+Activities+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:08.719+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-6', 'cv-6-matrix', 'cv-6-capability-to-operational-activities-mapping']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1415884637

1415884640

1415884639

**Description**

The CV-6 describes the mapping between the capabilities required and the activities that enable those capabilities.

It is important to ensure that the operational activity matches the required capability. The CV-6 DoDAF-described Model provides a bridge between capability analyzed using CVs and operational activities analyzed using OVs. Specifically, it identifies how operational activities can be performed using various available capability elements. It is similar in function to the SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix. The capability to activity mappings may include both situations where activities fully satisfy the desired capability and those where the activity only partially meets the capability requirement.

The intended usage of the CV-6 includes:

- Tracing capability requirements to operational activities.
- Capability audit.

**Implementation**

CV-6 can be represented using a CV-6 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Capabilities are used as row elements and the Operational Activities are used as column elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This model is analogous to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix' space=''] – but provides the interface between Capability and Operational Models rather than Operational to System Models.

1415884636

**Related Page:**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Capability' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Operational Activity' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activity Part of Capability' space='']

The CV-6 DoDAF-described Model provides a bridge between capability analyzed using CVs and operational activities analyzed using OVs. Specifically, it identifies how operational activities can be performed using various available capability elements. It is similar in function to the SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix. The capability to activity mappings may include both situations where activities fully satisfy the desired capability and those where the activity only partially meets the capability requirement.</p><p>The intended usage of the CV-6 includes:</p><ul><li>Tracing capability requirements to operational activities.</li><li>Capability audit.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>CV-6 can be represented using a CV-6 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Capabilities are used as row elements and the Operational Activities are used as column elements.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="CV-6 Capability to Operational Activities Mapping" ac:alt="CV-6 Capability to Operational Activities Mapping"><ri:attachment ri:filename="CV-6.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) CV-6 Capability to Operational Activities Mapping" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>This model is analogous to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-5a Operational Activity to System Function Traceability Matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> – but provides the interface between Capability and Operational Models rather than Operational to System Models.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d773adc2-77f0-48e6-818e-2e24c7776074"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415884636</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Page:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="2aa70e64-ec49-49fb-843d-7d0b9b529a0a" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Capability" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Operational Activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Activity Part of Capability" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Activity Part Of Capability]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438384 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00827: (2026x) CV-7 Capability to Services Mapping

- page_id: `254438384`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438384/2026x+CV-7+Capability+to+Services+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:09.021+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-7', 'cv-7-capability-to-services-mapping', 'cv-7-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1416278289

1416278291

1416278290

**Description**

The CV-7 describes the mapping between the capabilities required and the services that enable those capabilities.

It is important to ensure that the services match the required capability. The CV-7 provides a bridge between capability analyzed using CVs and services analyzed using SvcVs. Specifically, it identifies how services can be performed using various available capability elements. It is similar in function to the SV-5a which maps system functions to operational activities. The capability to service mappings may include both situations where a service fully satisfies the desired capability and those where the service only partially meets the capability requirement.

The intended usage of the CV-7 includes:

- Tracing capability requirements to services.
- Capability audit.

**Implementation**

CV-7 can be represented using a CV-7 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. Service Interfaces are used as row elements and Capabilities are used as column elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This model is analogous to the SV-5a Operational Activity to System Function Traceability Matrix – but provides the interface between Capability and Service Models rather than Operational to System Models.

1416278288

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439590 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00828: (2026x) D1 Master Data

- page_id: `254439590`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439590/2026x+D1+Master+Data
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:24.599+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Deployed Resources viewpoint
- labels: ['d1-master-data', 'd1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The D1 Master Data view specifies standard reference data in the deployed space. This includes organizations, locations, facilities, and individual platforms. The D1 view presents all the data as a hierarchy and provides a text definition for each one by displaying the following properties:

- The name used for this element in the architecture.
- Alternative names for this element, for example, if the element is listed in the NATO ontology it may have multiple names.
- A brief description of the element.

**Implementation** 
The D1 view can be represented using:

- An D1 table. [ATTACHMENT filename='D1_table.png']

- A D1 MS Word document report.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Generating document based reports' space='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Definition
- Alias
- Same As
- UPDM Element

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438480 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00829: (2026x) D2 Deployed Resources

- page_id: `254438480`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438480/2026x+D2+Deployed+Resources
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:21.666+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Deployed Resources viewpoint
- labels: ['d2-deployed-resources', 'd2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The D2 Deployed Resources view covers the structure of specific, individual Resources. This could be actual instances of systems, organizations or natural resources.

The D2 shows how specific individual resources are deployed and configured. The scope for the D2 view is broad and covers human and non-human resources. This view can therefore be used for depicting organizational structures.

The D2 view may be used to:

- Specify configuration of deployed resources.
- Identify architecture stakeholders.
- Identify process owners.
- Illustrate current or future organization structures.

**Implementation**

The D2 view can be represented using:

- An D2 Deployed Resources diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='D2.png']
- A UML Class diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating D2 diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Instantiating Structures' space='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Organization
- Post
- Person
- Command
- Competence
- Provides Competence
- Requires Competence
- Actual Organization
- Actual Post
- Actual Person
- Fills Post
- Actual Organization Relationship
- Actual Organization Role
- Owns Process

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438502 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00830: (2026x) Data and Information viewpoint

- page_id: `254438502`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438502/2026x+Data+and+Information+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:28.110+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['data-and-information-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

638453214

638453216

638453215

“DoDAF-described Models within the Data and Information Viewpoint provide a means of portraying the operational and business information requirements and rules that are managed within and used as constraints on the organizations business activities. Experience gained from many enterprise architecture efforts within the DoD led to the identification of several levels of abstraction necessary to accurately communicate the information needs of an organization or enterprise. The appropriate level or levels of abstraction for a given architecture are dependent on the use and the intended users of the architecture. Where appropriate, the data captured in this viewpoint needs to be considered by COIs.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

**Cameo Data Modeler plugin integration**

You can use the Entity Relationship diagram for conceptual, logical, and physical data modeling in all views of the Data and Information Viewpoint. It supports the information engineering notation within these views.

638453212

**Related Pages:**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f7d6786f-7719-4cd5-81df-175fe62c0c68"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">638453214</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="08e9b6c9-497b-4a13-b54a-0a904edc964a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">638453216</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e46d5349-7404-4b2a-919d-508b2d852743"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">638453215</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>“DoDAF-described Models within the Data and Information Viewpoint provide a means of portraying the operational and business information requirements and rules that are managed within and used as constraints on the organizations business activities. Experience gained from many enterprise architecture efforts within the DoD led to the identification of several levels of abstraction necessary to accurately communicate the information needs of an organization or enterprise. The appropriate level or levels of abstraction for a given architecture are dependent on the use and the intended users of the architecture. Where appropriate, the data captured in this viewpoint needs to be considered by COIs.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]</p><p><strong>Cameo Data Modeler plugin integration</strong></p><p>You can use the Entity Relationship diagram for conceptual, logical, and physical data modeling in all views of the Data and Information Viewpoint. It supports the information engineering notation within these views.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="92f8d61c-1ff4-4508-b657-bc841db9feb5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">638453212</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="0eab76c7-9323-48a9-8aae-cc175fee1043" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439772 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00831: (2026x) Date and Time Settings dialog

- page_id: `254439772`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439772/2026x+Date+and+Time+Settings+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:35.198+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Dialogs
- labels: ['date-and-time-settings-dialog']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Date and Time Settings** dialog is provided for defining a particular date and time. The dialog supports ISO8601, the International Standard for the representation of date and time.

The dialog can be usedfor elements or views that have date and time properties (e.g. Actual Project Milestone, Actual Project Enterprise Phase, etc.).

To open the **Date and Time Settings** dialog

1. Open element's Specification window.
2. Select the Date property value cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='[...]button_small.png'] . The Date and Time Settings dialog opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Dialog element | Element type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Set the date and time | Drop-down list | A date and time value that exists in the model.Select a new date and time value from the drop-down list. |
| Select timeline | Text box | A package for the data grouping. |
| Date | Visual calendar | A date value in the visual calendar.To select a day, click the day on the calendar.To move through months, use arrows on the left and on the right in the title section of the calendar.To move through years, use double-arrows on the left and on the right in the title section of the calendar. |
| Date | Spin box | A date value in the text format (“YYYY-MM-DD”).To change the time value you can do one of the followingType a new valid date value.Click up and down arrows to increase or decrease the current value. |
| Time | Spin box | A time value in the text format (“hh-ss-mm”).To change the time value you can do one of the followingType a new valid time value.Click up and down arrows to increase or decrease the current value. |
| Time zone | Drop-down list | A time zone value.Select a new time zone value from the drop-down list.The default time zone value is the one of your computer regional settings. |

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [...] (by the Select timeline box) | Click to select or create a new Timeline. The Select Timeline dialog opens. |
| Today | Click to set the Date box value to the current date of your computer. |
| Now | Click to set the Time box value to the current time of your computer. |
| Default | Click to set the Time zone box value to the value that is set in your computer. |

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Capability Provision Creation Wizard
- Deployment Milestones wizard

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1097099203

1097099205

1097099204

##### Description

UPDM: An ArchitecturalDescription describes the architecture for an EnterprisePhase. The DefinesArchitecture stereotype establishes a relationship between ArchitecturalDescription and EnterprisePhase.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Realization

##### Related DoDAF views

[AV-1 Overview and Summary Information](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) AV-1+Overview+and+Summary+Information)

##### Related MODAF views

[AV-1 Overview and Summary Information](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) AV-1+Overview+and+Summary+Information)

##### Related NAF views

[NAV-1 Overview and Summary Information](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) NAV-1+Overview+and+Summary+Information)

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1414795219

1414795221

1414795220

**To delete a Capability from the chart**

1. Select a Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete From Chart button.

**To delete a Capability from the model**

1. Select a Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

## PAGE 00839: (2026x) Deleting Sub Capabilities from CV-3 diagram

- page_id: `254439442`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1414814072

1414814075

1414814074

**To delete a Sub Capability from the chart**

1. Select a Sub Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete From Chart button.

**To delete a Sub Capability from the model**

1. Select a Sub Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

915397083

915397086

915397084

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: Asserts that an ActualOrganisationResource started to use, or is slated to start using a CapabilityConfiguration from a specific point in time.This is used to describe capabilities going into service with specific organizations or posts.

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| configuration : CapabilityConfiguration[1..*] | Affected CapabilityConfigurations. |
| usedBy : ActualOrganizationalResource[1..*] | ActualOrganizationalResources using CapabilityConfiguration deployed at this Milestone. |

##### Extensions

UML Instance Specification

##### Related DoDAF views

[CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) CV-5+Capability+to+Organizational+Development+Mapping)

[SV-8 Systems Evolution Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/SV-8+Systems+Evolution+Description)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/MODAF.+StV-5+Capability+to+Organization+Deployment+Mapping)

[SV-8 Systems Evolution Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/SV-8+Systems+Evolution+Description)

##### Related NAF views

[NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/NCV-5+Capability+to+Organisational+Deployment+Mapping)

[NSV-8 System Evolution Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P190/NSV-8+System+Evolution+Description)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439778 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00842: (2026x) Deployment Milestones wizard

- page_id: `254439778`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439778/2026x+Deployment+Milestones+wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:35.741+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Dialogs
- labels: ['deployment-milestones-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The purpose of the **Deployment Milestones** wizard is to define Capability deployment in a particular time frame of the enterprise life cycle.

To open the **Deployment Milestones** wizard do the following

1. Open a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping' space=''] (DoDAF), StV-5 (MODAF), NCV-5 (NAF), or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Dr Deployment Schedule' space=''] (NAF 4.0) table.
2. Click a cell wherein you want to add a Capability Deployment.
3. Click the + button that appears on the right side of the cell. [ATTACHMENT filename='Dr_table_add_resources.png']

The input data for the wizard is the Capability to be deployed and the Actual Organizational Resource responsible for that Capability deployment.

The wizard consists of three steps:

1. Select Resources. How to select Resources?In this step you are defining Resources required for the deployment of Capability. You can search for existing Resources or create a new one.[IMAGE alt='' src='']You can finish the Deployment Milestones creation after the wizard’s first step, if you do not need to set other than default start and end dates.
2. Set the start date and time for the selected Resources to be used by the Actual Organizational Resource in deploying the Capability. How to specify the start date and time for the selected Resources?In this step you can specify the start date and time for the Capability Deployment with selected Resources. For the date and time specification the wizard provides you with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Date and Time Settings dialog' space='']**Date and Time Setting** dialog. By default selected start date and time are the same as corresponding dates of the table context (Enterprise Phase).The start date for the Resource usage can not be later than the end date of the table context. You will be able to finish the **Deployment Milestones** wizard only if start date is specified.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Set the end date and time for the selected Resources to be no longer used by the Actual Organizational Resource in deploying the Capability. How to specify the end date and time for the selected Resources?In this step you can specify the end date and time for the Capability Deployment with selected Resources. For the date and time specification the wizard provides you with the Date and Time Setting dialog. By default selected end date and time are the same as corresponding dates of the table context (Enterprise Phase).The end date for the Resource usage can not be earlier than the start date of the table context. You will be able to finish the **Deployment Milestones** wizard if the end date is not specified.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Elements that can be Actual Organizational Resources are listed in the following table.

|  | DoDAF | MODAF / NAF/ NAF 4.0 |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Actual Organizational Resources | OrganizationIndividual PersonRole | Actual OrganizationActual Post |

An Actual Organizational Resource uses assigned Resources to deploy a Capability in a specified time period. On the basis of the data specified in the wizard the following actions are taken:

1. Resources are linked to the Actual Organizational Resource through a pair of Deployment and No Longer Used Milestones.
2. Start and end dates are set for a Resource usage through a pair of Deployment and No Longer Used Milestones.
3. Capability of Performer (DoDAF) or an Exhibits (MODAF/NAF) relationship is created between Resources and the Capability. The relationships are not duplicated. If a relationship already exists, it is reused.

The wizard suggests you default start and end dates for the Resource usage. The dates matches start and end dates of an Enterprise Phase. As soon as the dates are defined Yukon finish the wizard while being in the first.

The Resource and its Deployed and No Longer Used Milestones are created in the same package that already contains the Enterprise Phase (the time frame of the Capability deployment).

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Dr table' space='']Creating Dr table
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating CV-5 table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying CV-5 table' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

For the date and time specification the wizard provides you with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Date and Time Settings dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Date and Time Setting</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>. By default selected start date and time are the same as corresponding dates of the table context (Enterprise Phase).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="2aeaf087-6e32-4bfb-8357-ed8801f7246a" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The start date for the Resource usage can not be later than the end date of the table context. You will be able to finish the <strong>Deployment Milestones</strong> wizard only if start date is specified.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:alt="Specifying deployment date in Deployment Milestones wizard" ac:title="Specifying deployment date in Deployment Milestones wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="deployment_milestones_wizard_step2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Deployment Milestones wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Set the end date and time for the selected Resources to be no longer used by the Actual Organizational Resource in deploying the Capability.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="011aa112-64ad-43e3-a5aa-b2d4ce9e1adb" ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to specify the end date and time for the selected Resources?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In this step you can specify the end date and time for the Capability Deployment with selected Resources. For the date and time specification the wizard provides you with the Date and Time Setting dialog. By default selected end date and time are the same as corresponding dates of the table context (Enterprise Phase).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="22148d28-e480-4577-83a3-a50b5eb00146" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The end date for the Resource usage can not be earlier than the start date of the table context. You will be able to finish the <strong>Deployment Milestones</strong> wizard if the end date is not specified.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:alt="Specifying no longer used date in Deployment Milestones wizard" ac:title="Specifying no longer used date in Deployment Milestones wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="deployment_milestones_wizard_step3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Deployment Milestones wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p>Elements that can be Actual Organizational Resources are listed in the following table.</p><table><tbody><tr><th> </th><th>DoDAF</th><th><p>MODAF / NAF/ NAF 4.0</p></th></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Actual Organizational Resources</strong></p></td><td><p>Organization</p><p>Individual Person</p><p>Role</p></td><td><p>Actual Organization</p><p>Actual Post</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p>An Actual Organizational Resource uses assigned Resources to deploy a Capability in a specified time period. On the basis of the data specified in the wizard the following actions are taken:</p><ol><li>Resources are linked to the Actual Organizational Resource through a pair of Deployment and No Longer Used Milestones.</li><li>Start and end dates are set for a Resource usage through a pair of Deployment and No Longer Used Milestones.</li><li><p>Capability of Performer (DoDAF) or an Exhibits (MODAF/NAF) relationship is created between Resources and the Capability.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="4c67e34d-03c2-411c-90f8-cc0ef439bd2b" ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The relationships are not duplicated. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439789 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00845: (2026x) Diagram Creation Wizard

- page_id: `254439789`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439789/2026x+Diagram+Creation+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:36.735+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Dialogs
- labels: ['diagram-creation-wizard', 'cv-3-creation-wizard', 'pv-2-creation-wizard', 'acv-2-creation-wizard', 'stv-3-creation-wizard', 'npv-1-creation-wizard', 'ncv-5-creation-wizard', 'lr-lines-of-development-creation-wizard', 'cr-capability-roadmap-creation-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Diagram Creation Wizard** is used to create the following diagrams:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) CV-3 Capability Phasing' space=''] (DoDAF)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) PV-2 Project Timelines' space=''] (DoDAF)
- AcV-2 Programme Timelines (MODAF)
- StV-3 Capability Phasing (MODAF)
- NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationships (NAF v.3)
- NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping (NAF v.3)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Lr Lines of Development' space=''] (NAF v.4)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Cr Capability Roadmap' space=''] (NAF v.4)

The name of this wizard depends on particular diagram name: **<diagram name> Creation Wizard**:

- CV-3 Creation Wizard
- PV-2 Creation Wizard
- AcV-2 Creation Wizard
- StV-3 Creation Wizard
- NPV-1 Creation Wizard
- NCV-5 Creation Wizard
- Lr Lines of Development Creation Wizard
- Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard

To open the **Diagram Creation Wizard**

1. Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> . The Diagram Creation Wizard opens.

The wizard consists of three steps:

1. Name and owning package specification. How to specify a name and owning package?In this step, you can specify the diagram name and select or create the owning package. If you want to create a new owning package, use the **Create Owner** button. Also, you can clone an existing package by clicking the **Clone** button.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Capability selection. How to select Capabilities?In this step, you can select Capabilities from the list.[IMAGE alt='' src='']ButtonDescription[IMAGE alt='' src='']Selects a Capability.Alternatively, double-click the Capability.[IMAGE alt='' src='']Removes a Capability.[IMAGE alt='' src='']Removes all Capabilities.
3. The chart property setting. How to set the chart properties?In this step, you can set the display style of the chart.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you do not wish to use the this wizard next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create <diagram name>** check box.

To restore the usage of the**Diagram Creation Wizard**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UPDM options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart)' space='']Creating Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart)
- Creating Cr diagram
- Creating CV-3 diagram
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating PV-2 diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 00847: (2026x) Displaying all Sub Capabilities in Cr diagram

- page_id: `254439070`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439070/2026x+Displaying+all+Sub+Capabilities+in+Cr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:35.065+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Concepts viewpoint > (2026x) Cr Capability Roadmap
- labels: ['display-all-in-cr', 'display-sub-capability-in-cr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To display all Sub Capabilities

1. Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to display.
2. In the Cr diagram toolbar, click the Display All button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To display all Sub Capabilities</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to display.</li><li>In the Cr diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Display All</strong> button.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438979 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00849: (2026x) Displaying Custom Measurements

- page_id: `254438979`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438979/2026x+Displaying+Custom+Measurements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:27.534+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures > (2026x) Managing Measurement Sets and Actual Measurements
- labels: ['add-measurement', 'delete-measurement', 'remove-measurement', 'clear-all', 'display-custom-measurements']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can display custom Measurements in addition to predefined ones in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space=''] (NOV-3), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space=''] (NSV-6), and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix' space=''] tables by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying Custom Measurements' space='']. Measurements can be displayed for the Resource Interactions, Operational Exchanges (in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space=''] (NOV-3) table), Operational Exchange, and Resource Interaction. 
When you are adding a new Measurement column, Measurements are collected from the elements used only in the active table. The collected Measurements are displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Measurements dialog' space='']**Measurements** dialog. You can select the desired Measurement which will be displayed as a newly created column in the table.

You can manage custom Measurements byremoving them[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying Custom Measurements' space='']. 
 
addTo add a column with Measurements

1. On the table toolbar, click **Show** **Columns** > **Measurements**.
2. In the******Measurements**dialog, click the **Operational** **Exchange** (or **Resource** **Interaction**) or **Operational** **Exchange** **Item** **([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space=''],** **NOV-3)** (or **Resource** **Interaction** **Item** **([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space=''],** **NSV-6)**) tab.
3. In the selected tab, click to choose the desired Measurement.
4. Click **OK**, when you are done.

removeTo remove columns with Measurements

1. On the table toolbar, click **Show** **Columns** > **Measurements**.
2. In the **Measurements**dialog, click the **Operational** **Exchange** (or **Resource** **Interaction**) or **Operational** **Exchange** **Item** **([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space=''],** **NOV-3)** (or **Resource** **Interaction** **Item** **([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space=''],** **NSV-6)**) tab.
3. In the opened tab, click the **Clear** **All** button to clear all the selected check boxes.
4. Click **OK**, when you are done.

The Measurement column is automatically removed after doing one of the following action:

- Removing the Operational Exchange from the model or table. The column is not removed if the Measurement of the removed Operational Exchange is used by the other Operational Exchange in the table.
- Removing the conveyed element from the model or table. The column is not removed if the Measurement of the removed conveyed element is used by the other Operational Exchange Item ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space=''] , NOV-3) (or Resource Interaction Item ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix' space=''] , NSV-6)) in the table.
- Removing the Measurement Set which includes the Measurement represented by a table column from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Measurement.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Click <strong>OK</strong>, when you are done. </span></li></ol><p style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><br ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">remove</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To remove columns with Measurements</span></p><hr /><ol><li style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">On the table <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">toolbar</span></span>, click <strong>Show</strong> <strong>Columns</strong> &gt; <strong>Measurements</strong>.</span></li><li style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">In the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></span><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><strong>Measurements</strong></span> </span>dialog</span>, click the <strong>Operational</strong> <strong>Exchange</strong> (or <strong>Resource</strong> <strong>Interaction</strong>) or <strong>Operational</strong> <strong>Exchange</strong> <strong>Item</strong> <strong>(<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>,</strong> <strong>NOV-3)</strong> (or <strong>Resource</strong> <strong>Interaction</strong> <strong>Item</strong> <strong>(<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-6]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>,</strong> <strong>NSV-6)</strong>) tab.</span></li><li style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">In the opened tab, click the <strong>Clear</strong> <strong>All</strong> button to clear all the selected check boxes.</span></li><li style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Click <strong>OK</strong>, when you are done.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="46e39ee0-6028-478a-94fe-36a95b282b78" ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Measurement column is automatically removed after doing one of the following action:</p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"> </span>Removing the Operational Exchange from the model or table. The column is not removed if the Measurement of the removed Operational Exchange is used by the other Operational Exchange in the table.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"> </span>Removing the conveyed element from the model or table. The column is not removed if the Measurement of the removed conveyed element is used by the other Operational Exchange Item (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, NOV-3) (or Resource Interaction Item (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-6]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-6]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, NSV-6)) in the table.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"> </span>Removing the Measurement Set which includes the Measurement represented by a table column from the model.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439155 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00850: (2026x) Displaying information indirectly related to element through derived properties

- page_id: `254439155`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439155/2026x+Displaying+information+indirectly+related+to+element+through+derived+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:40.835+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures > (2026x) Managing Traceability and Impact Analysis
- labels: ['create-derived-properties', 'edit-derived-properties', 'delete-derived-properties', 'display-derived-properties', 'display-information-indirectly-related-to-element-through-derived-properties']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

While working with your models, sometimes in the same diagram or table, you need to see information that is not directly related to an element. You can display that information in the following ways:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying information indirectly related to element through derived properties' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying information indirectly related to element through derived properties' space=''] (as a new column)

To display the information indirectly related to an element, you need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying information indirectly related to element through derived properties' space='']. A property whose values are calculated is called a derived property.

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying information indirectly related to element through derived properties' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying information indirectly related to element through derived properties' space=''], or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying information indirectly related to element through derived properties' space=''] derived properties in the:

- Element’s Specification window
- Table

You can manage derived properties using the **Expression** dialog.

create_newTo create a new derived property

1. Open the Expression dialog by doing one of the following:
  - In the selected element’s Specification window, click the Traceability property group and then click the Create button.
  - In the toolbar of an active table, click Show Columns and then select New Derived Property .
2. In the Expression dialog, specify the name and expression for the derived property.
3. Click **OK**.

If you want to create a derived property with the result other than an element, you need to change the derived property type manually.

editTo edit a derived property

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the selected element’s Specification window, click the Traceability property group, select a derived property you want to edit, and then click the Edit button.
  - In the active table, from the derived property column heading’s shortcut menu, select Edit Expression.
2. In the Expression dialog make changes and click OK .

removeTo remove a derived property

1. Do one of the following:

- In the selected element’s Specification window, click the Traceability property group, select a derived property you want to edit, and then click the Delete button.
- In the active table, from the derived property column heading’s shortcut menu, select Remove Derived Property .

display_on_noteTo display derived properties on a Note shape

1. Create a Note for the selected element or select an existing one.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select a Note and click the Edit Element Properties smart manipulator.The Compartment Edit dialog opens.
  - Right-click the Note shape and select Edit Compartment . The Compartment Edit dialog opens. Choose Element Properties tab.
3. In the**Hidden** list, move properties you want to display on a Note shape to the **Selected** list by doing one of the following:
  - Select one or more properties and click the > or >> button.
  - Double-click to select properties one by one.
4. Click OK . Selected derived properties are displayed in the Note.

display_in_tableTo display derived properties in a table

1. On the table toolbar, click Show Columns button and select Select Columns .
2. In the opened Select Columns dialog, select properties you want to display.
3. Click OK , when you are done.

**Case Study**

Let’s say, we need to show *Warning Order* and *Medical Condition* exchange elements that are produced and consumed by the performer *Search Node*. Exchange elements are information indirectly related to the *Search Node* element.

We will analyze the OV-2 diagram from the DoDAF sample project.

First, we will show the exchange elements produced by the*Search Node*.

Let’s create the **Produces Exchange Element** derived property for the performer *Search Node*. Open the *Search Node* Specification window and in the **Traceability** property group click the **Create** button. In the **Expression** dialog, select the **Metachain Navigation**property group. Then specify the derived property name and the expression for the meta chain as it is shown in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the following figure, you can see that a new subgroup named **Other** is created in the Performer Specification window. Our created **Produces Exchange Element** derived property is placed under this subgroup. The **Produces Exchange Element** derived property has Warning Order and Medical Condition exchange elements as its values.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Next, we need to display values of the **Produces Exchange Element** derived property for the *Search Node* performer in the OV-2 diagram.

A derived property is applicable to an element type, not the specific element. In this particular example, our created derived properties are applicable to all performers, not only to Search Node.

We can use a Note element wherein we can display information indirectly related to the Search Node. Let’s create a Note and connect it to the *Search Node* performer. Then click the **Edit Compartment**. In the **Compartment Edit** dialog, select the **Element Properties** tab. From the properties list, select to display the Produces Exchange Element property. After clicking the **OK** button, Warning Order and Medical Condition exchange elements are displayed in the Note.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Now, let’s create a generic table wherein we can also display information indirectly related to the *Search Node*, as well as to other performers. For that purpose, we select Performer as an**Element Type** and add existing performers to the table. Then we select which information to display as new columns. Previously created **Produces Exchange Element** derived property is also available to add as a new column through the **Show Columns** menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Now, let’s create a new derived property directly in the generic table. This property will show which exchange elements are consumed by the performer element. We can do that using the same **Expression** dialog and doing the same actions as described previously. In the following figure is the example of the **Consumes Exchange Element** derived property expression.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

When the expression is specified, the newly created derived property is displayed as a new column.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>While working with your models, sometimes in the same diagram or table, you need to see information that is not directly related to an element. You can display that information in the following ways:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="display_on_note"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[In the Note]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying information indirectly related to element through derived properties" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="display_in_table"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[In the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying information indirectly related to element through derived properties" /></ac:link> (as a new column)</li></ul><p>To display the information indirectly related to an element, you need to <ac:link ac:anchor="create_new"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create derived properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying information indirectly related to element through derived properties" /></ac:link>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438993 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00851: (2026x) Displaying Measurement Values

- page_id: `254438993`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438993/2026x+Displaying+Measurement+Values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:28.532+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures > (2026x) Managing Measurement Sets and Actual Measurements
- labels: ['edit-measurement-values', 'display-measurement-values']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

While working with diagrams, you can display and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying Measurement Values' space=''] for the element in the:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying Measurement Values' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying Measurement Values' space=''] .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

in_compartmentTo display measurement values in the compartment area of the element shape

1. Right-click the element, for which you want to display measurements.
2. Click Edit Compartments and select Measurements tab.
3. Select the measurements you want to display.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

In the Symbol Properties dialog, you can select to display the name of the measured element.

in_note_or_commentTo display measurement values in the note or comment

1. Create a note or comment for the element which measurements you want to display.
2. Right-click the note or comment and select Edit Compartments .
3. Choose Measurements tab and select the measurements you want to display.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

- Only measurements with predefined values can be displayed.
- You can also display indirectly related measurement values.

editTo edit measurement values

1. Select an element with the displayed measurement values you want to edit.
2. Double-click a measurement value you want to edit.
3. Type a new value.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<strong>Measurements</strong> tab.</li><li>Select the measurements you want to display.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>, when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the Symbol Properties dialog, you can select to display the name of the measured element.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor"><ac:parameter ac:name="">in_note_or_comment</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To display <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439151 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00852: (2026x) Displaying Sub Capabilities deleted from Cr diagram

- page_id: `254439151`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439151/2026x+Displaying+Sub+Capabilities+deleted+from+Cr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:40.696+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Concepts viewpoint > (2026x) Cr Capability Roadmap
- labels: ['display-deleted-from-chart-in-cr', 'display-sub-capability-deleted-from-cr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The capability name becomes *Italic* when one or more sub capabilities are deleted from the chart.

To display deleted Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to restore.
2. From the selected Capability’s shortcut menu, select Display Deleted From Chart and do one of the following:
  - Select the Sub Capability you want to be displayed.
  - Select Display All to display all deleted Sub Capabilities.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The capability name becomes <em>Italic</em> when one or more sub capabilities are deleted from the chart.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To display deleted Sub Capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to restore.</li><li>From the selected Capability’s shortcut menu, select <strong>Display Deleted From Chart</strong> and do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select the Sub Capability you want to be displayed.</li><li>Select<strong> Display All</strong> to display all deleted Sub Capabilities.</li></ul></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439392 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00853: (2026x) Displaying Sub Capabilities deleted from CV-3 diagram

- page_id: `254439392`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439392/2026x+Displaying+Sub+Capabilities+deleted+from+CV-3+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:02.865+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Capability viewpoint > (2026x) CV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: ['display-sub-capabilities-deleted-from-cv-3', 'display-deleted-from-chart-in-cv-3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1414781495

1414781497

1414781496

The capability name becomes *Italic* when one or more sub capabilities are deleted from the chart.

**To display deleted Sub Capability**

1. Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to restore.
2. From the selected Capability’s shortcut menu, select Display Deleted From Chart and do one of the following:
  - Select the Sub Capability you want to be displayed.
  - Select Display All to display all deleted Sub Capabilities.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8884ae06-15c9-4c6a-aa55-3aaa17a66296"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1414781495</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="27bb5c2b-9289-47a2-b811-71d5945d1a3c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1414781497</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd6edc17-0f20-42fc-8051-b0e168aeb4ac"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1414781496</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9d0bf235-0c85-43f2-9efc-d9a2e8dbfb52"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The capability name becomes <em>Italic</em> when one or more sub capabilities are deleted from the chart.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>To display deleted Sub Capability</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to restore.</li><li>From the selected Capability’s shortcut menu, select <strong>Display Deleted From Chart</strong> and do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select the Sub Capability you want to be displayed.</li><li>Select<strong> Display All</strong> to display all deleted Sub Capabilities.</li></ul></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438560 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00854: (2026x) DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model

- page_id: `254438560`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438560/2026x+DIV-1+Conceptual+Data+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:42.697+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Data and Information viewpoint
- labels: ['div-1-conceptual-data-model', 'div-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

638611895

638611898

638611897

**Description**

The DIV-1, a new DoDAF-described Model in DoDAF V2.0, addresses the information concepts at a high-level on an operational architecture.

The DIV-1 is used to document the business information requirements and structural business process rules of the architecture. It describes the information that is associated with the information of the architecture. Included are information items, their attributes or characteristics, and their inter-relationships.

The intended usage of the DIV-1 includes:

- Information requirements.
- Information hierarchy.

**Implementation**

DIV-1 can be represented using a DIV-1 diagram which is based in the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The DIV-1 defines each kind of information classes associated with the Architectural Description scope, mission, or business as its own Entity, with its associated attributes and relationships. These Entity definitions correlate to[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] information elements and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space=''] inputs, outputs, and controls.

638611894

**Related Pages:**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exchange Element' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Details' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Entity Item' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Entity Attribute' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Logical Data Model' space='']
