It describes the information that is associated with the information of the architecture. Included are information items, their attributes or characteristics, and their inter-relationships.</p><p>The intended usage of the DIV-1 includes:</p><ul><li>Information requirements.</li><li>Information hierarchy.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>DIV-1 can be represented using a DIV-1 diagram which is based in the UML Class diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model" ac:alt="DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model"><ri:attachment ri:filename="DIV-1.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The DIV-1 defines each kind of information classes associated with the Architectural Description scope, mission, or business as its own Entity, with its associated attributes and relationships. These Entity definitions correlate to<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> information elements and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5b Operational Activity Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> inputs, outputs, and controls.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="905b1da1-33d2-434d-96b2-f6e68fb0a889"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">638611894</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="70442e64-058d-4dd3-97b7-40a34871c5e9" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Exchange Element" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Details" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Entity Item" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Entity Attribute" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Logical Data Model" /></ac:link></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438509 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00855: (2026x) DIV-2 Logical Data Model

- page_id: `254438509`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438509/2026x+DIV-2+Logical+Data+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:30.015+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Data and Information viewpoint
- labels: ['div-2-logical-data-model', 'div-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

639161871

639161873

639161872

**Description**

The DIV-2 allows analysis of an architecture’s data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation specific or product specific issues. Another purpose is to provide a common dictionary of data definitions to consistently express models wherever logical-level data elements are included in the descriptions.

**Implementation**

DIV-2 can be represented using:

- A DIV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='DIV-2.png']
- A UML Class diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.

Data described in a DIV-2 may be related to Information in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic' space=''] or and Activity Resource (where the Resource is Data) flow object in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space='']. This relation may be a simple subtype, where the Data is a proceduralized (structured) way of describing something. Recall that Information describes something. Alternatively, the relation may be complex using Information and Data whole part (and overlap) relationships.

The DIV-2 information entities and elements can be constrained and validated by the capture of business requirements in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-6a Operational Rules Model' space=''].

The information entities and elements modeled in the DIV-2 also capture the information content of messages that connect life-lines in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description' space=''].

The DIV-2 may capture elements required due to Standards in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) StdV-1 Standards Profile' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) StdV-2 Standards Forecast' space=''].

639161869

**Related Pages:**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Entity Item' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Entity Attribute' space='']

This relation may be a simple subtype, where the Data is a proceduralized (structured) way of describing something. Recall that Information describes something. Alternatively, the relation may be complex using Information and Data whole part (and overlap) relationships.</p><p>The DIV-2 information entities and elements can be constrained and validated by the capture of business requirements in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-6a Operational Rules Model" /></ac:link>.</p><p>The information entities and elements modeled in the DIV-2 also capture the information content of messages that connect life-lines in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-6c Event-Trace Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>The DIV-2 may capture elements required due to Standards in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) StdV-1 Standards Profile" /></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) StdV-2 Standards Forecast" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c1d8dcbc-6327-46ea-afae-eef077274023"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">639161869</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="93573017-db9a-44ca-8e6e-641fd363c5cb" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Entity Item" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Entity Attribute" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Entity Relationship" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Details" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Exchange Element" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Logical Data Model" /></ac:link></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00856: (2026x) DIV-3 Physical Data Model

- page_id: `254438504`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438504/2026x+DIV-3+Physical+Data+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:28.527+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Data and Information viewpoint
- labels: ['div-3-physical-data-model', 'div-3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

639670783

639670786

639670784

**Description**

The DIV-3 defines the structure of the various kinds of system or service data that are utilized by the systems or services in the Architectural Description. The Physical Schema is one of the models closest to actual system design in DoDAF. DIV-3 is used to describe how the information represented in the DIV-2 Logical Data Model is actually implemented.

While the mapping between the logical and physical data models is relatively straightforward, the relationship between the components of each model (e.g., entity types in the logical model versus relational tables in the physical model) is frequently one-to-many or many-to-many.

**Implementation**

DIV-3 can be represented using:

- A DIV-3 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='DIV-3.PNG']
- A UML Class diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition Diagram.

The Physical Data Model is one of the Architectural Products closest to actual system design in the Framework. DIV-3 is used to describe how the information represented in the Information Model ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) DIV-2 Logical Data Model' space='']) is actually implemented.

639670781

**Related Pages:**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Entity Item' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Entity Relationship' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exchange Element' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Physical Data Model' space='']

The Physical Schema is one of the models closest to actual system design in DoDAF. DIV-3 is used to describe how the information represented in the DIV-2 Logical Data Model is actually implemented.</p><p>While the mapping between the logical and physical data models is relatively straightforward, the relationship between the components of each model (e.g., entity types in the logical model versus relational tables in the physical model) is frequently one-to-many or many-to-many.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>DIV-3 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A DIV-3 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="DIV-3 Physical Data Model" ac:alt="DIV-3 Physical Data Model"><ri:attachment ri:filename="DIV-3.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) DIV-3 Physical Data Model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>A UML Class diagram.</li><li>A SysML Block Definition Diagram.</li></ul><p>The Physical Data Model is one of the Architectural Products closest to actual system design in the Framework. DIV-3 is used to describe how the information represented in the Information Model (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) DIV-2 Logical Data Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[DIV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) is actually implemented.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7ece6622-3c31-413b-9a8e-89303ea26eba"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">639670781</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="fad414e7-f337-46d3-bc56-7a33da39b73d" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Entity Item" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Entity Attribute" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Entity Relationship" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Exchange Element" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Physical Data Model" /></ac:link></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438364 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00857: (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views

- page_id: `254438364`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438364/2026x+DoDAF+2.0+viewpoints+and+views
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:06.949+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation
- labels: ['dodaf', 'department-of-defense-architecture-framework']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

564848754

564848757

564848756

The Department of Defense Architecture Framework (DoDAF) is an architecture framework for the United States Department of Defense (DoD) that provides visualization infrastructure for specific stakeholders concerns through viewpoints organized by various views. These views are artifacts for visualizing, understanding and assimilating the broad scope and complexities of an architecture description through tabular, structural, behavioral, ontological, pictorial, temporal, graphical, probabilistic, or alternative conceptual means.

This Architecture Framework is uniquely suited to large systems with complex integration and interoperability challenges, and it is unique in its employment of "operational views." These views offer overview and details aimed at specific stakeholders within their domain and in interaction with other domains in which the system will operate.

The DoDAF provides a foundational framework for developing and representing architecture descriptions that ensure a common denominator for understanding, comparing, and integrating architectures across organizational, joint, and multinational boundaries. It establishes data element definitions, rules, and relationships and a baseline set of products for the consistent development of systems, integrated, or federated architectures. These architecture descriptions may include families of systems (FoS), systems of systems (SoS), and net-centric capabilities for inter-operating and interacting in the non-combat environment.

The purpose of DoDAF is to define concepts and models usable in DoD's six core processes:

1. Joint Capabilities Integration and Development (JCIDS)
2. Planning, Programming, Budgeting, and Execution (PPBE)
3. Defense Acquisition System (DAS)
4. Systems Engineering (SE)
5. Operational Planning (OPLAN)
6. Capability Portfolio Management (CPM)

The DoDAF process (the following figure) is only recommendable. You can choose the creation sequence of diagrams according to your needs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

564848753

**Related Pages:**

****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9daf03e9-70d5-4f63-a328-fb2585ddfa8d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">564848754</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b6b3a3cc-2dcc-4aa5-933a-a50234a93fa2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">564848757</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e9132e8-30bb-4ff4-8411-18ebebb826ba"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">564848756</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><div><p>The Department of Defense Architecture Framework (DoDAF) is an architecture framework for the United States Department of Defense (DoD) that provides visualization infrastructure for specific stakeholders concerns through viewpoints organized by various views. These views are artifacts for visualizing, understanding and assimilating the broad scope and complexities of an architecture description through tabular, structural, behavioral, ontological, pictorial, temporal, graphical, probabilistic, or alternative conceptual means.</p><p>This Architecture Framework is uniquely suited to large systems with complex integration and interoperability challenges, and it is unique in its employment of &quot;operational views.&quot; These views offer overview and details aimed at specific stakeholders within their domain and in interaction with other domains in which the system will operate.</p><p>The DoDAF provides a foundational framework for developing and representing architecture descriptions that ensure a common denominator for understanding, comparing, and integrating architectures across organizational, joint, and multinational boundaries. It establishes data element definitions, rules, and relationships and a baseline set of products for the consistent development of systems, integrated, or federated architectures. These architecture descriptions may include families of systems (FoS), systems of systems (SoS), and net-centric capabilities for inter-operating and interacting in the non-combat environment.</p><p>The purpose of DoDAF is to define concepts and models usable in DoD's six core processes:</p></div><div><ol><li>Joint Capabilities Integration and Development (JCIDS)</li><li>Planning, Programming, Budgeting, and Execution (PPBE)</li><li>Defense Acquisition System (DAS)</li><li>Systems Engineering (SE)</li><li>Operational Planning (OPLAN)</li><li>Capability Portfolio Management (CPM)</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e414ad8-e171-49a8-916c-81ef877956af"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The DoDAF process (the following figure) is only recommendable. You can choose the creation sequence of diagrams according to your needs.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="DoDAF process" ac:alt="DoDAF process"><ri:attachment ri:filename="DoDAF_process_2.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6a8d1558-c779-4035-95a6-1396f5847601"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">564848753</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="78e24942-5155-46ba-b0c9-08ab7e7f3e7a" /><strong><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438463 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00858: (2026x) Dr Deployment Schedule

- page_id: `254438463`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438463/2026x+Dr+Deployment+Schedule
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:19.334+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Deployed Resources viewpoint
- labels: ['dr-deployment-schedule', 'dr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Dr Deployment Schedule view addresses the roll-out of resource packages (new capabilities, organizational change, etc.) into individual organizations. This view shows the planned capability deployment into individual organizations for a set of Enterprise Phases. Each Enterprise Phase snapshot lists the Organizations and the Resource Package that it is planned to receive. Each Resource Package may be traced to the Capability it delivers. The Dr view may also specify the possible interactions between the deployed Capability so that planners can see at a glance which organizations have compatible systems, and when.

The Dr view may be used for:

- Fielding planning.
- Business change planning.
- Capability integration planning.
- Capability options analysis.
- Capability redundancy/overlap/gap analysis.
- Identification of deployment level shortfalls.

**Implementation**

the Dr view can be represented using:

- A Dr table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- A Dr spreadsheet report.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Capability Configuration
- Exhibits
- Actual Organization
- Actual Post
- Deployed Milestone
- No Longer Used Milestone
- Actual Project
- Enterprise Phase
- Whole-Life Enterprise

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Each Enterprise Phase snapshot lists the Organizations and the Resource Package that it is planned to receive. Each Resource Package may be traced to the Capability it delivers. The Dr view may also specify the possible interactions between the deployed Capability so that planners can see at a glance which organizations have compatible systems, and when.</p><p>The Dr view may <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439965 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00859: (2026x) Dragging and Dropping

- page_id: `254439965`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439965/2026x+Dragging+and+Dropping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:46.899+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures
- labels: ['drag', 'drop', 'select-drag-drop-rule', 'drag-and-drop']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Create relationships or assign property values by dragging and dropping one element onto another right in the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

There are a number of predefined rules to create relationships or assign property values automatically. You may also set up your own drag-and-drop specifications between any elements.

To select a rule

1. Do one of the following:
  - Drag a selected element onto another, hold it for a few seconds and then release it. A shortcut menu is displayed.
  - Right-click an element and drag it onto another (This action is valid only for Windows operating system users). A shortcut menu is displayed.
2. From the shortcut menu, select one of the rules you want to apply.

The following table lists the predefined drag and drop rules. There rules define the possible results when dragging source element on the target element.

| Source Element | Target Element | Created Relationship | Assigned Property |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Organization | Project | Organizational Project Relationship |  |
| Service AccessPhysical ArchitectureCapability Configuration | NodePerformer | Implements |  |

## PAGE 00860: (2026x) Enduring Task

- page_id: `254438875`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438875/2026x+Enduring+Task
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:21.330+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Capability Viewpoint and Strategic Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

915538724

915538727

915538726

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A type of behavior recognized by an enterprise as being essential to achieving its goals, i.e., a strategic specification of what the enterprise does.

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Class

##### Related MODAF views

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439653 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00862: (2026x) Enterprise Architecture Web Report Portal

- page_id: `254439653`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439653/2026x+Enterprise+Architecture+Web+Report+Portal
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:27.703+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Supportive Views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Publish your project as interactive web portal and share it with stakeholders, including even those incapable of reading models in MagicDraw! The published web portal eliminates less needed elements from the model tree and proposes the most relevant views of enterprise architecture project, such as capability / strategic, operational, systems, services, standards, projects. This allows you to discover your project in a user-friendly and easily browsable environment.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can also provide feedback on any element or diagram while reviewing an Enterprise Architecture Portal report of the project.Use quick search to find elements and diagrams for viewing their custom contents and share this information with colleagues. Publishing the project as a web portal is as easy as generating any report.

To publish a report

1. On the main menu, click **Tools** > **Report Wizard.**
2. Select one of the following templates:
  - DoDAF > DoDAF Web Report
  - **MODAF** > **MODAF Web Report**
  - **NAF** > **NAF Web Report**
3. Follow the steps of the report wizard.

Place the output in a shared directory or on web server to make it available for co-workers.The following table introduces what you can expect in your web report. In every view of the report, you may find the following information:

- What elements are collected
- Collected element properties
- Related elements
- Related diagram types

| Capability View - Enterprise Phase |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Enterprise Phase Properties | Related Elements | Related Diagams |
| IdentifierNameStart DateEnd DateDocumentation | GoalsCapabilitiesReferences | Relation Map |

915612840

915612839

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A specific, required objective of the enterprise that the architecture represents.

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| endDate : ISO8601DateTime[1] | The time and date at which the Phase ends. |
| startDate : ISO8601DateTime[1] | The time and date at which the Phase starts. |
| goals : EnterpriseGoal[*] | The Goal towards which this Phase is directed and is in support of. |
| visions : EnterpriseVision[*] | The Vision towards which this Phase is directed and is in support of. |
| statementTasks : EnduringTask[*] | Collection of statement tasks. |
| fulfills : Mission [*] |  |
| describedBy : ArchitecturalDescription [*] |  |

##### Extensions

UML Class

915643752

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: The overall aims of an enterprise over a given period of time.

DoDAF: (DoDAF::Vision): An end that describes the future state of the enterprise, without regard to how it is to be achieved; a mental image of what the future will or could be like.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Class

##### Related MODAF views

## PAGE 00869: (2026x) Environment

- page_id: `254439757`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439757/2026x+Environment
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:16:20.277+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 All Views Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1097168225

1097168227

1097168226

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A definition of the conditions in which something exists or functions. An Environment may be specified in terms of LocationType (e.g., terrain), Climate (e.g., tropical), and LightCondition (e.g., dark, light, dusk, etc.).

DoDAF: An object that encompasses meteorological, geographic, and control features mission significance.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1097184755

1097184757

1097184756

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A definition of the conditions in which something exists or functions. An Environment may be specified in terms of LocationType (e.g., terrain), Climate (e.g., tropical), and LightCondition (e.g., dark, light, dusk, etc.).

DoDAF: An object that encompasses meteorological, geographic, and control features mission significance.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Class

##### Related DoDAF views

[CV-2 Capability Taxonomy](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) CV-2+Capability+Taxonomy)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. StV-2 Capability Taxonomy](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/MODAF.+StV-2+Capability+Taxonomy)

##### Related NAF views

[NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P190/NCV-2+Capability+Taxonomy)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439800 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00871: (2026x) Environment Selection dialog

- page_id: `254439800`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439800/2026x+Environment+Selection+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:37.388+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Dialogs
- labels: ['environment-selection-dialog']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Environment Selection** dialog opens when starting the UPDM plugin for the first time.

To select the enterprise architecture framework environment

1. Select the environment according to the architecture you are going to build: Read the brief descriptions of each enterprise architecture framework provided in the dialog (see the following figure). This information may help you decide what to choose.
  - DoDAF 2.0
  - MODAF
  - NAF v. 3
  - NAF 4.0
2. Click to select the Expert check box, if you want the user interface to be complex and have all details exposed. And if you are a new user, leave the Expert check box unselected.
3. Click the Continue button. The sample project corresponding the selected enterprise architecture framework will be opened (only if using Cameo Enterprise Architecture tool).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can change the selected environment and its mode via the **Select Perspective** dialog.

To open **Select Perspective** dialog

1. Click Options > Perspectives > Perspectives . The Select Perspective dialog opens.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <strong>Environment Selection</strong> dialog opens when starting the UPDM plugin for the first time.</p><p>To select the enterprise architecture framework environment</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the environment according to the architecture you are going to build:<br /><ul><li>DoDAF 2.0</li><li>MODAF</li><li>NAF v. 3</li><li>NAF 4.0</li></ul>Read the brief descriptions of each enterprise architecture framework provided in the dialog (see the following figure). This information may help you decide what to choose.</li><li>Click to select the <strong>Expert</strong> check box, if you want the user interface to be complex and have all details exposed. And if you are a new user, leave the <strong>Expert</strong> check box unselected.</li><li>Click the <strong>Continue</strong> button. The sample project corresponding the selected enterprise architecture framework will be opened (only if using Cameo Enterprise Architecture tool).</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:alt="Environment Selection dialog" ac:title="Environment Selection dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="environment_selection_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Environment Selection dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can change the selected environment and its mode via the <strong>Select Perspective</strong> dialog.</p><p>To open <strong>Select Perspective</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Perspectives</strong> &gt; <strong>Perspectives</strong>.<br />The <strong>Select Perspective</strong> dialog opens.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A relationship specifying the need to exchange information between nodes.

DoDAF: NA - this is a specialization of OperationalExchange (DoDAF::Interface).

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Data Type

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

915692784

915692787

915692786

##### Description

UPDM: Relationship between a Node and a capability the node provides.

MODAF/NAF: (MODAF::CapabilityForNode): An assertion that a Node is required to have a Capability.

DoDAF: A couple that represents the capability that a performer manifests.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Dependency

##### Related DoDAF views

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439067 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00874: (2026x) Exporting tables to Excel spreadsheets

- page_id: `254439067`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439067/2026x+Exporting+tables+to+Excel+spreadsheets
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:34.804+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures > (2026x) Managing Tables
- labels: ['export-tables-to-excel-spreadsheets']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can export your active OV-3, NOV-3, OV-6a, NOV-6a, AV-2, NAV-2. SV-6, NSV-6, SV-10a, NSV-10a, SOV-4a, StV-5, CV-5, SvcV-10a, SV-7 Actual, SV-7 Typical, NSV-7 Actual, NSV-7 Typical, SvcV7-Actual, and SvcV-7 Typical tables into the Excel Spreadsheet.

To export a table

1. On the table toolbar, click the Export button.
2. In the Choose file dialog, specify the file name and location.
3. Click Save . The table is exported to Excel spreadsheet.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can export your active OV-3, NOV-3, OV-6a, NOV-6a, AV-2, NAV-2. SV-6, NSV-6, SV-10a, NSV-10a, SOV-4a, StV-5, CV-5, SvcV-10a, SV-7 Actual, SV-7 Typical, NSV-7 Actual, NSV-7 Typical, SvcV7-Actual, and SvcV-7 Typical tables into the Excel Spreadsheet.</p><p>To export a table</p><hr /><ol><li>On the table toolbar, click the <strong>Export</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Choose </strong><strong>file</strong> dialog, specify the file name and location.</li><li>Click <strong>Save</strong>.<br />The table is exported to Excel spreadsheet.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exporting_table-to_excel.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Exporting tables to Excel spreadsheets" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````
